[A41] PERSONAL OR DOMESTIC ARTICLES -- A41 covers personal or domestic articles such as clothing, footwear, luggage, personal accessories and household items designed for individual use, including personal hygiene products, small utensils and related equipment.
[A41B] SHIRTS -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Underwear covers shirts, hosiery, baby linen and hankerchiefs.  In particular, the following subjects are classified as follows:  **Glossary:** - Dressing-gown: A loose gown worn while making one's toilet or when in dishabille. - Shirt: An upper body garment having a collar and an opening at the front which is fastened closed via fastening elements (e.g. buttons) disposed adjacent the opening, also referred to as a men's dress shirt, button-up shirt or button-down shirt - Chemise: Undergarment with shoulder strap worn beneath dress or the like, also referred to as slip - Bodices: Baby garment usually with sleeves but leaving the legs uncovered and fastening means at the crotch, also referred to as a onesie - Jacket: Lightweight long-sleeve garment typically having a collar and lapel extending below the front sides of the collar providing a central opening between the lapels, also referred to as a dress jacket or sport coat - Waistcoat: Upper body garment without sleeves also referred to as a vest in American English - Vest: T-shirt or casual top with or without sleeves - Jersey: Knitted garment with or without sleeves, also referred to as a sweater in American English - Sweater: Garment typically worn for playing team sports, also referred to as a jersey in American English - Blouse: Shirt having features specific to women, also referred to as a women's top - Overalls: A one-piece garment having an upper body portion (e.g. short or long sleeve top, bib secured by shoulder straps, etc.) and lower body portion (e.g. pants, shorts, etc.), also referred to as coveralls or bodysuits - Leggings: Lower body overgarment with or without seats for protecting a garment worn underneath, also referred to as chaps
[A41B 1/00] Shirts -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Shirt with buttons and a collar, i.e. men's dress shirt.

[A41B 2300/00] Details of shirts, underwear, baby linen or handkerchiefs not provided for in other groups of this subclass -- A41B 2300/00 pertains to the particular design elements of shirts, underwear, baby linen and handkerchiefs that are not covered by other groups in the subclass, such as special stitching, trims, pleats, cuff constructions or decorative details. It captures all ancillary features that define the garment's appearance or function beyond basic shape.
[A41B 2300/20] Inserts -- This CPC subclass A41B 2300/20 relates to inserts used in shirts, underwear, baby linen or handkerchiefs when the particular type of insert is not covered by other groups. It covers any additional lining, padding or decorative piece incorporated into such garments.
[A41B 2300/22] Elastic inserts -- A41B 2300/22 relates to elastic components incorporated into shirts, underwear, baby linen or handkerchiefs, such as bands, cords or inserts that provide stretch or improved fit, when these features are not covered by other subclasses of the shirt group.
[A41B 2300/24] Folded inserts -- A41B 2300/24 covers folded inserts used in shirts, underwear, baby linen or handkerchiefs that are not addressed in other groups of the subclass, such as pockets, stays, or other internal folds incorporated into the garment.
[A41B 2300/30] Closures -- A41B 2300/30 covers the specific design and construction of closures used on shirts, underwear, baby linen and handkerchiefs when those items are not addressed in other groups of the subclass. It includes fasteners such as buttons, snaps, hooks, zippers or other mechanisms that join fabric parts.
[A41B 2300/32] using hook and loop-type fasteners -- This subclass covers shirts, underwear, baby linen or handkerchiefs whose closure relies on hook-and-loop type fasteners, such as Velcro, when no other specific fastening arrangement is detailed.
[A41B 2300/322] using slide fasteners -- A41B 2300/322 classifies shirts, underwear, baby linen and handkerchiefs whose specific details are not covered elsewhere, specifically when slide fasteners (zippers) are used as the means of fastening.
[A41B 2300/324] using snap fasteners -- A41B 2300/324 is a Cooperative Patent Classification entry for shirts, underwear, baby linen or handkerchiefs that employ snap fasteners as a closure method, covering designs not otherwise classified in more specific groups of this subclass.
[A41B 2300/326] using hooks and eyelets -- The entry covers shirts, underwear, baby linen and handkerchiefs that employ hooks or eyelets for fastening or decorative purposes, where such features are not addressed in other subclasses. It includes designs, arrangements and implementations of these hardware elements in the garment construction.
[A41B 2300/328] using adhesive -- This CPC subclass A41B 2300/328 pertains to shirts, underwear, baby linen or handkerchiefs whose construction employs an adhesive. It includes inventions that attach, bond or secure fabric components of these garments by glue, pressure-sensitive tape or similar bonding agents instead of stitching or other mechanical fasteners.
[A41B 2300/33] using straps or ties -- A41B 2300/33 refers to shirts, underwear, baby linen or handkerchiefs whose design incorporates straps or ties, covering items not classified elsewhere in the shirts subclass. It belongs to the hierarchy Human Necessities → Personal or Domestic Articles → Shirts.
[A41B 2300/332] using elastic bands -- This entry covers the use of elastic bands in shirts, underwear, baby linen or handkerchiefs that are not otherwise classified, describing how elastic is applied to provide stretch, fit or gather the material. It includes methods of attaching, positioning and configuring elastic within these garments.
[A41B 2300/35] Seams -- This subclass covers the construction and characteristics of seams used in shirts, underwear, baby linen and handkerchiefs where the seam type is not specified in other groups. It includes methods of joining fabric, seam types, reinforcement and stitching patterns.
[A41B 2300/52] made by welding or gluing -- This subgroup covers shirts, underwear, baby linen or handkerchiefs whose components are joined by welding or gluing rather than stitching. It includes items where fabric parts are bonded using heat, adhesive or similar processes, and is applied when such construction details are not addressed in other groups of the subclass.
[A41B 2400/00] Functions or special features of shirts, underwear, baby linen or handkerchiefs not provided for in other groups of this subclass -- The A41B 2400/00 group covers any functional or special characteristics of shirts, underwear, baby linen or handkerchiefs that are not covered by other subclasses, such as unique fastening methods, integrated accessories, or novel fabric treatments. It captures innovations that go beyond standard design elements within these garments.
[A41B 2400/20] Air permeability -- A41B 2400/20 covers the air-permeability characteristics of shirts, underwear, baby linen and handkerchiefs, specifying how easily air passes through the fabric. It is used to classify products where breathability is a functional feature not addressed in other groups of the same subclass.
[A41B 2400/22] Breathability, i.e. being vapour permeable and waterproof -- A41B 2400/22 relates to shirts that combine vapor permeability with waterproofing, providing breathability while preventing liquid intrusion. This functional feature is classified under shirts whose special properties are not covered by other groups in the same subclass.
[A41B 2400/32] Therapeutic use -- A41B 2400/32 relates to garments such as shirts, underwear, baby linen or handkerchiefs that are specially constructed for therapeutic purposes, including the delivery of medication, compression, heat or other health-benefiting functions not covered by other groups in the subclass.
[A41B 2400/322] Massage -- This subclass covers shirts, underwear, baby linen or handkerchiefs that incorporate a massage function, such as integrated vibrating or pressure elements for therapeutic stimulation. It includes designs where the massage capability is built into the garment rather than provided as a separate device.
[A41B 2400/324] Acupuncture -- The CPC subclass A41B 2400/324 covers shirts, underwear, baby linen or handkerchiefs that incorporate acupuncture features, such as built-in needles, pressure points or therapeutic inserts designed to stimulate specific areas of the body. It includes any functional or special element related to acupuncture that is not covered by other groups in the shirts subclass.
[A41B 2400/34] antimicrobial or antibacterial -- This CPC subgroup covers shirts, including underwear or baby linen, that are treated to inhibit microbial growth. The code A41B 2400/34 designates garments whose fabrics or finishes provide antimicrobial or antibacterial properties, distinct from other functional features in the same subclass.
[A41B 2400/36] Deodorising or perfuming -- A41B 2400/36 relates to shirts that incorporate deodorising or perfuming features, where the garment is treated or constructed to emit fragrance or neutralise odours. The classification applies when the scent function is integral to the shirt rather than provided by separate accessories.
[A41B 2400/38] Shaping the contour of the body or adjusting the figure -- A41B 2400/38 covers shirt-type garments, underwear, baby linen or handkerchiefs that incorporate elements specifically designed to shape the wearer's body contour or modify the figure, such as built-in shaping panels, compression zones or adjustable fits that go beyond standard functional features. This code captures special contour-adjusting functions not covered elsewhere.
[A41B 2400/44] Donning facilities -- This CPC entry covers features on shirts, underwear, baby linen or handkerchiefs that aid the wearer in putting the garment on, such as openings, seams, tabs, hooks or other mechanisms designed to facilitate donning; it falls under A41B 2400 for functions or special features not provided for in other groups of the subclass.
[A41B 2400/52] disposable -- The CPC entry A41B 2400/52 designates disposable shirts, underwear, baby linen or handkerchiefs whose disposability is a functional characteristic not covered elsewhere. It classifies such items within the hierarchy of human necessities, personal or domestic articles, and shirts.
[A41B 2400/60] Moisture handling or wicking function -- This subclass addresses shirts and related garments that incorporate moisture-management features, such as wicking or sweat-absorbing properties. It includes designs, fabrics, or treatments that move liquid away from the skin to keep the wearer dry and comfortable.
[A41B 2400/62] through several layers -- A41B 2400/62 covers shirts, underwear, baby linen or handkerchiefs that are constructed with two or more layers of material, providing added body, insulation or decorative effect. The classification is used for designs whose multi-layer structure is not addressed in other groups of the subclass.
[A41B 2400/70] Removability -- A41B 2400/70 covers shirts, underwear, baby linen and handkerchiefs that incorporate detachable elements such as collars, cuffs, sleeves or inserts, allowing parts of the garment to be removed for cleaning, adjustment or style changes. The classification addresses removable features not covered in other groups of the subclass.
[A41B 2400/72] from the top of a stack of removable layers -- The code A41B 2400/72 refers to shirts with removable layers. This classification is for shirts with special features not covered elsewhere. It pertains to shirts with layers that can be removed from the top.
[A41B 2400/80] Friction or grip reinforcement -- This CPC entry covers features added to shirts, underwear, baby linen or handkerchiefs to increase friction or grip, such as textured areas, non-slip coatings, adhesive strips or other reinforcement methods not covered by other groups in the subclass.
[A41B 2400/82] with the body of the user -- This subclass covers shirts, underwear, baby linen or handkerchiefs that incorporate features formed to fit or conform to the wearer's body, such as built-in supportive structures, compression panels or ergonomic shaping. It includes garments with body-moulded sections or integrated elements that serve a functional purpose not covered in other groups of the subclass.
[A41B 2500/00] Materials for shirts, underwear, baby linen or handkerchiefs not provided for in other groups of this subclass -- A41B 2500/00 covers the specific materials employed in making shirts, underwear, baby linen or handkerchiefs that are not listed in any other group of this subclass, such as particular fabrics, blends or treated textiles used for these personal garments.
[A41B 2500/10] Knitted -- A41B 2500/10 designates knitted fabrics or yarn constructions employed as materials for shirts, underwear, baby linen or handkerchiefs when those materials are not covered by more specific groups within the subclass. It falls under the hierarchy Human necessities → Personal or domestic articles → Shirts → Materials for shirts, underwear, baby linen or handkerchiefs not provided for in other groups. This entry captures generic knitted textile forms used in these garment and household items.
[A41B 2500/20] Woven -- A41B 2500/20 designates woven fabrics used in shirts, underwear, baby linen or handkerchiefs that are not covered by any other specific material groups within the shirts subclass. It includes any woven textile composition intended for these garments when no more detailed classification applies.
[A41B 2500/30] Non-woven -- A41B 2500/30 pertains to non-woven fabrics employed as materials for shirts, underwear, baby linen or handkerchiefs that are not classified elsewhere in the subclass. It falls under the CPC hierarchy Human Necessities → Personal or Domestic Articles → Shirts and addresses textile compositions such as felt, bonded fibers or other non-woven structures used in garment production.
[A41B 2500/40] Papers -- A41B 2500/40 covers paper materials used in the manufacture of shirts, underwear, baby linen, handkerchiefs and similar textile articles when such paper is not listed in other groups of the subclass. It includes any paper-based components that serve as a functional or decorative layer in these personal or domestic garments.
[A41B 2500/50] Synthetic resins or rubbers -- A41B 2500/50 in the Cooperative Patent Classification denotes the use of synthetic resins or rubbers as material components for shirts, underwear, baby linen or handkerchiefs that are not covered by other groups within the shirts subclass, falling under personal or domestic articles.
[A41B 2500/52] in sheet form -- A41B 2500/52 covers sheet-form textile and non-textile materials intended for making shirts, underwear, baby linen or handkerchiefs that are not classified in other groups of the subclass. These materials are presented as flat sheets rather than woven or assembled garments.
[A41B 2500/54] in coated form -- A41B 2500/54 designates coated materials used for shirts, underwear, baby linen or handkerchiefs that are not covered in other more specific groups. It includes fabrics that have received a surface treatment such as water-repellent, flame-retardant, antimicrobial or other functional coatings.

[A41B 9/026] characterized by the opening -- A41B 9/026 covers shirts and undergarments that are defined primarily by the presence of an opening, such as a front, back, or side slit, cuffs, or collar openings that allow the garment to be put on or taken off. It is used for items like button-front shirts, zippered tops, or other apparel where a deliberate cut or closure creates an access opening.

A skirt or dress worn under a garment usually to shape the garment worn thereover (e.g. lift the skirt or dress to create a bell-shape) or provide warmth. [A41C] CORSETS -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Corsets or girdles being a special type of undergarments for shaping or correction of a figure, with or without stays or steels, auxiliary belts, straps or fasteners; corsets with or without brassieres, e.g. of annular or tubular shape; girdles, e.g. with high waist part, with open bottom, or girdles of panty type, e.g. with elongated thigh part with or without suspenders.  Brassieres being a type of undergarments for supporting or shaping breasts having connected or separate cups including strapless, backless brassieres, e.g. self-adhesive bra cups, or brassieres with stiffening or bust-forming inserts, e.g. with pads containing gel or foam filling, with pockets; bra specially adapted for nursing mothers, e.g. fold-down convertible bra or with replaceable absorbing pads or cups; bra cups with massage elements.  Details of corsets, girdles or brassieres relating to materials used in making process thereof, e.g. elastic corsets including corsets made of rubber, or of layered materials; special materials for nursing mother brassieres or brassieres for sport activities.  Machines or appliances for manufacturing corsets or brassieres, such as integrated manufacturing system or production lines including, e.g. cutting or sewing machines, ironing-machines, attachment mechanisms for assembling component parts, circular knitting machines, machines for molding cup-shaped parts.  Methods for manufacturing corsets or brassieres, mostly the combination of individual process steps such as mechanical treatment e. g. pattern cutting or sewing, physical treatment with the use of high temperature or pressure e.g. hot pressing forming die for a brassiere cup, thermoforming for shape retention or hardening of the articles, high temperature adhering and pressing process without sewing, ultrasonic adhering, antibacterial treatment, aromatizing or steeping by perfume, chemical methods for densification of the knitted stitches; methods of manufacturing one piece bra; finishing edges.  **Glossary:** - Corset: (n) tight-fitting undergarments often reinforced by stays, worn to support and shape the waistline, hips, and breasts. - Girdle: (n) a woman's corset extending from waist to thigh - Stay: (n) a flat strip of steel, plastic, etc., used especially for stiffening corsets, collars, etc. (in.pl.) hist. a corset esp. with whalebone etc. stiffening, and laced - Steel: (n) a steel stiffener in a corset, etc.

[A41D] OUTERWEAR -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Outerwear covers athletic garments, jackets, trousers, shorts, skirts, maternity clothing, overgarments (overcoats, capes), dressing-gowns, bathing costumes, and pyjamas.  In particular, the following subjects are classified as follows:  **Glossary:** - Dressing-gown: a loose gown worn while making one's toilet or when in dishabille. - Jacket: lightweight long-sleeve garment typically having a collar and lapel extending below the front sides of the collar providing a central opening between the lapels, also referred to as a dress jacket or sport coat - Waistcoat: upper body garment without sleeves also referred to as a vest in American English - Vest: T-shirt or casual top with or without sleeves - Jersey: knitted garment with or without sleeves, also referred to as a sweater in American English - Sweater: garment typically worn for playing team sports, also referred to as a jersey in American English - Blouse: shirt having features specific to women, also referred to as a women's top - Overalls: a one-piece garment having an upper body portion (e.g. short or long sleeve top, bib secured by shoulder straps, etc.) and lower body portion (e.g. pants, shorts, etc.), also referred to as coveralls or bodysuits - Leggings: lower body overgarment with or without seats for protecting a garment worn underneath, also referred to as chaps - Shirt: an upper body garment having a collar and an opening at the front which is fastened closed via fastening elements (e.g. buttons) disposed adjacent the opening, also referred to as a men's dress shirt, button-up shirt or button-down shirt - Chemise: undergarment with shoulder strap worn beneath dress or the like, also referred to as a slip - Bodices: baby garment usually with sleeves but leaving the legs uncovered and fastening means at the crotch, also referred to as a onesie

[A41D 13/0056] using evaporative effect -- The subclass A41D 13/0056 covers protective outerwear such as surgical gowns or gear that defends against impacts and incorporates an evaporative effect to remove moisture and provide cooling. This evaporative function may be achieved through specially treated fabrics, coatings or built-in ventilation that enhances wearer comfort while maintaining protection. It falls under the CPC hierarchy for professional, industrial or sporting protective garments.

[A41D 13/0575] in an openable pocket -- A41D 13/0575 classifies protective outerwear-such as surgical gowns or impact-resistant garments-that include an openable pocket, the pocket being integrated into the garment's protective structure for convenient access or storage while preserving the garment's safety function.
[A41D 13/0581] with permanent fastening means -- A41D 13/0581 classifies protective outerwear such as surgical gowns, industrial work coats or sports protective garments that incorporate permanent fastening means like zippers, snaps, Velcro or welded seams. The subclass is intended for items designed to guard the wearer against hazards, impact or contamination and where the fasteners are integral to the garment's structure.
[A41D 13/0587] Integral with the garment -- A41D 13/0587 covers protective outerwear in which the safety features are built directly into the garment's structure, such as integrated padding, armor plates or shielding that cannot be removed separately. The classification applies to professional, industrial or sporting garments designed to guard the wearer against impacts, blows or other hazards, where the protective element is an inherent part of the clothing.
[A41D 13/0593] in a sealed pocket -- A41D 13/0593 designates protective outerwear that incorporates a sealed pocket, allowing the wearer to store items isolated from the environment. It applies to professional, industrial or sporting garments such as surgeons' gowns or impact-resistant apparel where containment of liquids, gases or contaminants is required.

This subcategory falls under the hierarchy of human necessities → personal or domestic articles → outerwear → components of garments.

[A41D 2300/322] using slide fasteners -- This subclass relates to outerwear garments that incorporate slide fasteners, commonly known as zippers, as a means of joining or adjusting parts of the clothing. [A41D 2300/326] using hooks and eyelets -- This subclass covers outerwear and garment details that incorporate hooks or eyelets for fastening, adjustment or decorative purposes, such as jackets, coats and similar apparel where these elements are integral to the construction.
[A41D 2300/328] using adhesive -- A41D 2300/328 covers outerwear garments where adhesives are employed in construction or repair, including bonding fabrics, seams, trims or accessories without stitching. The classification applies to methods and materials that use adhesive bonding to assemble or modify coats, jackets and similar apparel. It is a subcategory of details of garments within personal or domestic articles.
[A41D 2300/33] using straps or ties -- A41D 2300/33 classifies outerwear garments whose construction or adjustment involves straps or ties, covering features that fasten, secure or decorate coats, jackets and similar apparel by means of such elements.

The classification addresses methods, constructions and arrangements of elasticized sections within these clothing items.
[A41D 2300/50] Seams -- A41D 2300/50 refers to the technical features of seams used in outerwear garments. It covers the construction, joining methods and characteristics of stitching that connect fabric pieces in coats, jackets and similar clothing. This subclass groups inventions that improve seam strength, flexibility, waterproofing or aesthetic appearance.
[A41D 2300/52] made by welding or gluing -- A41D 2300/52 designates outerwear details that are assembled by welding or gluing instead of stitching, covering construction elements such as seams, panels or decorative parts that are joined using these adhesive or fused methods.
[A41D 2400/00] Functions or special features of garments -- A41D 2400/00 classifies garments based on their functional or special features, such as thermal regulation, waterproofing, reinforcement, safety elements or other performance-enhancing properties. It applies to outerwear items whose design provides specific benefits beyond basic covering.
[A41D 2400/10] Heat retention or warming -- A41D 2400/10 covers outerwear that incorporates heat-retaining or warming functions, such as insulated jackets, thermal linings, heating elements, or other mechanisms designed to keep the wearer warm. It is used to classify garments whose primary purpose is to provide thermal protection in cold environments.
[A41D 2400/12] using temperature-controlled means -- The A41D 2400/12 entry covers outerwear equipped with temperature-controlled means, such as heating or cooling elements integrated into garments to regulate the wearer's climate. [A41D 2400/14] inflatable -- The A41D 2400/14 subclass pertains to outerwear that incorporates inflatable components, such as jackets, vests or suits designed to expand for added insulation, buoyancy or protective cushioning. It covers garments whose primary function or special feature relies on an inflatable structure integrated into the clothing.
[A41D 2400/24] Reducing drag or turbulence in air or water -- The subclass A41D 2400/24 relates to outerwear engineered to lower aerodynamic or hydrodynamic resistance, such as streamlined suits, tight-fit garments, or fabrics with surface treatments that reduce drag in air or water. It includes designs that guide airflow or water flow over the body to improve performance for activities like swimming, cycling, skiing, or wind-assisted sports.
[A41D 2400/26] UV light protection -- A41D 2400/26 covers garments designed to protect the wearer from ultraviolet radiation, such as UV-filtering fabrics or coatings applied to outerwear. It falls under functions or special features of garments within the outerwear subclass.

[A41D 2600/00] Uses of garments specially adapted for specific purposes -- A41D 2600/00 covers the specific uses of outerwear that has been specially adapted for particular purposes, such as protective, therapeutic, athletic, ceremonial or occupational functions. The classification looks at how the garment is employed rather than its materials or construction, including adaptations for weather, safety, health or specialized activities.
[A41D 2600/10] for sport activities -- A41D 2600/10 designates outerwear specially adapted for sport activities, covering clothing such as sports jackets, training coats, and other garments engineered to provide protection, comfort or performance during athletic or recreational exercise.
[A41D 2600/102] Motorcycling -- A41D 2600/102 covers garments specially adapted for motorcycling, including jackets, trousers, gloves, boots and related protective wear that provide abrasion resistance, impact cushioning and weather protection for riders. The classification applies to clothing incorporating reinforced panels, armor inserts, ventilation or reflective elements intended for use on motorcycles.
[A41D 2600/104] Cycling -- A41D 2600/104 classifies garments and accessories specially designed for cycling, including padded shorts, form-fitting jerseys, tights, and protective outerwear that enhance rider comfort, aerodynamics and safety. The code covers apparel adapted to the specific functional and ergonomic demands of bike riding.
[A41D 2600/106] Fishing -- A41D 2600/106 covers outerwear specially adapted for fishing activities, including garments designed to protect the wearer from water, wind, and cold while allowing freedom of movement for casting, handling equipment, and navigating wet environments. This classification groups fishing-specific apparel within the broader categories of personal or domestic articles and outerwear.
[A41D 2600/108] Hunting -- A41D 2600/108 covers outerwear designed specifically for hunting, including garments and accessories that provide camouflage, durability, and functionality for hunting activities. The classification pertains to
[A41D 2600/20] for working activities -- A41D 2600/20 covers outerwear specially designed for occupational use, including work jackets, coveralls, and similar garments that provide protection, utility or visibility for manual, industrial or technical tasks. The subclass groups clothing adapted to the demands of specific working activities.
[A41D 2600/202] Welding -- A41D 2600/202 covers protective outerwear specially adapted for welding, such as jackets, coats, and sleeves that incorporate heat-resistant and spark-proof materials to shield the wearer from sparks, flames and intense temperatures encountered during welding operations. This subclass falls under the broader categories of human necessities, personal or domestic articles, and outerwear.
[A41D 27/00] Details of garments or of their making -- A41D 27/00 relates to the specific features and construction of garments, covering elements such as seams, trims, fastenings, decorative stitching and other detailed aspects of how a piece of clothing is made. It includes descriptions of these particulars and the methods used to produce them. This subclass falls under outerwear within the broader category of personal or domestic articles.

[A41G] ARTIFICIAL FLOWERS -- In this subclass, non-limiting references (in the sense of paragraph 39 of the Guide to the IPC) may still be displayed in the scheme.
[A41G 1/00] Artificial flowers, fruit, leaves, or trees -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Artificial Christmas trees -> A47G33/06  **Glossary:** - Garland: A wreath made of flowers, leaves, etc., worn on the head like a crown, or hung about an object for decoration.
[A41G 1/001] characterised by their special functions -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Floral handling -> A01G5/06 - Christmas tree decorations -> A47G33/08 - Processes for producing ornamental bodies, natural ornaments and imitations -> B44C5/06
[A41G 1/002] Animated or provided with moving parts -- A41G 1/002 is a CPC subclass for artificial flowers, fruit, leaves or trees that incorporate moving parts or animation. It includes designs where components such as petals, stems or branches are mechanically or electrically actuated to create motion for decorative or novelty purposes. This subclass groups inventions that add dynamic features to otherwise static artificial botanical items.
[A41G 1/003] Emitting sounds or music -- Artificial flowers, fruit, leaves or trees that incorporate a sound-producing element, such as a music box, speaker or other acoustic device, are covered by this subclass. It includes designs where the decorative plant replica emits music, tones or other audible effects for novelty or ornamental purposes.
[A41G 1/004] Changing colors -- This subclass covers methods for altering the colour of artificial flowers, fruit, leaves or trees, such as applying dyes, pigments, light-responsive coatings or interchangeable colour elements, allowing the appearance to change after manufacture.
[A41G 1/005] luminous or luminescent -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Decoration with light effects, holograms -> B44F1/10 - Lighting with household decorative objects -> F21V33/0028 - Indicating arrangements with diodes -> G09F9/33 - Illuminated signs, luminous advertising being electroluminescent -> G09F13/22 - Circuits for light sources, controlling -> H05B47/105
[A41G 1/006] Diffusing perfume or the like -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Holders for deodorisation -> A61L9/12 - Scents -> C11B9/00
[A41G 1/007] Artificial trees -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Artificial Christmas trees -> A47G33/06
[A41G 1/008] Artificial fruits or vegetables -- A41G 1/008 refers to artificial fruits or vegetables, including imitation produce used for decorative or display purposes such as fake fruit, vegetables, leaves, and trees. These items are classified under artificial flowers within the broader categories of personal or domestic articles and human necessities.
[A41G 1/009] Artificial grass  surfaces simulating grass  E01C13/08 -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Floral handling -> A01G5/06 - Christmas tree decorations -> A47G33/08 - Processes for producing ornamental bodies, natural ornaments and imitations -> B44C5/06 - Formation of filaments -> D01D5/42 - Flexible sheet glued on macromolecular material, characterized by backing -> D06N7/0036 - Models for demonstration purposes (trees, rocks,..) -> G09B25/08  **Glossary:** - Turf: A sod of grass, with the roots and earth adhering.
[A41G 1/02] Implements, apparatus, or machines for making artificial flowers, or the like -- A41G 1/02 covers machines, tools and apparatus designed for manufacturing artificial flowers, fruit, leaves or trees, including devices that shape, assemble or finish synthetic stems, petals and foliage. The subclass groups equipment used in producing decorative plant replicas for commercial or hobby use.
[A41G 1/04] Garlands -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group.  Multicolour garland fabricating method for use in decoration field, involves taking garland in stack of sheet, taking another garland to ream another stack of sheet, and integrating ends of stacks to combine two garlands together.  **Glossary:** - Garland: A wreath made of flowers, leaves, etc., worn on the head like a crown, or hung about an object for decoration.
[A41G 11/00] Artificial feathers -- A41G 11/00 designates artificial feathers, including synthetic or man-made feather structures used for decorative purposes such as trimming garments, hats, costumes and ornamental arrangements. The class covers materials that imitate the appearance, texture or colour of natural feathers for fashion, accessory and decorative applications.
[A41G 11/02] Implements or machines for making artificial feathers -- A41G 11/02 covers devices, tools and machines specifically designed to produce artificial feathers, such as equipment that cuts, shapes, dyes or assembles synthetic materials to resemble natural plumage. The classification is used for inventions related to the manufacture of artificial feathers for decorative or functional purposes.

[A41G 3/0008] Fastening thereof -- A41G 3/0008 covers the means of attaching or securing wigs, a subcategory of artificial flowers within personal or domestic articles. It includes fasteners, clips, adhesives and other devices used to hold wigs in place on the head. This entry falls under the hierarchy of human necessities → personal or domestic articles → artificial flowers → wigs.
[A41G 3/0016] by adjusting or elastic means -- A41G 3/0016 covers wigs that are shaped, secured or altered by adjustment mechanisms or elastic components, such as stretchable bands, straps, hooks or other movable parts that enable the wig to fit or be styled to the wearer's head. This subclass falls under artificial flowers within personal or domestic articles, focusing on devices that provide a flexible or adaptable fit.
[A41G 3/0025] by adhesive means -- A41G 3/0025 covers artificial flower products, such as wigs, that are attached or secured by adhesive means. It includes methods and materials for bonding the artificial hair or fibers to a base, focusing on glues, tapes or similar substances used to affix the wig.
[A41G 3/0033] by mechanical fasteners, e.g. clasps or combs -- A41G 3/0033 designates wig constructions that are secured to the head by mechanical fasteners such as clasps or combs, distinguishing these attachment methods from adhesives, elastic bands or other means. This subclass falls under artificial flowers within personal or domestic articles of the human necessities group.
[A41G 3/0041] Bases for wigs -- A41G 3/0041 covers structural elements that support or secure wigs to the wearer's head, including clips, pads, combs, netting, straps, adhesives and other fastening devices. These bases may be integrated into the wig or supplied separately to improve fit, stability or comfort. The code applies to both detachable and permanently attached wig support systems.

[A41H] APPLIANCES OR METHODS FOR MAKING CLOTHES, e.g. FOR DRESS-MAKING OR FOR TAILORING, NOT OTHERWISE PROVIDED FOR -- In this subclass, non-limiting references (in the sense of paragraph 39 of the Guide to the IPC) may still be displayed in the scheme.
[A41H 1/00] Measuring aids or methods -- A41H 1/00 covers tools and techniques that assist in measuring dimensions for garment construction, fabric cutting or body fitting, such as rulers, tape measures, gauges, fit-checking devices and related methods. These aids are used to obtain accurate length, width, circumference or other size data during dress-making, tailoring or related apparel processes.
[A41H 1/02] Devices for taking measurements on the human body -- A41H 1/02 covers devices used to obtain dimensional or physiological measurements of the human body, such as body-size gauges, posture analyzers, or scanning instruments. These tools are intended for tailoring, garment fitting, or health-related assessments.
[A41H 1/04] Stands for taking measurements -- A41H 1/04 covers stands designed to hold a person or garment steady while body measurements are taken, aiding accurate sizing in dress-making or tailoring. These devices are classified under appliances or methods for making clothes within the human necessities hierarchy.
[A41H 1/06] in combination with marking -- A41H 1/06 covers measuring devices for clothing production that incorporate a marking function, such as rulers or gauges with built-in pens or indicia for directly indicating measurements on fabric. The classification applies to tools that simultaneously measure and apply marks to aid cutting, stitching or tailoring processes.
[A41H 1/10] Measuring jackets for marking-out patterns -- A41H 1/10 covers measuring jackets designed to aid in marking out patterns on garments. These tools are used by tailors and dressmakers to transfer measurements onto fabric, facilitating accurate cutting and fitting. They belong to the subgroup of clothing-making appliances dealing with pattern
[A41H 11/00] Tracing-wheels -- A41H 11/00 covers tracing-wheels, the handheld tools used in dress-making and tailoring to transfer pattern lines onto fabric. The device typically consists of a wheel with teeth or rollers that roll over a pattern and imprint marks on the cloth, allowing precise cutting and stitching. It is classified under appliances for making clothes within the broader human necessities category.
[A41H 15/00] Cloth-holders -- A41H 15/00 covers devices designed to hold or support fabric during garment construction or handling, such as clothespins, pins, clamps, hangers and similar accessories used by dress-makers, tailors or in domestic laundry. It falls under appliances for making clothes within the personal or domestic articles category.

These items are classified as personal or domestic articles related to the preparation of clothing.
[A41H 21/00] Supports for the body whilst trying-on or fitting -- A41H 21/00 covers devices that support or hold a person's body while trial-fitting garments, such as dress forms, mannequins, adjustable supports and similar apparatus that aid accurate measurement or adjustment of clothing during trying-on.
[A41H 23/00] Devices for applying chalk -- A41H 23/00 covers equipment designed to apply chalk to fabrics, such as chalk pencils, dispensers or marking tools used by tailors and dressmakers to trace patterns, seams or fitting lines on material. These devices facilitate precise temporary markings during garment construction.
[A41H 25/00] Appliances or methods for marking-out, perforating or making buttonholes -- A41H 25/00 relates to devices and techniques used to mark, perforate or create buttonholes in fabrics, typically employed in dress-making, tailoring and other garment-construction processes. It falls within the CPC hierarchy for personal or domestic articles, specifically appliances for making clothes.
[A41H 25/02] Buttonhole shears -- Buttonhole shears are specialized cutting tools designed to create precise, clean buttonholes in fabric, featuring a narrow blade and a guide to control the shape and size of the opening. They are used in tailoring and dress-making for consistent, professional results.

It includes templates, layout sheets and cutting guides used by dress-makers or tailors to define the shape and size of each part before sewing.

[A41H 3/04] Making patterns by modelling on the human body -- A41H 3/04 covers the creation of garment patterns by shaping them directly on a person's body, using the wearer as a three-dimensional model to generate the flat cutting outlines. This method replaces traditional flat-pattern drafting with direct body-based measurement and marking to produce the final pattern pieces.
[A41H 3/06] Patterns on paper -- A41H 3/06 covers paper patterns employed in garment construction, such as templates drawn or printed for dress-making or tailoring to guide the cutting of fabric pieces. It lies under the broader categories for personal or domestic articles and appliances or methods for making clothes, specifically addressing patterned cloth for dress-making or tailoring.
[A41H 31/00] Other aids for tailors -- A41H 31/00 covers auxiliary devices and tools that assist tailors in garment construction, such as measuring gauges, pattern-making aids, cutting guides, marking instruments and specialized pressing equipment, which are not classified elsewhere under basic sewing or cutting machinery. [A42] HEADWEAR -- A42 headwear includes items designed to be worn on the head such as hats, caps, helmets, hairnets and other coverings used for fashion, protection or cultural purposes. The class covers a wide range of materials, forms and accessory features, encompassing both everyday and specialised protective headgear.

[A42B 1/008] with means for heating or cooling -- This subclass covers hats that incorporate integrated heating or cooling features, such as
[A42B 1/009] with means for light reflection -- A42B 1/009 covers hats that incorporate means for light reflection, such as reflective strips, fibers, or coated surfaces, to enhance visibility in low-light conditions. The classification applies to headwear designed for safety or signaling purposes by reflecting ambient or vehicle light.
[A42B 1/011] Arrangements for deodorising or perfuming -- A42B 1/011 relates to hats that incorporate systems for deodorising or perfuming, including built-in or attachable devices that release fragrance or neutralise odours while the headwear is worn. This entry falls under Hats in the Headwear group of the Human Necessities section of the CPC.
[A42B 1/012] Sanitary or disposable, e.g. for use in hospitals or food industry -- A42B 1/012 covers hats that are designed to be sanitary or disposable, intended for single-use situations such as hospitals, food processing or other hygienic environments. These items are typically made of lightweight, low-cost materials and are meant to be discarded after use.
[A42B 1/014] specially adapted for protection against insects -- A42B 1/014 covers hats that are specially adapted to protect the wearer from insects, such as those equipped with nets, screens or other barrier features. These designs are intended for outdoor use where insect bites or stings are a concern.
[A42B 1/017] specially adapted for promoting health, e.g. with means for pressing on acupuncture points -- A42B 1/017 covers hats that are designed to promote health, for example by incorporating elements that press acupuncture points on the head. Such headwear integrates therapeutic features, like protrusions or pressure zones, intended to stimulate specific points for wellness benefits.
[A42B 1/0175] using magnets -- A42B 1/0175 pertains to hats that incorporate magnets for purposes such as fastening, adjusting fit, attaching accessories, or providing magnetic closures. The subclass includes designs where magnetic elements replace or supplement conventional fasteners, enabling parts of the headwear to be joined or repositioned magnetically.
[A42B 1/018] with means for protecting the eyes, ears or nape, e.g. sun or rain shields -- A42B 1/018 covers hats that integrate protective elements for the eyes, ears or nape, such as built-in visors, ear flaps or rear shields designed to guard the wearer against sunlight, rain or other environmental exposure.
[A42B 1/0181] with means for protecting the eyes -- A42B 1/0181 is a Cooperative Patent Classification sub-group for hats that incorporate features to protect the eyes, such as brims, visors or built-in eye shields. It falls under the hierarchy Human Necessities → Headwear → Hats, and covers designs where eye protection is an integral part of the headgear.
[A42B 1/0182] Peaks or visors -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Eyeshades -> A61F9/045
[A42B 1/0183] with stiffening means -- A42B 1/0183 covers hats that incorporate stiffening means to retain or enhance their shape, such as brims, crowns or edges reinforced with wire, plastic, or other rigid materials. This sub-class is used for designs where structural support is a defining feature of the headwear.
[A42B 1/0184] detachable or movable, e.g. rotatable -- The subclass A42B 1/0184 covers hats that include elements which can be detached or moved, for example parts that rotate to adjust fit, style or function, enabling the headgear to be reconfigured or altered after manufacture.
[A42B 1/0185] extendable, e.g. telescopic -- The subclass A42B 1/0185 covers hats equipped with an extendable or telescopic mechanism that lets the brim, crown or other parts be lengthened or shortened for adjustable size or shape. It includes designs where sections slide relative to each other to provide a variable fit or style.
[A42B 1/0186] with means for protecting the ears or nape -- A42B 1/0186 covers hats that incorporate features designed to shield the ears or the nape of the neck from weather or impact. Such protective elements may include flaps, ear covers or extended brims integrated into the headwear.
[A42B 1/0187] Removable protection for the nape, e.g. sun or rain shields -- A42B 1/0187 concerns hats equipped with a detachable element that protects the wearer's nape, such as a shield against sun or rain. The removable nape cover can be added to or removed from the hat as needed, providing flexible weather protection.
[A42B 1/0188] Protection for the ears, e.g. removable ear muffs -- A42B 1/0188 designates ear-protecting accessories intended for use with hats, such as removable ear muffs that shield the ears from cold, wind or noise. The entry covers structures or elements that can be attached to or integrated into headwear and later detached.
[A42B 1/0189] Linings -- A42B 1/0189 in the Cooperative Patent Classification pertains to the inner linings of hats, covering materials and structures that form the interior of the crown, brim or band. It includes fabrics, padding, insulating or moisture-wicking layers used to shape, support, protect or enhance wearer comfort.
[A42B 1/019] characterised by their material -- A42B 1/019 falls under the Cooperative Patent Classification for headwear, specifically hats, and defines hat designs that are distinguished primarily by the material from which they are made, such as textile, leather, metal or synthetic compounds. This subclass groups inventions where the choice of material is the key technical feature.
[A42B 1/0192] Paper -- A42B 1/0192 refers to hats whose primary material is paper, encompassing the design, construction and finishing of paper headwear such as novelty caps, sun hats or promotional foam-like hats. The subclass includes methods of shaping, reinforcing and decorating paper hats, as well as variations that combine paper with other lightweight layers for stability or aesthetic effect.
[A42B 1/0195] Antimicrobial or antibacterial -- A42B 1/0195 is a CPC subclass for headwear, specifically hats, that are treated to provide antimicrobial or antibacterial effects, for example by incorporating silver, copper, biocidal chemicals or other agents that inhibit microbial growth on the hat's surface.
[A42B 1/02] Hats -- A42B 1/02 pertains to hats, a subset of headgear that covers items such as brimmed caps, skull caps, and other forms of head coverings worn for protection, fashion, or ceremonial purposes. This subclass groups together all types of hats regardless of material or specific style, distinguishing them from other headwear like helmets or hair accessories.
[A42B 1/04] Soft caps -- A42B 1/04 pertains to soft caps, flexible head coverings made from textile, knit, or similar pliable materials and lacking a rigid brim. These caps are designed for casual wear, sports, or protective purposes and may include features such as adjustable straps, ear flaps, or ventilation openings.
[A42B 1/041] Peakless soft head coverings, e.g. turbans or berets -- A42B 1/041 covers peakless soft head coverings, such as turbans and berets, that are made of flexible or pliable material and lack a brim or projecting peak. The classification is used for designs that provide a smooth, rounded fit on the head without rigid structure.
[A42B 1/045] fastened under the chin, e.g. bonnets -- A42B 1/045 covers headwear that is fastened under the chin, such as bonnets, describing a chin-secured fastening method to keep the hat in place.
[A42B 1/046] Balaclavas -- Balaclavas are close-fitting head coverings that enclose the entire head and neck, leaving openings for the eyes and often the mouth, typically made of knitted or sewn material for warmth or protective purposes. They fall within the A42B 1/046 subclass for hats that fully cover the wearer's head and face.
[A42B 1/048] Detachable hoods -- A42B 1/048 covers hats that incorporate a hood which can be removed or separated from the main headwear. The classification includes designs where the hood is attached by fasteners, straps, or detachable connections, allowing the wearer to add or discard the hood as needed. It applies to any head covering where the hood is not permanently fixed.

[A42B 1/06] Caps with flaps -- This CPC subclass A42B 1/06 covers caps that incorporate flaps, such as visors or ear coverings, intended to protect the wearer's head from sun, rain or cold. It is nested under Human Necessities > Headwear > Hats.
[A42B 1/08] with protection against blows -- A42B 1/08 covers hats that provide protection against blows, including safety helmets, hard hats and other headgear designed to absorb or deflect impact to safeguard the wearer's head.
[A42B 1/10] Miners' caps -- A42B 1/10 refers to miners' caps, a type of protective headwear intended for use in mining environments, typically constructed from hard or reinforced material to shield the wearer from falling rock, debris and other occupational hazards.
[A42B 1/12] Bathing caps -- A42B 1/12 covers bathing caps, which are close-fitting, waterproof or water-resistant head coverings worn during swimming, bathing or water-based activities to keep the hair dry and reduce drag. The caps are typically made of rubber, silicone, latex, neoprene or similar flexible materials and may include a strap or elastic band to secure them on the head.
[A42B 1/14] Straw hats -- A42B 1/14 covers hats made primarily from woven or plaited straw, including summer caps, boater-style headgear and other lightweight, breathable styles worn for protection from sun or for fashion. The classification includes all variations where straw is the main structural material, irrespective of brim shape or decorative trim.
[A42B 1/16] Millinery -- A42B 1/16 covers millinery, the design and manufacture of women's hats and headwear. It includes the shaping, trimming and decorative techniques used to create fashionable hats, as well as the materials and construction methods specific to such accessories. This subclass falls under Human Necessities → Headwear → Hats in the CPC system.
[A42B 1/18] Coverings for protecting hats, caps or hoods against dust, rain, or sunshine -- In CPC A42B 1/18 the focus is on accessories that shield hats, caps or hoods from environmental elements such as dust, rain or sunlight. These coverings are designed to be attached to or used with headwear to preserve the garment and improve wearer comfort.
[A42B 1/201] Collapsible or foldable -- A42B 1/201 covers hats that are designed to be collapsed or folded, allowing them to be compactly stored or transported. It includes any structural features or mechanisms that let the crown or brim be flattened, rolled or otherwise reduced in size without damage.
[A42B 1/203] Inflatable -- A42B 1/203 relates to hats that incorporate inflatable elements, such as air-filled crowns, brims or decorative structures, enabling the hat to change shape, size, or provide cushioning. These inventions typically use gas-filled chambers or flexible membranes to achieve the inflatable property.
[A42B 1/205] made of separable parts -- A42B 1/205 relates to headwear, specifically hats that are constructed from separable parts, such as detachable brims, crowns, or decorative elements that can be assembled, removed or interchanged to alter the hat's appearance or function. This subclass groups designs where the hat's components are not permanently fixed.
[A42B 1/206] Transformable, convertible or reversible hats -- A42B 1/206 covers hats that can be altered in form or function, including reversible designs, convertible styles that change shape or purpose, and other headwear that can be transformed or reconfigured for different uses.
[A42B 1/208] made from a flat sheet -- A42B 1/208 covers hats that are produced from a single flat sheet of material, such as fabric, paper or metal, which is shaped into a head covering without additional assembled parts. This subclass relates to headwear formed by folding, curling or otherwise manipulating a flat sheet to create the hat's structure.

[A42B 1/241] Pockets therefor -- A42B 1/241 covers hats that incorporate pockets, such as concealed compartments or external pouches integrated into the headwear for storing small items. It relates to design features that provide functional storage within the hat structure.

[A42B 3/0486] Airbags -- Airbags are inflatable protective devices designed to cushion and safeguard the wearer's head and face during impact, commonly integrated into helmets or headgear to absorb shock and reduce injury. They function by rapidly filling with gas upon collision, creating a barrier that distributes forces over a larger area. This entry classifies the technology of such safety accessories within helmets under the CPC heading for headwear.
[A42B 3/0493] Aerodynamic helmets; Air guiding means therefor -- This CPC subclass covers helmets that are shaped or equipped to improve aerodynamics, typically for sports or motor activities. Features include streamlined shells, air passages, vents, or other means that direct airflow over the head to reduce drag and improve stability.
[A42B 3/06] Impact-absorbing shells, e.g. of crash helmets -- A42B 3/06 covers helmets whose outer shell is designed to absorb and dissipate impact energy, typically by using deformable or energy-absorbing materials that protect the wearer during a crash. The classification includes crash helmets and similar protective headgear where the shell itself provides the primary shock-attenuation function.

[A42B 3/062] with reinforcing means -- A42B 3/062 covers helmets that incorporate reinforcing means, such as internal frames, ribs, plates or other structural elements, to increase strength or impact resistance. The reinforcement may be metal, composite, polymer or another material and is integrated into the helmet shell or liner.
[A42B 3/063] using layered structures -- A42B 3/063 relates to helmets that achieve protection or other performance benefits by employing layered constructions, such as multiple material sheets, laminates, or composite layers arranged sequentially. The subclass covers designs where the layers serve to absorb impact energy, improve strength, or provide additional functions within the head-wear device.
[A42B 3/064] with relative movement between layers -- A42B 3/064 covers helmets whose construction permits relative movement between two or more layers, such as inner liners that can slide, rotate or shift against an outer shell to improve ventilation, impact absorption or fit. Examples include helmets with detachable padding, adjustable cushions or mechanically decoupled outer and inner parts.
[A42B 3/065] Corrugated or ribbed shells -- A42B 3/065 covers helmets whose outer shell is formed with corrugated or ribbed structures, using ridges or pleats to reinforce strength and improve impact resistance. [A42B 3/283] Air inlets or outlets, with or without closure shutters -- A42B 3/283 covers designs for helmets that incorporate passages for ventilation, including air inlets and outlets, and may also include mechanisms such as shutters or covers that can open or close these openings.
