[A43D 21/122] with pincers and clamps -- A43D 21/122 covers lasting machines that incorporate pincers and clamps for shaping and securing shoe uppers during the manufacturing process. The subclass falls under Human Necessities → Footwear → Machines, tools, equipment or methods for manufacturing or repairing footwear, and specifies devices that use clamping mechanisms to hold the material while it is stretched over a lasting form.
[A43D 21/125] with a plurality of pincers  A43D21/122  takes precedence -- A43D 21/125 in the Cooperative Patent Classification refers to lasting machines for footwear that utilize multiple pincers. This entry specifies a method or device where several pincers are employed to hold and shape the shoe upper onto the last, with A43D 21/122 taking precedence when applicable.
[A43D 21/127] with wipers  A43D21/16 ,  A43D21/18  take precedence -- A43D 21/127 in the Cooperative Patent Classification refers to lasting machines for footwear that incorporate wipers, though entries A43D21/16 and A43D21/18 have priority when applicable.
[A43D 21/14] for toe or heel parts, with nailing devices -- A43D 21/14 covers lasting machines that form toe or heel sections of footwear and incorporate nailing devices to attach these parts. The classification is used for equipment that shapes and secures the upper portions of shoes during manufacturing, specifically those that combine lasting and nail-driven fastening.
[A43D 21/16] with lasting pincers toe- or heel-embracing wipers -- A43D 21/16 covers lasting machines that use lasting pincers fitted with toe- or heel-embracing wipers, which clean or protect the material around the toe or heel during the lasting operation. The wipers help preserve shape and prevent damage while the shoe is formed.
[A43D 21/163] with heel-embracing wipers -- A43D 21/163 covers lasting machines for footwear that include heel-embracing wipers, devices that sweep or clean the heel region of a shoe during the lasting process to improve fit and finish. The wipers are positioned to contact the heel as the upper is shaped over the last.
[A43D 21/166] with toe-embracing wipers -- This subclass of the Cooperative Patent Classification covers lasting machines for footwear that incorporate toe-embracing wiper devices, which engage the toe region during the lasting process to smooth, shape or finish the toe area. It is used for inventions that add such wipers to improve the quality or efficiency of shoe manufacturing.
[A43D 21/18] with lasting pincers and straight-acting wipers, also for forming the shank portions of shoes -- A43D 21/18 covers lasting machines equipped with lasting pincers and straight-acting wipers, which can also shape the shank parts of shoes. The devices are used in footwear manufacturing to hold and form the upper around the last while simultaneously forming the supportive shank section.
[A43D 2200/00] Machines or methods characterised by special features -- A43D 2200/00 relates to machines or methods for manufacturing or repairing footwear that incorporate particular technical features, such as specialized stitching, bonding, shaping or finishing mechanisms. It covers equipment and processes that go beyond basic shoe-making operations by providing distinctive functional or structural elements.
[A43D 2200/10] Fully automated machines, i.e. machines working without human intervention -- A43D 2200/10 designates fully automated footwear-manufacturing or repair machines that operate without human intervention, encompassing robotic or computer-controlled systems that handle all production steps from material feeding to finishing. This subclass falls under Human Necessities → Footwear → Machines, tools, equipment or methods for manufacturing or repairing footwear → Machines or methods characterised by special features.
[A43D 2200/20] Machines including a carousel with different working posts around a turning table -- A43D 2200/20 refers to machines or methods for making or repairing footwear that incorporate a carousel layout, with several work stations positioned around a rotating table. The classification captures apparatus where the turning table moves the footwear through different processing steps, enabling continuous or semi-continuous production. This entry falls under the CPC hierarchy for footwear manufacturing equipment.
[A43D 2200/30] Machines including a magazine, e.g. for feeding blanks, pre-forms or sheets -- This subgroup covers machines for manufacturing or repairing footwear that incorporate a magazine to hold and feed material such as blanks, pre-forms or sheets into the processing unit. It falls under the footwear equipment hierarchy of the CPC.
[A43D 2200/40] Multitool machines, e.g. machines with a plurality of tools or working heads -- A43D 2200/40 covers multitool machines used in footwear production, such as equipment that incorporates several tools or working heads in a single device. These machines combine functions like cutting, stitching, shaping or assembling to streamline shoe manufacturing processes. The classification groups apparatus with multiple interchangeable or simultaneous operations for making or repairing shoes.
[A43D 2200/50] Machines using pneumatic means -- A43D 2200/50 covers machines or methods for manufacturing or repairing footwear that are powered by compressed air, such as pneumatic presses, air-driven stitching devices and other air-operated equipment. It falls under the CPC group for footwear-related machinery and tools.
[A43D 2200/60] Computer aided manufacture of footwear, e.g. CAD or CAM -- A43D 2200/60 covers computer-aided processes for making shoes, such as using CAD (computer-aided design) or CAM (computer-aided manufacturing) systems to design, model, or drive machinery that produces footwear. It includes both software tools and the automated equipment that apply digital data to fabricate shoe components.
[A43D 23/00] Single parts for pulling-over or lasting machines -- A43D 23/00 designates individual components employed in pulling-over or lasting machines, which are specialized devices used to shape and attach uppers to shoe soles during footwear production. The code falls under the CPC hierarchy for machines, tools, equipment or methods for manufacturing or repairing footwear.
[A43D 23/02] Wipers -- The A43D 23/02 subgroup covers wipers that are fitted to pulling-over or lasting machines used in footwear production, serving to remove excess material, smooth the shoe surface, or prevent sticking during the lasting process. These devices are integral components of machines that shape and finish shoes.
[A43D 23/022] Sole-pressers -- A43D 23/022 designates sole-pressers, which are individual components of pulling-over or lasting machines used in the production or repair of footwear. They press the sole onto the shoe upper during the lasting process.
[A43D 23/025] Last-supports -- A43D 23/025 covers single components that support the last-the foot-shaped form-within pulling-over or lasting machines used for shoe manufacturing. These parts hold the last in place during the lasting process, ensuring proper shaping of the footwear upper. It falls under the CPC hierarchy for machines, tools or equipment for making or repairing footwear.
[A43D 23/027] Pincers -- A43D 23/027 refers to pincers used as single parts in pulling-over or lasting machines, which are specialized tools designed for the manufacturing or repairing of footwear. These pincers are essential for gripping and shaping materials during the production process.
[A43D 23/04] Last-carriers -- A43D 23/04 covers single components of pulling-over or lasting machines used in footwear production, specifically the last-carriers that hold the shoe lasts during the shaping and attachment processes. This subclass falls under the hierarchy of Human Necessities → Footwear → Machines, tools, equipment or methods for manufacturing or repairing footwear.
[A43D 23/06] Devices for cutting-off superfluous material on the uppers or linings -- A43D 23/06 covers devices that trim or cut away excess material from shoe uppers or linings during manufacturing or repair, typically mounted on pulling-over or lasting machines. These tools improve fit and finish by removing surplus leather, fabric or synthetic sections.
[A43D 25/00] Making or fastening soles, heels or welts, or preparing same for fastening to the shoe -- This classification covers processes, tools and equipment used to attach or prepare shoe components such as soles, heels and welts for fastening to footwear, including methods of bonding, stitching, riveting, gluing and related techniques.
[A43D 25/047] Devices for lasting with adhesives or for gluing together insoles and uppers -- A43D 25/047 covers equipment and methods for lasting shoes that employ adhesives, including devices that glue insoles to uppers or bond soles, heels and welts to the shoe. These tools are used in footwear manufacturing or repair where adhesive bonding replaces mechanical fastening.
[A43D 25/053] with rotating gripping means -- A43D 25/053 refers to machines or methods for making or fastening shoe soles, heels or welts that employ rotating gripping devices to hold and position the components during assembly. This subclass falls under footwear manufacturing equipment within the CPC hierarchy.
[A43D 25/06] Devices for gluing soles on shoe bottoms -- A43D 25/06 in the Cooperative Patent Classification covers devices specifically designed for the process of attaching soles to shoe bottoms through gluing, ensuring a secure and durable bond. This classification is part of a broader category that includes various methods and tools for manufacturing or repairing footwear.
[A43D 25/063] Multiple pressing devices in a closed circuit, e.g. rotating multiple pressing devices -- A43D 25/063 covers shoe-making equipment that uses several pressing units linked in a closed loop, often rotating, to apply pressure to soles, heels or welts during manufacturing or fastening. The arrangement enables continuous processing of footwear components in a coordinated sequence.
[A43D 25/066] Heel clamps or heel presses for glueing heels to shoe bottoms -- A43D 25/066 designates devices such as heel clamps or heel presses that hold a heel in place while adhesive bonds it to the shoe sole. These tools are used in the manufacturing or repair of footwear to ensure proper alignment and secure attachment of heels.
[A43D 25/07] using flexible diaphragm pressing devices -- A43D 25/07 covers the use of flexible-diaphragm pressing devices to fasten or prepare soles, heels or welts for attachment to shoes. This subcategory falls under Human Necessities → Footwear → Machines, tools, equipment or methods for manufacturing or repairing footwear, specifically for making or fastening shoe components.
[A43D 25/08] Welt hold-down devices -- A43D 25/08 relates to devices that hold down or position the welt during shoe assembly, ensuring the welt remains securely in place while it is attached to the upper and sole. These mechanisms are used in manufacturing or repairing footwear to facilitate proper fastening of the welt.
[A43D 25/10] Press-pads or other supports of shoe-gluing presses -- A43D 25/10 covers press-pads and similar support
[A43D 25/12] Devices for gluing heel-breasts to heels or for gluing coverings on heels -- The subclass A43D 25/12 covers equipment used in shoe production to apply adhesive for attaching heel-breasts to the heel or for bonding protective or decorative coverings onto heels. It includes any device that fastens soles, heels or welts by gluing rather than mechanical fastening.
[A43D 25/123] Devices for glueing heel-breasts to heels -- This classification covers apparatus designed to apply adhesive and secure heel-breasts to the heel portion of a shoe during production. It is part of the footwear manufacturing sub-group that deals with making or fastening soles, heels, or welts. The code A43D 25/123 is used in CPC indexing for such glue
[A43D 25/126] Devices for glueing coverings on heels -- A43D 25/126 covers devices used to glue coverings onto heels during the manufacture or repair of footwear, including equipment that applies adhesive to soles, heels or welts to secure them to the shoe. [A43D 25/14] Devices for filling the shoe bottom -- A43D 25/14 covers devices used to fill or reinforce the bottom part of a shoe, such as toe fillers, heel pads, insoles or other inserts that add material to the sole, heel or welt to improve fit, support or shape. These tools are employed during the manufacturing or repair of footwear.
[A43D 25/16] Devices for making glued platform shoes -- A43D 25/16 relates to equipment and methods for producing glued platform shoes, covering devices that form, attach or prepare soles, heels or welts for adhesion to the shoe upper. [A43D 25/18] Devices for applying adhesives to shoe parts -- A43D 25/18 covers machines and tools that apply adhesive to shoe components such as soles, heels or welts during manufacturing or repair. The classification includes any device that dispenses, spreads or directs glue onto shoe parts to enable fastening.
[A43D 25/181] by rollers -- A43D 25/181 relates to processes for making or fastening shoe soles, heels or welts with the use of rollers. It is classified under the CPC subgroup for machines, tools, equipment or methods used in manufacturing or repairing footwear.
[A43D 25/183] by nozzles -- A43D 25/183 covers methods of making or fastening shoe soles, heels or welts that employ nozzle devices, such as spraying adhesive, injecting molten material or otherwise delivering bonding substances to attach or shape the components.
[A43D 25/185] by imprinter plates -- In CPC A43D 25/185, the process of making or fastening soles, heels or welts to footwear is carried out using imprinter plates that transfer material or pattern onto the shoe component, facilitating attachment or decorative imprinting during manufacturing or repair.
[A43D 25/186] by dipping -- A43D 25/186 refers to a shoe-making or repair process in which soles, heels or welts are attached by dipping them in an adhesive, cement or polymer coating and then bonding them to the shoe. The method emphasizes coating the component before fastening, rather than applying a separate layer of material.
[A43D 25/188] by grids -- A43D 25/188 covers processes for making or attaching shoe components that employ grid-like structures, such as a network of intersecting strips or bars, to form or secure the sole, heel or welt. It includes the preparation of the grid, its placement on the shoe and any fastening steps that rely on the grid pattern.
[A43D 25/20] Arrangements for activating or for accelerating setting of adhesives, e.g. by using heat -- A43D 25/20 pertains to methods and apparatus for activating or speeding up the setting process of adhesives in footwear manufacturing, often through the application of heat. This classification is part of a detailed system covering tools and techniques for making or attaching soles, heels, or welts to shoes.
[A43D 27/00] Machines for trimming as an intermediate operation -- A43D 27/00 refers to machines employed in the intermediate stage of footwear production for trimming operations, such as removing excess material, shaping parts, or cleaning surfaces before final assembly. These devices are classified under human necessities → footwear → machines, tools, equipment or methods for manufacturing or repairing footwear.
[A43D 27/02] Soles or heels fixed on shoe bottoms -- A43D 27/02 relates to machines that permanently attach soles or heels to shoe bottoms during footwear production, covering equipment that fixes the lower part of a shoe to the upper structure as an intermediate manufacturing step. This subclass falls under the hierarchy of human necessities, footwear, and machinery for making or repairing shoes.
[A43D 27/04] Heel-end portions of soles fixed on shoe bottoms -- A43D 27/04 covers machines, tools and equipment used in an intermediate step of footwear production to trim the heel-end portions of soles that are already fixed to shoe bottoms. It falls under the class for manufacturing or repairing footwear.
[A43D 27/06] Heel breast flaps -- A43D 27/06 covers machines used in the intermediate trimming of footwear, specifically those that shape or cut the heel-breast flaps of shoes during production. The class falls under equipment for manufacturing or repairing footwear.
[A43D 29/00] Machines for making soles from strips of material -- A43D 29/00 refers to machines used in footwear production that form soles by joining strips of material, typically through stitching, bonding, lamination or other joining techniques. These devices are designed to cut, align and secure the strips into a continuous sole, facilitating efficient manufacturing of shoes.
[A43D 3/00] Lasts -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Lasts and shoe trees.  **Glossary:** - Last: a foot shape form used to build a shoe - Shoe tree: a foot shape form used to put inside a shoe in order to maintain over time its original shape, they are sometimes nevertheless still denominated lasts
[A43D 3/02] Lasts for making or repairing shoes -- A43D 3/02 covers lasts, the foot-shaped forms used in the production or repair of shoes. The classification includes designs and devices that shape, support or hold the shoe upper while it is constructed, stitched or cobbled, encompassing both new-shoe molding and refurbishing applications.
[A43D 3/021] for orthopaedic footwear -- A43D 3/021 covers lasts specially shaped for manufacturing or adjusting orthopedic footwear, providing the form needed to accommodate medical foot conditions. These tools are used in the production or repair of therapeutic shoes.
[A43D 3/022] comprising means, e.g. hooks, for holding, fixing or centering shoe parts on the last -- A43D 3/022 covers shoe lasts that incorporate elements such as hooks for holding, fixing or centering shoe components on the last during manufacturing or repair. The subclass falls under footwear-related machinery and tools used to shape and assemble shoes.
[A43D 3/023] Hinge constructions for articulated lasts -- A43D 3/023 covers hinge constructions used in articulated shoe lasts, enabling parts of the last to move relative to each other for better shaping and fitting of footwear during manufacturing or repair. The classification includes designs, mechanisms and adjustments that allow the last to open, close or flex.
[A43D 3/024] Lasts with a fore part and heel section relatively slideable to one another along an inclined line of cut -- This CPC subclass A43D 3/024 covers lasts where the fore part and heel section are joined so they can slide relative to each other along an inclined line of cut, facilitating adjustable shaping of footwear during manufacture or repair
[A43D 3/025] Longitudinally expansible lasts -- Longitudinally expansible lasts are shoe-forming tools whose length can be adjusted along the longitudinal axis, allowing a single last to accommodate multiple shoe sizes or provide a customizable fit during manufacturing or repair. This feature enables efficient production of varied footwear dimensions without needing separate fixed-size lasts.
[A43D 3/026] Lasts for making rubber footwear or for vulcanizing rubber soles to footwear -- A43D 3/026 covers lasts specifically designed for producing rubber footwear or for vulcanizing rubber soles onto shoes. These are shaped molds that hold the shoe or sole in place while the rubber material is formed, bonded or cured, ensuring the final product conforms to the intended foot shape and dimensions.
[A43D 3/027] Lasts with exchangeable parts, e.g. for changing the form or for remodelling -- A43D 3/027 pertains to footwear lasts designed with interchangeable components that can be removed and replaced to modify the shape or remodel the form during shoe manufacturing or repair. This allows a single base device to be adapted for different sizes, styles or corrective adjustments without needing entirely new lasts.
[A43D 3/028] Tack retaining means for lasts -- A tack retaining means for lasts is a device mounted on a shoe last that holds the small metal tacks used to secure material during shaping, preventing their movement while the shoe is built. It is a specialized tool employed in the manufacturing and repair of footwear.
[A43D 3/029] Constructional features of the heel section -- The CPC subclass A43D 3/029 concerns constructional features of the heel section of footwear lasts, the shaping tools used in shoe production. It covers the geometry, removable components and mounting arrangements of the heel portion that influence how a shoe's heel is formed.
[A43D 3/04] Pneumatic lasts -- A43D 3/04 designates pneumatic lasts, which are foot-shaped forms equipped with air-filled chambers or pressure systems to adjust shape during shoe manufacturing. The device allows dynamic fitting of uppers and aids in material handling, improving accuracy and comfort in footwear production.
[A43D 3/06] Wedge locks for lasts -- Wedge locks for lasts are devices that secure a shoe last in place by using a pair of complementary wedge-shaped elements that engage when the last is positioned in the shaping or finishing machine, simplifying alignment and release during footwear production.
[A43D 3/08] Devices for stretching special parts of shoes -- A43D 3/08 covers apparatus designed to apply controlled tension to specific regions of footwear, such as toe boxes, heels or arches, in order to reshape, enlarge or restore the original form of the shoe. These devices are used by manufacturers, repairers or consumers to stretch targeted areas without damaging the overall structure of the shoe.
[A43D 3/10] Devices for removing lasts -- Devices for removing lasts are tools used in shoe manufacturing to release the foot-shaped form (last) from the shoe upper or sole after shaping. They may be manual or mechanically assisted and are designed to apply force without damaging the last or the finished product.
[A43D 31/00] Machines for making or inserting shank stiffeners -- A43D 31/00 covers machines used in shoe production to form or insert shank stiffeners, the structural components that reinforce the sole. These devices are specialized tools for attaching rigid or flexible inserts that improve foot support and durability in footwear.
[A43D 31/02] Machines for making shank stiffeners -- A43D 31/02 covers machines specifically designed to produce shank stiffeners that reinforce the heel area of shoes. These devices form, shape or attach the stiffening element during footwear manufacturing, improving durability and support. The classification falls under footwear equipment used in making or repairing shoes.
[A43D 31/04] Machines for inserting shank stiffeners -- A43D 31/04 covers machinery designed to insert stiffening elements such as shanks into footwear during production. These devices are used in shoe manufacturing to provide structural support to the sole area.
[A43D 3/12] Devices for inserting or reinserting lasts -- Devices for inserting or reinserting lasts are tools used in shoe production and repair to hold, guide, or replace the foot-shaping form (the last) within the manufacturing apparatus. They enable precise positioning, easing the mounting or removal of lasts during shaping, stitching or finishing operations.
[A43D 3/14] Stretching or spreading lasts -- The subclass A43D 3/14 covers devices, tools and methods for stretching or spreading shoe lasts-the forms used to shape footwear during production. It includes equipment that adjusts the dimensions or shape of lasts to accommodate different sizes, designs or manufacturing requirements.
[A43D 3/1408] Devices for heating or drying shoes -- A43D 3/1408 designates devices specifically intended to heat or dry shoes. It belongs to the CPC hierarchy Human necessities > Footwear > Machines, tools, equipment or methods for manufacturing or repairing footwear > Lasts, and covers any apparatus that warms or removes moisture from footwear.
[A43D 3/1416] Boot-trees -- Boot-trees are devices that hold or shape a boot while it is being formed, repaired or finished, typically providing a rigid support for the heel and upper sections during manufacturing processes. They are used in shoe-making to maintain the boot's contour and facilitate tasks such as stitching, lasting or polishing.
[A43D 3/1425] Devices for sole stretching -- A43D 3/1425 covers apparatus designed to stretch the sole of a shoe, typically used in shoe repair or manufacturing to restore shape, improve fit, or prepare the sole for further processing. The devices may be mechanical, hydraulic, or inflatable and are intended to apply controlled tension to the sole material without damaging the footwear.
[A43D 3/1433] Shoe-trees -- Shoe-trees are inserts placed inside a shoe to preserve its shape, prevent creasing and aid drying after use. They are part of the subclass covering machines, tools, equipment or methods for manufacturing or repairing footwear, specifically the category of lasts.
[A43D 3/1441] spreading the width of a shoe, i.e. laterally expandable -- A43D 3/1441 covers shoe lasts that can be adjusted laterally to spread the width of a shoe, allowing the footwear to be expanded during manufacturing or repair. The classification falls under machines, tools, equipment or methods for making or fixing footwear, specifically those dealing with adjustable lasts.
[A43D 3/145] adjustable -- A43D 3/145 covers adjustable lasts used in the manufacturing or repair of footwear. The subclass includes lasts whose dimensions or shape can be altered to accommodate different shoe sizes or styles, enabling versatile production.
[A43D 3/1458] by a screw -- This subclass refers to methods or devices for forming or adjusting shoe lasts in which a screw is used to
[A43D 3/1466] stretching the length of a shoe, i.e. longitudinally expandable -- A43D 3/1466 covers methods or devices that lengthen a shoe along its longitudinal axis, enabling the footwear to be stretched or expanded in length. It pertains to tools, equipment or processes applied to lasts during manufacturing or repair to achieve a longitudinally expandable shoe.
[A43D 3/1475] adjustable -- The subclass covers lasts that can be adjusted in shape, size or dimensions to suit different shoe designs or foot measurements during manufacturing. It includes mechanisms, devices or methods that allow the length, width, curvature or other parameters of a last to be altered for customized or variable-size footwear production.
[A43D 3/1483] by a screw -- A43D 3/1483 covers shoe lasts that are secured, adjusted or assembled by means of a screw, using the screw as the principal element for holding the last in place during manufacturing or repair of footwear. This method falls under machines, tools, equipment or methods for making or fixing footwear.
[A43D 3/1491] with means for sweaty feet, e.g. with disinfecting or deodorant means  cleaning of a footwear  A47L23/20 -- A43D 3/1491 relates to footwear lasts that incorporate features to address sweaty feet, such as built-in disinfecting, deodorising or cleaning mechanisms for the shoe. The classification covers designs where the last itself provides means to reduce odor or moisture during manufacturing, fitting, or use of the footwear.
[A43D 33/00] Machines for assembling lifts for heels -- A43D 33/00 covers machines that assemble lifts for heels in shoe production, including devices that position, bond or fasten heel inserts onto footwear. The subclass comprises automated or semi-automated equipment used on manufacturing lines for attaching heel lifts to shoes.
[A43D 33/02] Gauges for heel lifts -- A43D 33/02 covers gauges specifically designed for measuring and checking heel lifts during footwear production, ensuring that the height and alignment of the lift are consistent and meet required specifications. These instruments are used in machines that assemble or adjust lifts for heels, forming part of the equipment category for making and repairing shoes.
[A43D 33/04] Nailing machines for building the heels from lifts -- A43D 33/04 covers machines that fasten lift components to the heel of shoes using nails or similar staples. These devices are used in footwear production to build or reinforce heel height by attaching removable or permanent lifts. The classification falls under footwear manufacturing equipment.
[A43D 33/06] Machines for sorting heel lifts -- A43D 33/06 covers machines designed to sort heel lifts during footwear manufacturing. These devices automate the organization of different heel lift types before they are attached to shoes, improving efficiency in assembling lift components.
[A43D 35/00] Presses for shaping pre-existing loose soles, shoe bottoms or soles fixed to shoe bottoms -- A43D 35/00 pertains to machines that press and shape pre-existing loose soles, shoe bottoms, or soles already attached to shoe bottoms, enabling the formation of the final footwear shape during manufacturing. This subclass falls under footwear-related equipment for shaping and repairing shoe components.
[A43D 37/00] Machines for roughening soles or other shoe parts preparatory to gluing -- It covers equipment used to texture or abrade shoe soles or other components before adhesive bonding, such as sanding, grinding or scarifying machines. These machines are employed in footwear manufacturing to improve glue adhesion.
[A43D 37/005] characterised by constructional details of the rotative tools used therewith -- A43D 37/005 designates machines used in footwear manufacturing for roughening soles or other shoe components prior to gluing, and specifically covers the constructional details of the rotative tools employed in that roughening process.
[A43D 39/00] Machines for making foot-supporting pads or instep-raisers for flat feet -- The CPC subclass A43D 39/00 refers to machines that produce foot-supporting pads or instep-raisers intended for flat feet, used in the manufacturing of footwear to provide arch support and improve comfort.
[A43D 43/00] Machines for making stitch lips, or other preparatory treatment of soles or insoles before fixing same -- A43D 43/00 covers machines that form stitch lips and perform other preparatory operations on shoe soles or insoles before they are attached to the upper. These devices shape, stitch, or treat the sole edge to facilitate fastening in footwear manufacturing.
[A43D 43/02] for making stitch lips by cutting -- A43D 43/02 covers machines that cut a preparatory stitch lip on soles or insoles of footwear before they are fixed to the upper. The equipment is used in the manufacturing process to create the edge that receives stitching for attaching the shoe components.
[A43D 43/06] for applying reinforcing materials to insoles -- A43D 43/06 classifies machines that apply reinforcing materials to shoe insoles during production, such as devices that stitch, bond or otherwise attach strengthening layers before the insoles are fixed to the sole. It falls under the broader group for equipment used in preparing soles or insoles in footwear manufacturing.
[A43D 44/00] Machines for attaching welts or rands -- A43D 44/00 covers machines used in footwear production that fasten welts or rands-protective or decorative strips-onto shoes. The equipment may employ stitching, bonding, or other attachment techniques to secure these elements during the manufacturing process.
[A43D 47/00] Machines for trimming or butting welts fixed on uppers -- A43D 47/00 covers machines used to trim or butt welts that are attached to shoe uppers, typically in footwear manufacturing. These devices shape, cut, or press the welt to the upper material to prepare for sole attachment.
[A43D 49/00] Machines for pounding -- A43D 49/00 refers to machines that pound or shape footwear components during production, such as devices that hammer soles or uppers into form. It is part of the equipment category for manufacturing or repairing shoes within the broader footwear class.
[A43D 5/00] Hand appliances or hand tools for making or repairing shoes, other than those covered by groups -- A43D 5/00 covers hand-operated devices and tools used in making or repairing shoes that are not classified in other footwear-machinery groups. It includes general purpose hand appliances such as hammers, presses, shaping implements and similar tools employed by shoemakers.
[A43D 5/005] for heel removing -- The A43D 5/005 subclass refers to hand-operated tools specifically designed to remove heels from shoes during repair or refurbishment. It falls under the broader category of machines, tools, equipment or methods for manufacturing or repairing footwear, and excludes devices covered in other groups of the same hierarchy.
[A43D 5/02] Stands for making footwear -- A43D 5/02 classifies devices that hold or support shoes while they are being assembled, repaired or finished. These stands enable shoemakers to position footwear securely for stitching, lasting, finishing or other hand-operated operations, facilitating hands-free work and consistent shaping.
[A43D 5/04] Knives -- A43D 5/04 designates hand-held knives employed in the making or repairing of footwear, such as cutting leather, fabric or other shoe components. This subclass groups knives that are not covered by other more specific tool categories within the footwear manufacturing hierarchy. It is part of the CPC section on human necessities, footwear, and the machines, tools, and equipment used for shoe production.
[A43D 5/06] Tools for cutting off the ends of nails or pegs in shoes -- A43D 5/06 covers hand-operated devices used in shoe making or repair that cut off the protruding ends of nails or pegs. These tools trim fastening elements after they have been driven into the sole or upper, providing a smooth finished surface.
[A43D 5/08] Awls -- A43D 5/08 designates awls, hand-operated pointed tools used to pierce or mark leather and other shoe-making materials during the manufacture or repair of footwear. They fall under the category of hand appliances for shoe construction that are not covered by other specific groups.
[A43D 5/10] Shoe-peg rasps -- A43D 5/10 relates to hand tools for shaping or smoothing shoe-pegs, such as rasps used in footwear manufacturing or repair to finish the pegs that hold the shoe lasts.
[A43D 51/00] Machines for lip-setting -- A43D 51/00 covers machines that set or shape the lip of a shoe upper, i.e., the curved edge that forms the opening of the footwear. These devices are used in the production line to form a precise, reinforced margin before the upper is attached to the sole. The classification falls under equipment for manufacturing or repairing footwear.
[A43D 5/12] Hand nailing apparatus for shoemaking -- A43D 5/12 covers hand-operated devices that drive nails in the shoe-making process, such as portable nail guns or manual nailing tools used to attach soles, heels or other components. It applies to hand tools not classified in any other specific shoe-making groups.
[A43D 5/14] Hand pincers for stapling -- A43D 5/14 designates hand pincers used for stapling in the manufacture or repair of footwear. These hand tools secure staples in shoes and are separate from other shoe-making hand appliances.
[A43D 53/00] Machines for trimming-off surplus material along the inseam -- The code A43D 53/00 designates equipment used in footwear production that removes excess material along the inner seam of a shoe. It includes machines that trim or cut surplus leather, fabric or other components to clean the inseam before final finishing. This subclass falls under the CPC hierarchy for machines, tools, or equipment for manufacturing or repairing footwear.
[A43D 55/00] Machines for flattening, pressing, or rubbing the inseams of lasted shoes -- A43D 55/00 pertains to machines used in shoe manufacturing to flatten, press or rub the inner seams of lasted shoes, improving fit and surface finish before final finishing operations.
[A43D 57/00] Machines for attaching the welt ends -- A43D 57/00 relates to machines that attach the welt ends in the production or repair of footwear. These devices secure the welt to the shoe upper and sole, streamlining the assembly process.
[A43D 59/00] Machines for rasping the lasting-margins of shoes which are sewn through -- A43D 59/00 designates machines that rasp the lasting-margins of shoes whose uppers are sewn through, a specialized tool used in footwear manufacturing for smoothing and finishing the edges where the upper is attached to the sole. This subclass falls under the broader category of equipment for making or repairing shoes.
[A43D 61/00] Machines for nail-pulling, nail-cutting, or nail-detecting -- A43D 61/00 covers machines used in footwear production that pull, cut, or detect nails in shoe components, helping to prevent damage and improve safety during assembly and repair. The devices may be mechanical or automated systems integrated into shoe-making processes.
[A43D 63/00] Machines for carrying-out other finishing operations -- This classification covers machines used in the final stages of shoe production that perform finishing tasks other than primary shaping, such as buffing, polishing, embossing, decorating or applying surface treatments. It includes equipment designed to
[A43D 67/00] Machines for fastening soles or heels by means of screws or screwed wire -- A43D 67/00 covers machinery designed to attach shoe soles or heels using screws or screwed wire, including devices that drive, position, or secure fasteners during shoe manufacturing or repair. These machines automate fastening, improving durability and production efficiency.
[A43D 69/00] Shoe-nailing machines -- A43D 69/00 refers to machines that fasten shoes by nailing, including equipment that drives nails or similar fasteners into uppers, soles or other footwear components during manufacture or repair. It falls under the CPC group for footwear manufacturing tools within the broader categories of human necessities and footwear.
[A43D 69/005] using nails being made from alternative materials, e.g. leather -- A43D 69/005 covers shoe-nailing machines that use nails made from alternative, non-metallic materials such as leather rather than traditional metal. The subclass addresses methods and equipment for attaching footwear components with these unconventional nails.
[A43D 69/02] using ready-made nails -- A43D 69/02 pertains to shoe-nailing machines that use ready-made nails as the fastening element. The class includes equipment for attaching soles or uppers to footwear where the nails are supplied pre-formed rather than produced by the machine itself.
[A43D 69/04] with apparatus for separating the nails from a wire or from a strip of metal or other material -- A43D 69/04 covers shoe-nailing machines that incorporate a mechanism to detach the individual nails from a supplying wire, strip of metal or other material, allowing the nails to be fed separately into the nailing head for footwear manufacturing.
[A43D 69/06] using ready-made staples -- A43D 69/06 covers shoe-nailing machines that use ready-made staples instead of forming nails in situ. It includes the feeding, positioning and driving mechanisms for applying pre-manufactured staples to footwear components during assembly or repair, and is classified under equipment for manufacturing or repairing footwear.
[A43D 69/08] with apparatus for making staples -- A43D 69/08 covers shoe-nailing machines that include an apparatus for forming staples, used in the production or repair of footwear. It is situated in the hierarchy Human Necessities → Footwear → Machines, tools, equipment or methods for manufacturing or repairing footwear.
[A43D 69/10] using ready-made wooden pegs -- The A43D 69/10 subclass refers to shoe-nailing machines that join soles using ready-made wooden pegs, specifying the use of pre-formed wooden pins as the fastening element in footwear manufacturing.
[A43D 69/12] with apparatus for separating the pegs from a strip of wood -- A43D 69/12 covers shoe-nailing machines that include a mechanism for separating the wooden pegs (nails) from a strip of wood before they are driven into footwear, facilitating continuous or automated nail feeding during shoe production.
[A43D 69/14] for nailing or stapling the lasted margin onto shoes with wooden soles -- A43D 69/14 covers machines that nail or staple the lasted edge of a shoe to a wooden sole, a process used in shoe-making to secure the upper to the sole during assembly. These devices are a specific type of footwear-manufacturing equipment within the broader category of machines, tools, equipment or methods for manufacturing or repairing footwear.
[A43D 71/00] Elements of nailing machines -- A43D 71/00 designates the individual components of nailing machines used in footwear production, including hammers, nail-driving devices, feed and positioning mechanisms, and related control elements that enable the attachment of soles or other parts to shoes.
[A43D 71/02] Driving mechanisms for moving the horn -- A43D 71/02 relates to the driving mechanisms that move the horn in machines for manufacturing or repairing footwear, such as nailing or stitching equipment. The claim covers the system-pneumatic, hydraulic, electric or mechanical-that transmits power to shift the horn and position the shoe component during processing.
[A43D 75/00] Nailing devices on pulling-over or lasting machines -- The subclass A43D 75/00 covers nailing devices fitted to pulling-over or lasting machines used in shoe production. It includes mechanisms that feed, position and drive nails to fasten uppers to lasts during the lasting process.
[A43D 79/00] Combined heel-pressing and nailing machines -- A43D 79/00 covers machines that simultaneously press and nail shoe heels, integrating heel-forming and fastening operations in a single unit for footwear production. These combined heel-pressing and nailing devices streamline manufacturing and improve efficiency.
[A43D 8/00] Machines for cutting, ornamenting, marking or otherwise working up shoe part blanks -- A43D 8/00 covers machines used in footwear production that cut, shape, ornament, mark or otherwise process shoe component blanks. These devices are designed to form, decorate or prepare raw shoe parts before assembly, fitting within the broader category of equipment for manufacturing or repairing footwear.
[A43D 8/003] specially adapted for forming upper blanks or leggings by stretching -- The class covers equipment designed to shape shoe uppers or similar legging blanks by applying stretch forces, enabling precise contouring before further processing. Such machines typically grip the blank and elongate it to achieve the desired form.
[A43D 8/006] for forming shoe stiffeners -- A43D 8/006 refers to machines for forming shoe stiffeners. It falls under footwear manufacturing equipment. This code is used for classification purposes.
[A43D 8/02] Cutting-out -- A43D 8/02 covers machines used to cut shoe-part blanks into the shapes required for footwear assembly. It includes equipment that severs, slices or otherwise removes material-by knives, lasers, water jets or similar means-to produce uppers, soles and other components, often with automated feeding or positioning systems.
[A43D 8/04] Stamping-out -- A43D 8/04 covers machines that shape shoe components by stamping, cutting, marking or otherwise working up blanks, including equipment that ornaments or treats footwear parts during production. It resides in the hierarchy of human necessities - footwear - machines, tools, equipment or methods for manufacturing or repairing footwear.
[A43D 8/06] Dies therefor -- A43D 8/06 pertains to dies employed in machines that cut, ornament, mark or otherwise process shoe part blanks. The classification covers tooling that shapes, embosses or imprints components such as soles, uppers or decorative elements during footwear manufacture or repair.

[A43D 8/10] Cutting-out using pattern grading -- A43D 8/10 covers machines that cut shoe component blanks using pattern grading, allowing a single graded template to produce multiple sized parts. The equipment automates layout, cutting and marking of the patterns to maximise material efficiency in footwear manufacturing.
[A43D 81/00] Machines for attaching top-lifts -- A43D 81/00 refers to machines that attach top-lifts to footwear during manufacturing or repair. Top-lifts are decorative or functional elements applied to the shoe upper, and the classified equipment automates their placement and securing.
[A43D 8/12] Patterns or templates therefor -- A43D 8/12 covers patterns or templates employed with machines that cut, ornament or otherwise work shoe part blanks. It includes devices for holding, positioning or shaping patterns that guide the accurate formation of footwear components.

[A43D 8/16] Ornamentation -- A43D 8/16 covers machines used to add decorative or identifying features to shoe parts, such as cutting, embossing, engraving, stamping, laser etching or other ornamentation methods applied to blanks during footwear manufacturing. It falls under equipment for working up shoe components.
[A43D 8/18] by punching or perforating -- A43D 8/18 covers machines used in footwear production that form holes, patterns or decorative openings in shoe part blanks by punching or perforating. These devices apply pressure with punches or similar tools to create perforations, enabling functional or aesthetic modifications during the cutting, ornamenting or marking stages of shoe manufacturing.
[A43D 8/20] Dies therefor -- A43D 8/20 covers dies employed in shoe-manufacturing equipment for cutting, decorating, marking or otherwise shaping shoe part blanks. The dies are specialized tools that transfer patterns or dimensions onto leather, synthetic or other shoe materials during the finishing or assembly stages. This subclass falls under machines and tools for producing footwear within the Human Necessities section.
[A43D 8/22] by embossing or printing -- A43D 8/22 classifies machines used in footwear production that apply decorative patterns to shoe part blanks through embossing or printing, such as rollers, plates or printing heads that imprint designs on leather, synthetic or fabric components during the cutting or finishing stages.
[A43D 8/24] Embossing using heat, e.g. high frequency electric current -- This subclass covers machines that emboss shoe part blanks by applying heat, commonly using high-frequency electric current, to create decorative or functional surface patterns.
[A43D 8/26] Marking for future work -- A43D 8/26 covers the marking of shoe-part blanks to indicate later processing steps. It applies to machines that apply symbols, codes, lines or other visual guides on footwear components before cutting, ornamenting, or assembling. The classification helps locate technologies that prepare blanks for subsequent manufacturing operations.
[A43D 8/265] Marking buttonhole locations -- A43D 8/265 covers machines and tools that locate and mark the positions for buttonholes on shoe uppers during manufacturing, ensuring precise placement before stitching or cutting. The equipment typically uses guides, templates or electronic systems to indicate where buttonholes should be formed on the footwear component.
[A43D 8/28] Patterns for drawing cut-outs -- A43D 8/28 covers pattern documents used to lay out cut-outs for shoe components, guiding machines that trim, decorate or otherwise process shoe blanks. The patterns define the shapes and dimensions of the pieces to be cut from leather, textile or other materials before assembly. This classification falls under tools and equipment for manufacturing footwear.
[A43D 8/30] Charting sheet material for subsequent cutting -- A43D 8/30 covers apparatus that creates layout or pattern sheets on material intended for later cutting of shoe parts, such as printers, plotters or marking devices that generate the cutting plan for blanks before they are processed.
[A43D 83/00] Heel-presses without nailing apparatus -- A heel-press is a machine for shaping or attaching a shoe heel by applying pressure instead of driving nails. It typically clamps the heel block and the shoe upper together, using hydraulic or mechanical force to secure the heel during footwear assembly.
[A43D 8/32] Working on edges or margins -- This subclass covers machines that work on the edges or margins of shoe components, such as cutting, trimming, ornamenting, marking or otherwise processing shoe part blanks.
[A43D 8/34] by skiving -- A43D 8/34 covers machines that process shoe part blanks by skiving, i.e., thinning or shaping material through cutting, ornamenting or marking operations. It falls under footwear manufacturing equipment within the broader human necessities category.
[A43D 8/36] by trimming the margins of sole blanks -- A subclass for equipment that removes excess material from the edges of sole blanks, shaping and finishing the sole by trimming its margins. These machines are used in shoe production to prepare and refine sole components before assembly.
[A43D 8/38] by cutting-out, e.g. using templates -- A43D 8/38 relates to machines that shape shoe components by cutting them out from blanks, typically using templates or patterned guides. The devices perform the cutting operation to define the outline of the part before ornamenting, marking or further processing. This subclass falls within equipment for manufacturing or repairing footwear.
[A43D 8/40] by folding, turning in or over, hammering -- A43D 8/40 covers machines that shape shoe components by folding, turning in or over, and hammering the material. These devices are used in the manufacturing or repair of footwear to form and ornament blanks.
[A43D 8/42] by singeing, contracting, dyeing -- A43D 8/42 refers to machines that finish shoe part blanks by singeing, contracting or dyeing, including equipment that cuts, decorates, marks or otherwise processes footwear components. It falls under the CPC hierarchy for human necessities → footwear → machinery for manufacturing or repairing shoes. This subclass specifically covers thermal or chemical treatment steps applied to shoe materials.
[A43D 8/44] Creasing presses -- A43D 8/44 covers creasing presses, machines that form permanent folds or bends in shoe components such as uppers, midsoles or soles during manufacturing. By applying pressure, often with heat, they create precise creases that improve the fit, flexibility and appearance of footwear.
[A43D 8/46] Splitting -- A43D 8/46 pertains to machines that split footwear components, such as uppers, soles or other shoe part blanks, into separate pieces during the manufacturing process. The equipment is used to divide material before subsequent cutting, ornamenting, marking or assembly steps.
[A43D 8/48] combined with skiving -- A43D 8/48 classifies machines that cut, ornament, mark or otherwise work shoe part blanks and that incorporate a skiving operation, i.e., removing material to thin or shape edges.
[A43D 8/50] Combined splitting and trimming of the heel-seat portions of sole blanks -- The CPC entry A43D 8/50 designates machines that perform a combined splitting and trimming operation on the heel-seat areas of sole blanks during shoe production. These devices integrate cutting and finishing steps to shape the heel portion of footwear soles in a single, continuous process.
[A43D 85/00] Machines or apparatus for turning, e.g. for making turn-shoes -- A43D 85/00 covers machines and apparatus used to turn footwear during production, such as equipment for making turn-shoes where the upper is reversed over the sole. The subclass includes turning devices, rotating fixtures and related tools that shape or assemble shoes requiring a turning step.
[A43D 8/52] Flexing -- A43D 8/52 Flexing covers machines that bend, flex or otherwise deform shoe components during manufacturing, such as lasts, uppers or soles, to shape them or improve fit. It falls under the subclass for equipment that works up shoe part blanks, including cutting, ornamenting and marking.
[A43D 8/54] by milling -- This subclass covers machines that shape or finish shoe components by milling, removing material from blanks to achieve the required form or decorative patterns.
[A43D 8/56] of sole blanks by slitting -- A43D 8/56 pertains to machines used to slit sole blanks during shoe manufacturing. It falls under equipment for cutting, ornamenting, marking or otherwise shaping shoe part blanks, specifically addressing the separation of sole material by slitting.
[A43D 86/00] Machines for assembling soles or heels onto uppers, not provided for in groups -- A43D 86/00 covers footwear-manufacturing machines that attach soles or heels to shoe uppers when the specific method is not covered by any other subclass. These devices are used in assembling the final shoe structure, handling components that differ from standard bonding, stitching or cementing processes. The subclass falls under human necessities → footwear → equipment for making or repairing footwear.
[A43D 87/00] Performing finishing operations upon uppers, soles, or heels on soled shoes -- The subclass A43D 87/00 covers processes used to finish the uppers, soles or heels of shoes that already have a sole attached. It includes operations such as polishing, coating, embossing, trimming or applying decorative treatments that improve appearance, texture or durability after the shoe has been assembled. This falls under the broader categories of human necessities, footwear, and equipment for manufacturing or repairing footwear.
[A43D 89/00] Sole-levelling machines with rolls -- A43D 89/00 covers machines that level the sole of footwear using rolls, providing a uniform thickness and shape during manufacture or repair. These sole-levelling machines employ rotating rollers to flatten and smooth the sole surface.
[A43D 9/00] Pulling-over or lasting -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - For the toe ends -> A43D15/00
[A43D 91/00] Stitch-separating or seam-indenting machines -- A43D 91/00 covers machines that separate stitches or create indentations in seams during the manufacture or repair of footwear. These devices are used to prepare shoe uppers for stitching, trimming, or finishing operations by precisely cutting or pressing the material along predefined lines. The class falls under the footwear manufacturing equipment hierarchy of the CPC system.
[A43D 93/00] Edge-indenting machines -- A43D 93/00 covers machines that form indentations along the edge of footwear, typically to create decorative or functional edge profiles such as stitching, embossing or reinforcement. These edge-indenting machines are used in the manufacturing or repair of shoes and other footgear.
[A43D 95/00] Shoe-finishing machines -- This subclass groups machines that carry out final surface treatments on shoes, such as polishing, buffing, coating or other finishing operations applied after the main construction of footwear, to improve appearance and durability.
[A43D 95/02] Machines for treating or smoothing shoe uppers to remove wrinkles, folds, or the like -- A43D 95/02 covers machines that treat or smooth shoe uppers to eliminate wrinkles, folds or similar surface irregularities. These devices are used in footwear production to improve the appearance and fit of the upper before final assembly.
[A43D 95/04] Machines for laying channel-flaps -- A43D 95/04 pertains to shoe-finishing equipment that automatically lays channel-flaps onto footwear, securing decorative or functional strips along the shoe's side channel. The machines are used in the final stages of shoe manufacturing to attach these flaps efficiently.
[A43D 95/06] Machines for colouring or chemical treatment -- A43D 95/06 in the Cooperative Patent Classification refers to machinery used specifically for coloring or chemically treating footwear during the finishing process. This classification is part of a broader category that includes various machines, tools, and methods for manufacturing or repairing footwear.
[A43D 95/08] Machines or tools for scouring, abrading, or finishing, with or without dust-separating -- A43D 95/08 covers machines and tools used in shoe production to scour, abrade or finish footwear surfaces, including equipment that smooths, polishes or textures leather or synthetic uppers. The devices may incorporate dust-separating systems to collect debris during the finishing process.
[A43D 95/10] Drying or heating devices for shoes -- A43D 95/10 covers apparatus and methods that dry or heat footwear, including shoe dryers, heated racks and warm-air blowers designed to remove moisture and improve comfort. It is part of the CPC group for machines, tools, equipment or methods for manufacturing or repairing shoes.
[A43D 95/12] Devices for conditioning, tempering, or moistening -- A43D 95/12 covers machines and apparatus that condition, temper or moisten footwear during manufacturing or finishing, such as steam cabinets, humidifiers or temperature-controlled chambers used to improve the shape, flexibility or comfort of shoes.
[A43D 95/125] with means, e.g. transport chains, for continuously transferring shoes through the devices -- A43D 95/125 covers shoe-finishing machines that incorporate mechanisms such as transport chains to move shoes continuously through the equipment. It falls under the hierarchy of human necessities → footwear → machines, tools, equipment or methods for manufacturing or repairing footwear.
[A43D 95/14] incorporating marking, printing, or embossing apparatus -- A43D 95/14 covers shoe-finishing machines that incorporate devices for applying markings, prints or embossing to footwear. The subclass includes equipment that combines polishing, buffing or other finishing operations with mechanisms for adding logos, decorative patterns, identification symbols or other surface designs directly onto shoes.
[A43D 95/16] Burnishing tools for shoemaking -- A43D 95/16 pertains to burnishing tools used in shoemaking, specifically devices that polish and smooth the surface of shoes during the finishing stage. These tools are part of shoe-finishing machines that improve the appearance and durability of footwear.
[A43D 95/18] Devices for heating the burnishing tools for shoemaking -- A43D 95/18 refers to devices that heat
[A43D 95/20] Machines for burnishing soles or heels -- A43D 95/20 covers machines that burnish the soles or heels of shoes, smoothing and polishing these surfaces to enhance appearance and durability. These devices are used in shoe-finishing operations within footwear manufacturing.
[A43D 95/22] Machines for burnishing the edges of soles, with or without devices for edge-indenting -- A43D 95/22 covers machinery used to polish or burnish the edges of shoe soles, optionally incorporating devices that create indentations along the edge for decorative or functional effect. The classification falls under Human Necessities → Footwear → Machines, tools, equipment or methods for manufacturing or repairing footwear → Shoe-finishing machines.
[A43D 95/24] Machines for buffing soles -- The CPC class A43D 95/24 covers machines used to buff the soles of footwear, smoothing or polishing them as part of the shoe-finishing process. These devices are employed in shoe-finishing operations to improve the appearance and durability of the sole surface.
[A43D 95/26] Devices for applying wax -- A43D 95/26 covers equipment used to apply wax to footwear, typically in shoe-finishing processes. The devices may include rollers, sprayers or brushes that uniformly spread wax on shoe surfaces to enhance shine, water-resistance or durability, and are classified under machines, tools, equipment or methods for manufacturing or repairing footwear.
[A43D 95/28] Machines for attaching protectors to soles or heels -- Machines for attaching protectors to soles or heels are equipment used in shoe-finishing processes to bond or fix protective caps, plates or other reinforcement elements onto footwear. They automate the application of these sol​e or heel protectors, improving durability and safety of the finished shoe.
[A43D 97/00] Machines for making pulling-on pieces -- This subclass covers machines and equipment specifically designed to manufacture pull-on footwear components such as slip-on shoes, moccasins and similar items that are put on without lacing or fastening. It includes devices that cut, shape, assemble or finish these pieces, focusing on processes that produce ready-to-wear, non-fastened shoe parts.
[A43D 98/00] Machines for making laces -- A43D 98/00 classifies machines designed specifically for producing shoelaces, including devices that form, cut, and finish laces used in footwear. It falls under the broader categories of human necessities, footwear, and equipment for manufacturing or repairing shoes.
[A43D 999/00] Subject matter not provided for in other groups of this subclass -- A43D 999/00 covers topics related to footwear manufacturing or repair that do not fit into more specific categories within the A43D subclass. This includes unique processes, tools, and equipment not addressed elsewhere in the classification.
[A44] HABERDASHERY -- A44 haberdashery covers clothing accessories and supplementary apparel such as hats, ties, scarves, gloves, belts and related fastening or ornamental items, typically made of textile, leather or synthetic materials. The subclass also includes protective and decorative components that complement primary garments.
[A44B] BUTTONS, PINS, BUCKLES, SLIDE FASTENERS, OR THE LIKE -- **Definition:** This place covers:  The following fasteners or their essential parts:  Making slide fasteners, touch-and-close fasteners (e.g. hook-and-loop or barb-and-pile fabrics) and other fasteners of this subclass.  Accessories or ancillary devices for the fasteners of this subclass when they are used exclusively with one of the varieties of fasteners (e.g. button cards) or have their operations either influencing or being influenced by the operation of the fastener (e.g. slide fastener pulling cords).  The following additional devices associated with either the fasteners of this class or accoutrements, garments, and haberdashery:  Note  The only "other types" of fasteners appropriate for this subclass are those that are capable of use with or actually used with accoutrements, garments, or haberdashery. Therefore, it must be either a commonly known practice to use a particular type of fastener with accoutrements, garments, or haberdashery (e.g., snap fasteners) or the patent documents for a particular type of fastener must frequently disclose their use with accoutrements, garments, or haberdashery. However, it is not required that all, or even the majority, of the fasteners covered by a particular category of fastener be specially adapted for use, or disclose their intended use, with accoutrements, garments, or haberdashery.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Fastening devices specially adapted for specific types of garments -> A41F - Buttonholes or eyelets for buttonholes -> A41F1/02 - Corset fasteners -> A41F1/04 - Glove fasteners -> A41F1/06 - Garter fasteners -> A41F1/08 - Belts, girdles or waistbands for trousers or skirts -> A41F9/00 - Thumbtacks -> B43M15/00 - Devices for fastening or securing constructional elements or machine parts together -> F16B - Hand-manipulated hooks or eyes (e.g. swivel hook for dog leash) -> F16B45/00  **Glossary:** - Button: A hand manipulated fastener that includes a rigid or semirigid portion (i.e. button) which is attached (e.g. sewn) to the structure it is intended to fasten or secure (e.g. clothing) in a manner that allows its reorientation by either movement about its attachment or movement of the structure it secures. A button is intended to be used with a complementary opening having a flaccid portion (e.g. buttonholes, looped cords) that forms the second essential part of the fastener. - Buckle: A hand manipulated fastener including engaging or holding means and structure for guiding during fastening, which can selectively hold a cooperating strap or belt that is either the structure secured (e.g. end of a dress belt) or a second portion of the fastener (e.g. securing strap of a backpack flap). - Pin: A hand manipulated fastener that includes both (1) a portion specifically shaped (e.g. pointed) to facilitate impaling of and penetration into the structure fastened or secured and (2) a remaining portion (e.g. head) intended to be manually pushed on that does not penetrate the structure fastened or secured. - Slide fastener: A hand manipulated fastener that includes (1) two, opposed, elongated, configured surfaces that are each attached by mounting means to a secured structure and are intended to directly contact and interlock with each other when fastened and (2) a sliding device that is much shorter in length than the surfaces and which travels along their length to forcibly shift them into or out of interlocking engagement

Illustrative example of subject matter classified in this group:  1.  **Glossary:** - Button: A knob or stud of metal or other material sewn or fixed by a shank or neck to articles of dress, usually for the purpose of fastening one part of the dress to another by passing through a button-hole, but often merely for ornament. - Badge: Any distinctive device, emblem, or mark worn to indicate a person's affiliation, rank, office, name, etc., or membership of an organization or support for a cause; also a small piece of metal, card, plastic, etc., bearing a distinguishing design or words and often worn purely for ornament.

[A44B 13/0029] characterised by their way of fastening to the support  A44B13/0052  and  A44B13/0058  take precedence -- A44B 13/0029 covers hook or eye fasteners whose defining feature is the manner in which they attach to the supporting material. The subclass groups all such fasteners unless a finer subclass, such as A44B 13/0052 or A44B 13/0058, specifies a particular fastening method and therefore takes precedence.

[A44B 13/0047] welded or moulded -- A44B 13/0047 refers to welded or moulded hook-or-eye fasteners used in buttons, pins, buckles and similar slide fasteners. The code classifies items where the fastening element is formed by welding or moulding processes rather than separate assembly. It falls under the broader groups of human necessities, haberdashery, and fastener types.
[A44B 13/0052] Strips of hook or eye fasteners -- Strips composed of multiple hook or eye fasteners mounted in a continuous line for use in clothing, accessories, or industrial fastening. The fasteners may be sewn, bonded or otherwise attached to the strip, which can be made from textiles, plastics or metal, and are intended to provide repeated, adjustable closure.
[A44B 13/0058] Eyelets or grommets -- A44B 13/0058 classifies eyelets or grommets, which are metal or plastic rings inserted into fabric or other materials to reinforce holes for laces, cords or hooks. These fasteners are used in clothing, accessories and other haberdashery items to prevent tearing and provide a durable attachment point.
[A44B 13/0064] characterised by their material -- A44B 13/0064 covers hook-or-eye fasteners that are distinguished by the material from which they are made, such as metal, plastic, wood or composite substances, within the broader category of buttons, pins, buckles and slide fasteners. This sub-class groups fasteners primarily on their compositional properties rather than shape or function.
[A44B 13/007] made of plastics -- A44B 13/007 is a CPC subclass that covers hook-or-eye fasteners made of plastics, used in buttons, pins, buckles and other slide-type fasteners. It falls under the Haberdashery group within the broader Human Necessities category.
[A44B 13/0076] characterised by their way of fastening to the support -- A44B 13/0076 covers hook or eye fasteners that are distinguished by the way they are secured to the supporting material, such as by stitching, rivetting, adhesive bonding or molding, and are used chiefly in garments and related accessories.

The class includes devices that lock, release or otherwise hold the interlocked components together.
[A44B 19/48] Arranging interlocking members before securing -- A44B 19/48 covers methods and devices for positioning the
[A44B 19/50] Securing one-piece interlocking members -- The subclass A44B 19/50 covers means for securing the two halves of a slide-fastener as a single, interlocking unit. It includes devices such as stops, catches or other features that lock the teeth together to prevent accidental opening of the zipper.
[A44B 19/52] Securing the interlocking members to stringer tapes while making the latter -- A44B 19/52 covers the method of fastening the teeth or interlocking elements of a slide fastener to the stringer tapes at the same time the tapes are being formed, ensuring that the fastening components are secured while the tape material is produced.
[A44B 19/54] while weaving the stringer tapes -- The entry A44B 19/54 covers the manufacturing step in which the stringer tapes of a slide fastener are woven together. This process creates the longitudinal strands that form the teeth or coils of the zipper, providing strength and precise alignment before the tapes are later interlocked for the final fastener.
[A44B 19/56] while knitting the stringer tapes -- A44B 19/56 covers the production of slide-fastener (zipper) stringer tapes by knitting the tapes directly, rather than by weaving or extruding separate elements. The entry includes methods, machinery and processes used to form the continuous knitted tapes that become the carrier strips for the interlocking elements of a zipper.
[A44B 19/58] Removing interlocking members to produce gaps -- A44B 19/58 relates to slide fasteners and covers methods or devices for disengaging the interlocking members of a zipper or similar fastening to create a gap. It includes mechanisms that separate the teeth, coils or hooks in order to open, remove or adjust the fastener.
[A44B 19/60] Applying end stops upon stringer tapes -- The entry covers methods for installing end stops on the stringer tapes of slide fasteners, such as zippers, to secure the tape ends and prevent the interlocking elements from separating. It includes processes for attaching, sealing or otherwise fixing the end-stop components to the tape during manufacture of fasteners used in garments and other products.
[A44B 19/62] Assembling sliders in position on stringer tapes -- A44B 19/62 addresses the process of attaching the sliders of a slide-fastener to the stringer tapes that form the zipper chain. It covers techniques for positioning and securing the individual slider components during assembly of the fastening device.
[A44B 19/64] Slider holders for assemblage of slide fasteners -- A44B 19/64 refers to devices that hold the slider component while a slide fastener (zipper) is being assembled. These holders facilitate the positioning and joining of the two zipper tapes before the slider is engaged, and are classified under haberdashery items such as buttons, pins and buckles.

[A44B 99/005] Turn-button fasteners -- Turn-button fasteners are small, usually metal or plastic closures that secure garments or accessories by rotating a button or similar element into a matching socket. They function like miniature screws, providing a neat, reusable fastening method often seen on coats, jackets, and decorative apparel. This subclass covers all such turn-button types not classified elsewhere.
[A44C] PERSONAL ADORNMENTS, e.g. JEWELLERY -- A44C covers personal adornments such as jewellery and other decorative items worn on the body. The class includes ornamental pieces made from metal, gems, plastics or natural materials that serve aesthetic or symbolic purposes rather than functional utility. It is situated under Human Necessities → Haberdashery.
