[A45B 2009/005] Shafts -- A45B 2009/005 classifies shafts that serve as cylindrical or conical members in furniture and related domestic articles, enabling rotation, height adjustment, or other mechanical movements. The subclass covers shafts used for supporting, transmitting motion, or linking components such as swivel bases, lift mechanisms, and reclining devices. It is a sub-category of A45B, which deals with parts of tables, chairs, desks and similar items.
[A45B 2009/007] of adjustable length, e.g. telescopic shafts -- The CPC code A45B 2009/007 classifies items whose length can be varied, specifically telescopic shafts used as extendable components in furniture, containers, handles, legs, poles or similar structures, where the shaft sections slide within one another to provide adjustable reach.
[A45B 2009/025] releasably connected to a wrist strap or a glove -- A45B 2009/025 refers to devices or accessories that can be detachably fastened to a wrist strap or a glove, enabling temporary attachment for tasks such as holding tools, supporting electronic equipment, or providing protective functions. The connection is designed to be easily released without damaging either the device or the strap or glove.
[A45B 2011/005] characterised by their shape -- A45B 2011/005 groups patent documents in which the defining technical feature of a liquid container-such as a bottle, jar or similar vessel-is its shape. The classification covers inventions where the form, contour or overall geometry of the container is the primary characteristic distinguishing it from prior art, including functional or aesthetic shape variations.
[A45B 2017/005] with additional rotation about a vertical axis -- A45B 2017/005 refers to furniture or furniture components that incorporate an extra movement allowing the item to rotate around a vertical axis, such as swivel chairs, rotating tables or cabinets with a turning mechanism. This feature provides enhanced adjustability or accessibility by enabling the entire piece or a part of it to turn horizontally while remaining supported.
[A45B 2019/001] with elastic ribs -- The CPC subclass A45B 2019/001 "with elastic ribs" designates patents for personal-use containers such as bags, suitcases or other carry-alls that incorporate one or more elastic rib elements to provide stretch, shape retention or reinforcement of the container's cavity. It falls under the A45B main group covering bags, luggage and similar items.
[A45B 2019/002] Umbrellas stored inside the stick when closed, e.g. collapsible into the handle -- A45B 2019/002 relates to umbrellas whose canopy collapses into the shaft when closed, allowing the entire device to be stored inside the handle for compactness and easy transport.
[A45B 2019/004] with rigid ribs -- A45B 2019/004 covers transportable containers or crates that incorporate rigid ribs as structural elements. The ribs are integral, non-flexible members that provide reinforcement, improve stacking strength, or maintain shape during handling and transport. This classification is used for packaging solutions where built-in ribbing is a defining feature.
[A45B 2019/005] without stick -- A45B 2019/005 classifies liquid containers that are provided without an integral stick or stirrer, covering bottles, jars and similar vessels where no built-in mixing element is present.
[A45B 2019/007] without stretchers -- A45B 2019/007 is a CPC subgroup for protective headgear that is constructed without stretchers, i.e., it lacks adjustable straps or tension-adjusting elements for securing it to the head. The classification applies to helmets, caps or similar safety equipment where retention is achieved by alternative means such as a rigid shell or
[A45B 2019/008] Umbrellas having upward pointing rib ends when closed, i.e. the lower dry side of the cover facing the outside when closed -- A45B 2019/008 covers umbrella designs whose ribs terminate upward so that, when the umbrella is folded, the dry side of the canopy faces outward. This configuration keeps the interior surface free of moisture and facilitates compact storage, distinguishing it from conventional umbrellas whose rib ends point downward.
[A45B 2019/023] Umbrellas with stretchers only -- A45B 2019/023 covers umbrellas whose canopy is supported solely by stretchers (ribs) that extend from the shaft to the edge of the canopy, without any additional central or peripheral support structures. The classification applies to rain- or sun-protecting devices where the stretchers are the only elements holding the fabric in shape.
[A45B 2019/026] Umbrellas without ribs -- A45B 2019/026 covers umbrellas whose canopy is supported without conventional ribs, using alternative structures such as flexible membranes, tensioned fabrics or inflatable elements to maintain shape. The classification applies to designs that eliminate rigid rib frameworks while still providing weather protection.
[A45B 2023/0006] Portable, self supported sunshades or weather protections -- A45B 2023/0006 covers devices that provide shade or protection from weather and are both portable and self-supporting, requiring no external structures for stability. Typical examples include freestanding umbrellas, pop-up canopies and collapsible shelters designed for easy transport and quick deployment.
[A45B 2023/0012] Ground supported umbrellas or sunshades on a single post, e.g. resting in or on a surface there below -- The code A45B 2023/0012 covers umbrellas or sunshades that are supported from below by a ground-anchored single post, such as a pole embedded in or resting on a surface, intended to provide shade for a specific area.
[A45B 2023/0018] with a canopy in form of an inverted cone -- A45B 2023/0018 covers items, typically furniture or protective structures, that incorporate a canopy shaped as an inverted cone. The classification is applied to designs where the conical canopy provides shelter or aesthetic enclosure for the object beneath, such as portable shade units, lounge furniture, or decorative canopies. This code groups inventions that employ that specific inverted-cone form to achieve coverage or visual effect.
[A45B 2023/0025] Umbrellas or sunshades mounted laterally on a wall or on an apparatus -- The CPC class A45B 2023/0025 covers umbrellas or sunshades that are mounted sideways on a wall or on a supporting apparatus, rather than being free-standing. It includes designs where the shade pivots or slides laterally to protect openings such as doors, windows or outdoor walkways, and can be fixed or retractable as part of a building or structural element.
[A45B 2023/0031] Cantilever umbrellas or sunshades with a support arm -- A45B 2023/0031 covers cantilever umbrellas or sunshades that are supported by a single arm extending from a fixed base, allowing the canopy to be positioned away from the pole. The entry includes devices where the support arm bears the load and provides shade over an area without surrounding columns.
[A45B 2023/0037] the support arm being attached to the stick or to the crown, the canopy being suspended there below -- In CPC A45B, which deals with umbrellas and parasols, the entry 2023/0037 addresses designs in which the support arm is attached to the stick or to the crown, allowing the canopy to be suspended directly below that attachment point.
[A45B 2023/0043] the support arm being attached to the stick or to runner, the canopy being suspended there above -- The entry concerns a carriage or stroller design where the support arm is mounted on the main stick or runner and the protective canopy is suspended from that arm, providing a stable and easily attached shelter.
[A45B 2023/005] the support arm being attached to a rib or being formed by a rib -- A45B 2023/005 covers furniture armrests that are either mounted on a supporting rib or are themselves formed as a rib, providing structural reinforcement for seating such as chairs or sofas.
[A45B 2023/0056] the support arm being formed by lazy-tongs, one support arm being a rib and the other arm being attached to the runner and to the rib -- A45B covers furniture and domestic articles, especially transport devices, and 2023/0056 specifies a support arm formed as lazy-tongs, with one arm acting as a rib and the other arm linked to a runner and to the rib, providing a foldable or sliding support structure.
[A45B 2023/0062] the support arm being telescopic -- This CPC entry concerns furniture accessories where the support arm is telescopic, enabling the arm to extend or retract for adjustable positioning of tables, chairs or similar items. It relates to mechanisms that provide variable reach or height.
[A45B 2023/0068] the support arm being foldable -- The entry covers furniture items whose support arm can be folded, such as chairs, sofas or tables whose arm pivots or collapses to allow compact storage, transport or reconfiguration. It pertains specifically to the structural features that enable the arm to be folded and secured in a closed position.
[A45B 2023/0075] the support arm being rotatable about a vertical axis for adjusting the position -- A45B 2023/0075 concerns devices in which a support arm can be rotated about a vertical axis to alter its orientation or position, typically found in adjustable furniture, workstations or mounting systems, allowing repositioning without changing the arm's height.
[A45B 2023/0081] the support arm being rotatable about a horizontal axis for adjusting the position -- A45B 2023/0081 refers to furniture elements whose support arm can rotate about a horizontal axis to change the position of the supported part, allowing height or angle adjustment of tables, chairs, work-stations or similar items. This feature provides ergonomic or functional positioning by pivoting the arm horizontally.
[A45B 2023/0087] having a planar opening movement wherein the struts or screen sheet rotate and spread around an axis, e.g. around the stick -- The subgroup covers devices whose opening action is planar, with the ribs, struts or screen sheet rotating and spreading outward about an axis such as a central stick. It applies to umbrellas, parasols, sun-shades and similar items where the covering panel expands by a side-to-side rotation rather than a folding or swinging motion.
[A45B 2023/0093] Sunshades or weather protections of other than umbrella type -- A45B 2023/0093 covers sunshades and weather-protective devices that are not umbrellas, such as fixed or retractable awnings, shade screens, and other structures designed to shield an area from sun, rain or wind. It includes mechanisms, mounting systems and material configurations specific to these non-umbrella protection solutions.
[A45B 2025/003] Accessories not covered by groups  A45B25/24  -  A45B25/30 -- A45B 2025/003 covers accessories for domestic or kitchen furniture that are not classified in the more specific groups A45B25/24 to A45B25/30, such as miscellaneous fittings, decorative elements or functional add-ons that do not fall under the defined sub-categories.
[A45B 2025/105] movable with respect to the shaft -- A45B 2025/105 relates to containers or packaging elements whose parts are designed to move relative to a shaft, such as caps, nozzles, or compartments that slide, rotate or telescope along the shaft for dispensing, sealing or adjustment functions. The classification covers the mechanical arrangements that enable this relative movement within the container structure.
[A45B 2025/146] with a crank connected to a rope -- A45B 2025/146 classifies furniture or related articles that incorporate a crank linked to a rope to effect movement or adjustment, such as raising, lowering, or tilting a table, bench or similar item by turning the crank which pulls the rope and changes the position of the device.
[A45B 2025/183] Covers with filtering or screening means for avoiding undesired radiation -- Covers equipped with filters or screens that block or reduce unwanted radiation, such as ultraviolet or infrared light, used on containers or openings to protect the contents from deterioration, contamination or health hazards caused by exposure to such radiation. This includes lids, caps or sleeves that incorporate material or structures specifically designed for radiation shielding.
[A45B 2025/186] Umbrellas with two or more covers -- A45B covers umbrellas and related protective devices, and the subdivision 2025/186 is applied to umbrellas that incorporate two or more covers or canopies, either stacked, detachable or interchangeable, to provide combined or selectable protection against rain, sun or wind.
[A45B 21/00] Umbrellas convertible into walking sticks -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Umbrellas convertible into walking sticks
[A45B 2200/00] Details not otherwise provided for in -- A45B 2200/00 covers specific details of walking sticks that are not addressed in other more specific classifications within the Cooperative Patent Classification system, focusing on unique or miscellaneous aspects.
[A45B 2200/05] Walking sticks -- Walking sticks are handheld devices used to support or aid a person while walking, often serving both functional and decorative purposes. They are classified under CPC A45B 2200/05, grouping them with other hand or travelling articles within the broader human necessities category.
[A45B 2200/055] for Nordic walking -- The A45B 2200/055 sub-classification covers walking sticks that are specially designed for Nordic walking, a fitness activity using poles to engage the upper body while walking. It belongs to the Human Necessities section, under Hand or Travelling Articles and the Walking Sticks group.
[A45B 2200/10] Umbrellas -- A45B 2200/10 designates umbrellas, covering portable devices with collapsible or fixed canopies used for personal protection against weather, including the shaft, frame, handle and associated mechanisms or covers, while excluding other walking-stick items.
[A45B 2200/1009] combined with other objects -- In the CPC, A45B 2200/1009 covers walking sticks that are integrated with or attached to additional objects such as built-in tools, devices or accessories, where the combination forms a single article. It falls under human necessities, hand or travelling articles, walking sticks.
[A45B 2200/1018] with illuminating devices, e.g. electrical -- This subclass covers walking sticks that incorporate an illumination device, typically an electric light, for visibility or signaling. It includes any walking stick equipped with a built-in light source, regardless of power source.
[A45B 2200/1027] with means for generating solar energy -- This subclass covers walking sticks that incorporate a solar energy generation system, such as photovoltaic cells integrated into the shaft or handle to power lights or electronic functions. It falls under human necessities, hand or travelling articles, and specifies walking sticks equipped with solar power features.
[A45B 2200/1036] with means for promoting air movement, e.g. ventilation holes, fans, ventilators, special shape for ventilation, suction means -- This subclass includes walking sticks that incorporate features to promote air movement, such as ventilation holes, built-in fans or ventilators, specially shaped sections for airflow, or suction mechanisms.
[A45B 2200/1045] with a misting device having at least one nozzle for spraying a fluid, e.g. water -- A45B 2200/1045 covers walking sticks that incorporate a misting system, featuring at least one nozzle to spray a fluid such as water. This subclass falls under the hierarchy Human Necessities → Hand or Travelling Articles → Walking Sticks, and pertains to designs where the misting device is integral to the stick.
[A45B 2200/1054] with containers for storing objects, e.g. box -- A45B 2200/1054 covers walking sticks that include an integrated container, such as a box, for storing objects. This sub-class falls under Human Necessities → Hand or travelling articles → Walking sticks and is used when the stick's storage feature is not covered by more specific categories.
[A45B 2200/1063] with tables -- A45B 2200/1063 classifies walking sticks that incorporate a built-in table, allowing the device to serve as a portable surface for writing, eating or other tasks while on the move. This subclass falls under Human Necessities → Hand or Travelling Articles → Walking Sticks.
[A45B 2200/1072] with other sunshades, awnings or tents -- A45B 2200/1072 pertains to walking sticks that incorporate additional features such as sunshades, awnings, or tents, not covered by more specific classifications. This code is part of the Cooperative Patent Classification system used by the EPO and USPTO.
[A45B 2200/1081] Umbrella handles -- A45B 2200/1081 covers umbrella handles, the component that connects the shaft to the canopy and is held by the user. It is placed under the CPC section for human necessities, specifically hand or travelling articles and walking sticks, and is used for patents describing the design, construction or improvement of such handles.
[A45B 2200/109] Sockets therefor -- The CPC subclass A45B 2200/109 defines sockets incorporated in walking sticks, covering attachment points or receptacles designed to hold accessories, tools or other elements. It falls under Human necessities → Hand or travelling articles → Walking sticks → Details not otherwise provided for.

[A45C] PURSES -- **Definition:** This place covers:  **Glossary:** - " flexible": providing a structure with no considerable resistance against deformation or decrease of the enclosed volume, whereby a two-dimensional stability may be provided, e.g. by a stiffened bottom - " semi-rigid": providing a structure with three-dimensional stability in an unloaded state and resilient resistance against deformation, or allowing a limited decrease, of the enclosed volume, e.g. by a resilient frame or a rigid frame with flexible side walls - " rigid": providing a structure with three-dimensional stability allowing no deformation leading to a considerable decrease of the enclosed volume - " collapsible": providing a feature other than use of flexible material for reducing the dimensions of flexible luggage or the enclosed volume of semi-rigid or rigid luggage - " extensible": means providing a feature other than use of flexible material for increasing the enclosed volume of flexible, semi-rigid or rigid luggage

[A45C 13/12] of press-button or turn-button fasteners -- A45C 13/12 covers fasteners for purses that are operated by a press-button or a turn-button mechanism, such as snap-type closures, push-button latches, or rotary catches that secure the bag's opening. This subclass falls under the broader categories of human necessities, hand or travelling articles, and purses.

[A45C 15/08] with watches -- A45C 15/08 designates purses, bags, luggage or similar receptacles that incorporate a watch as part of the item. The sub-class covers any hand-held article where a time-keeping device is integrated into the construction or accessory features.
[A45C 2001/003] Security cash bags -- A45C 2001/003 covers security cash bags used for the safe transport and storage of money. These bags are constructed from reinforced or tamper-evident materials and incorporate features such as locks, seals, or locking devices to deter theft and provide accountability during handling. The classification includes variations designed for banks, retailers, and other entities that require protected cash conveyance.
[A45C 2001/006] Cash boxes, e.g. with compartments -- A45C 2001/006 covers cash boxes, such as those with compartments, designed for securely holding money, coins or tickets in retail, banking or office environments. The classification includes devices that provide separate sections to organize different denominations or types of cash.
[A45C 2001/022] releasably fitted to a button or clothing item -- A45C of the CPC covers clothing accessories and dress fasteners. Sub-group 2001/022 refers to items that can be attached to a button or garment in a releasable way, such as detachable ornaments, fasteners or decorative elements that can be removed without permanent alteration.
[A45C 2001/026] combined with key rings or the like -- A45C 2001/026 covers containers such as boxes, bags or cases that are designed to be joined with key rings or similar attachment devices, allowing the item to be fastened to keys, belts or other objects for convenient transport. The classification includes both permanent and detachable linkages.
[A45C 2001/028] Rigid coin holders -- A45C 2001/028 pertains to rigid coin holders, devices made of non-flexible materials such as metal or hard plastic that keep coins fixed in place for storage, organization or display, commonly used in cash registers, vending machines, wallets or similar applications.
[A45C 2001/062] Clips for holding banknotes -- A45C 2001/062 relates to clips designed to hold banknotes, including money-clip devices, wallet inserts and similar fastening elements that secure paper currency without folding or enclosing it. The classification covers the shape, material and attachment features of such clips used for personal cash storage.
[A45C 2001/065] combined with card holders -- A45C 2001/065 relates to personal containers that incorporate card-holding elements, such as wallets, ticket or access-card cases, and other carriers where slots, pockets or compartments are combined with the primary storage function. This subclass groups designs that integrate card-holder features directly into the container's structure.
[A45C 2001/067] Rigid casings -- A45C 2001/067 covers rigid casings used in packaging, storage or transport, encompassing solid containers such as boxes, trays, bins, bottles and cans that retain their shape and do not collapse under normal handling. The entry falls within the broader A45C group for containers and packages.
[A45C 2001/083] combined with card holders -- A45C 2001/083 covers bags or sacks that incorporate a built-in holder for cards, such as credit-card or identification-card slots integrated into handbags, wallets, travel bags or similar containers. The classification applies to any carrying device where a card-holding feature is combined with the bag's primary function.
[A45C 2001/086] with additional compartments for storing other kinds of objects -- The A45C 2001/086 subclass groups containers that incorporate one or more additional compartments for holding different kinds of objects alongside the main content. It applies to designs such as multi-section boxes, storage units with separate pockets, or any container where extra integrated spaces are provided for distinct items.
[A45C 2001/101] with means for carrying on the body -- The CPC subclass A45C 2001/101 covers items that are intended to be worn on the body and include built-in means for carrying or supporting the wearer, such as belts, straps, harnesses or other attachment devices integrated into household or personal-use articles. It groups inventions where the primary function is body carriage or support.
[A45C 2001/102] Rigid coin-holders -- A45C 2001/102 covers rigid coin-holder devices such as trays, racks, boxes or lockable compartments designed to store, organize or dispense coins in machines or retail equipment. The classification applies to structures that keep coins immobile while allowing easy access for collection or payment.
[A45C 2001/104] with one or more recesses for receiving a coin, i.e. each recess receiving one single coin -- A45C 2001/104 covers containers or devices that incorporate one or more individual recesses each sized to receive a single coin. The design is intended for coin-controlled operation, such as vending mechanisms, coin-accepting dispensers, or money-storage boxes, where each recess holds a separate coin before release. This feature enables precise coin handling and counting within the broader class of containers for storing or transporting articles.
[A45C 2001/105] with one or more recesses for receiving coins, i.e. the coins being stored side by side in a single recess, e.g. groove with coins flat in one planar surface -- This CPC subgroup covers containers or trays that incorporate one or more recessed grooves designed to hold coins side-by-side within a single slot, keeping the coins flat on a planar surface. It applies to devices such as cash registers, vending machines or accounting tools where coins are stored in these recesses.
[A45C 2001/107] with coins stored in piles, i.e. several coins stacked in a compartment -- A45C 2001/107 covers containers that store coins in piles, meaning several coins are stacked in a compartment rather than kept loose or in separate slots. This subclass pertains to designs where the storage area allows coins to accumulate vertically, facilitating easy retrieval and counting.
[A45C 2001/108] with means for controlling the dispensing of coins, e.g. followers, pushers or selectors -- A45C 2001/108 classifies coin-dispensing mechanisms that incorporate devices to regulate the release of coins, such as followers, pushers or selectors. It covers apparatuses where these control elements ensure accurate, orderly dispensing in vending, parking or similar automated payment systems.
[A45C 2001/125] Treat collecting devices, e.g. bags for collecting contributions -- A45C 2001/125 covers devices designed to gather contributions, such as bags, containers, or other receptacles used for collecting tips, donations, or small payments in venues like restaurants, hotels, charitable events, or public spaces. It includes features that facilitate easy handling, sealing, or transport of the collected items.
[A45C 2003/002] Mesh materials -- A45C is the CPC group for textile articles used in the kitchen, such as cloths, napkins and related items. The sub-entry 2003/002 specifies mesh materials, covering woven or knitted netting employed in kitchen products like sieves, colanders, strainers and other perforated textile components.
[A45C 2003/005] Bags having two volumes connected at upper ends, e.g. saddle bags -- The A45C 2003/005 subgroup covers bags composed of two separate volumes that are joined together at their upper ends, forming a single unit with distinct compartments. Typical examples are saddle bags for bicycles or motorcycles that provide two side-by-side storage spaces.
[A45C 2003/007] Sport bags -- A45C 2003/007 covers sport bags, including backpacks, duffel, tote and other portable containers designed specifically for carrying sports equipment, clothing or accessories. The entry includes bags with features such as multiple compartments, ergonomic straps, water-resistance and ventilation, intended for use in activities like running, hiking, gym workouts or team sports. It also covers specialized sport-specific designs such as cycling saddlebags, ski-boot carriers and golf-bag pouches.
[A45C 2003/008] Travelling bags -- A45C 2003/008 covers portable containers intended for carrying personal belongings while traveling, including suitcases, trunks, carry-ons, backpacks and trolley bags, as well as accessories that facilitate transport.
[A45C 2005/032] semi-rigid, i.e. resistant against deformation and resilient, e.g. with a resilient frame -- A45C 2005/032 covers semi-rigid articles such as containers, trays or similar items that are resistant to deformation yet retain some flexibility, typically achieved by incorporating a resilient frame or structure. The entry is used in the CPC for products that combine firmness with a degree of resilience.
[A45C 2005/035] soft-sided, i.e. with flexible side walls covering a rigid frame -- The code A45C 2005/035 designates articles whose structure consists of a rigid internal frame that is enveloped by flexible side walls, typical of soft-sided luggage, bags and cases. It includes items
[A45C 2005/037] with a hard shell, i.e. rigid shell as volume creating element -- A45C 2005/037 covers articles whose shape or volume is formed by a hard, rigid shell that serves as the primary structural element, such as containers, housings or protective cases made of solid material rather than flexible or inflatable components. The classification applies to designs where the shell itself defines the interior space and provides the necessary strength and protection.
[A45C 2005/10] Travelling baskets -- It covers portable baskets intended for easy transport, such as laundry, shopping or personal items
[A45C 2005/142] with spherical rolling means, e.g. ball casters -- A45C 2005/142 pertains to furniture, transport means or similar articles that incorporate spherical rolling components, such as ball casters, to provide smooth movement across a surface.
[A45C 2005/147] for climbing stairs -- A45C 2005/147 covers devices that aid people in climbing stairs, including stair-lifts, step-assisted platforms and other mechanisms that provide powered or manual assistance for moving upward or downward on stairways.
[A45C 2005/148] Other arrangements of the rolling means -- The subclass A45C 2005/148 covers inventions that use rolling devices for food preparation in unconventional configurations, such as atypical placements, combined functions or novel support structures that differ from standard rolling pins or cylinders. It includes alternative arrangements of rollers integrated into work surfaces, containers or other kitchen apparatus.
[A45C 2007/0004] convertible into an open planar surface, e.g. a sheet -- A45C 2007/0004 covers articles that can be transformed from a three-dimensional shape into an open, flat planar form such as a sheet, typically by unfolding, unrolling or spreading. It includes items like collapsible containers, flexible screens, protective covers and other structures designed to lie flat for storage or use after being expanded or erected.
[A45C 2007/0009] with an elongated shape, i.e. belt-shaped -- A45C 2007/0009 designates items that have an elongated, belt-like shape, covering accessories such as straps, belts, or other wrap-around components where the primary characteristic is a long, thin profile. This subclass falls under A45C, which concerns bags, cases and similar carrying articles.
[A45C 2007/0013] with fasteners along the periphery of the surface -- A45C 2007/0013 covers garments or textile products that incorporate fastening elements positioned around the outer edge of a surface, such as zippers, buttons, hooks or other closures arranged along the perimeter rather than centrally. This subclass is used to identify articles whose primary means of closure runs along the border of the material.
[A45C 2009/002] into a seat -- A45C 2009/002 in the Cooperative Patent Classification refers to luggage or bags designed to fit specifically into a seat, often for storage or transport purposes. This classification is used by both the European Patent Office and the United States Patent and Trademark Office.
[A45C 2009/005] into a vehicle, e.g. scooter -- A45C 2009/005 covers devices for transporting a small vehicle such as a scooter inside a larger vehicle. It includes racks, carriers, storage compartments and securing mechanisms that enable a scooter to be loaded, held in place and removed from a car, van or similar transport vehicle.
[A45C 2009/007] into other hand or travelling articles -- A45C 2009/007 covers containers and packaging intended for hand-held or travel use, including portable bottles, flasks, collapsible or fold-able containers and other articles designed to be carried easily while moving. The classification addresses features that facilitate convenient handling, compact storage and durability required for use on the go.
[A45C 2011/006] for storing a plurality of disposable lens packages -- The CPC subclass A45C 2011/006 covers devices or containers designed to hold several disposable contact-lens packages together for convenient storage and transport. It includes multi-compartment cases, trays or holders that keep individual sealed lens units organized and protected until use.
[A45C 2011/007] Receptacles for personal medical or care products, e.g. drugs, condoms or patches; Small first-aid kits -- A45C 2011/007 covers containers specifically shaped or configured to hold personal medical or care items such as medication, condoms, transdermal patches, or compact first-aid supplies. The classification includes small kits or receptacles intended for portable, individual use and for keeping these products protected, organized and readily accessible.
[A45C 2011/186] with protection from unauthorised reading by remotely readable data carriers -- The CPC subclass A45C 2011/186 covers articles-typically garments, accessories or other consumer goods-equipped with remotely readable data carriers such as RFID or NFC tags that incorporate security features to prevent unauthorised reading. It includes methods and devices that encrypt, block or otherwise protect the stored information from illicit access.
[A45C 2011/188] Media card holders, e.g. memory cards, SIM cards -- A45C 2011/188 covers devices designed to hold or protect small electronic cards such as memory cards, SIM cards, or other media cards. The classification includes holders, cases, and accessories that secure the cards while facilitating insertion, removal, or connection to equipment. It applies to both consumer and professional applications.
[A45C 2011/322] Sheath-like bags for protecting a key-blade or for receiving a key -- A45C 2011/322 covers sheath-like bags designed to protect a key-blade or to hold a key, typically made of flexible material such as leather, fabric or polymer. The entry includes protective covers, key cases and similar accessories that encase the key for safety, concealment or convenience.
[A45C 2013/025] for holding portable computers or accessories therefor -- A45C-2013/025 covers devices designed to hold or transport portable computers and related accessories, such as laptop cases, bags, sleeves, and mounts. The classification includes any structure or container that secures a notebook, tablet or its peripherals for convenient carrying, storage or use while on the move.
[A45C 2013/026] Inserts -- A45C 2013/026 covers inserts used in packaging, such as compartments, dividers, trays, pads or other fitted elements that are placed inside containers to protect, separate or organize the packed goods. The code applies to designs that are integral to or removable within the primary packaging.
[A45C 2013/028] for holding or compacting items by creating vacuum -- A45C 2013/028 relates to devices that hold or compress objects by removing air to create a vacuum, encompassing vacuum chambers, packaging machines and sealing apparatus that compact items for storage or transport.
[A45C 2013/1007] with two parallel resilient strips, hinged at their ends and opened by pressure in longitudinal direction on both ends -- The entry refers to a furniture element, such as a table or similar support, that uses two parallel resilient strips hinged at their ends; the strips are drawn apart by applying longitudinal pressure to both ends, creating a spring-loaded opening motion.
[A45C 2013/1015] of hook and loop type -- A45C 2013/1015 covers fasteners that operate by a hook-and-loop mechanism, such as Velcro, used to join textile articles or components. The classification includes the design, construction, and attachment methods of these reusable, adjustable closures.
[A45C 2013/1061] of elastic straps or bands -- A45C 2013/1061 classifies elastic straps or bands employed as closures for containers, bottles, cans or similar packaging. It covers the use of stretchable or rubberized bands that secure lids, caps or other closure elements, providing a flexible retaining mechanism.
[A45C 2013/223] Detachable handles -- A45C 2013/223 covers detachable handles used on articles such as furniture, containers, or devices where the handle can be removed or re-attached without damaging the article. It includes mechanisms, fittings or fastening methods that allow easy installation, removal, or interchange of the handle. The classification is applied to inventions that improve the functionality, ergonomics or modularity of such removable handles.
[A45C 2013/226] Handles foldable into the luggage -- This subclass covers luggage or bags equipped with handles that can be folded or retracted into the body of the item, allowing the handle to be stored inside the case when not in use. It includes designs where the handle is integrally formed and may be locked in a folded position to improve compactness and protect the handle during transport.
[A45C 2013/265] the handle being adjustable in rotation to a towing element -- A45C 2013/265 relates to transport devices, particularly luggage or containers, where the pulling handle can be rotated relative to the towing element such as a wheel or strap. The classification covers mechanisms that allow the handle to be turned to different angles while remaining attached, facilitating maneuverability and ergonomic use.
[A45C 2013/267] the handle being slidable, extractable and lockable in one or more positions -- A45C 2013/267 classifies cup, mug or similar tableware whose handle can slide along the vessel, be removed and secured in one or more set positions, offering adjustable or detachable grip options for ergonomic or storage purposes.
[A45C 2013/303] with strap loop for carrying an object by passing the hand through the loop, the loop resting partially around the wrist -- The subclass refers to articles that include a strap loop designed so the user can pass a hand through the loop and have it rest partly around the wrist while carrying the object. This feature is typical of bags, briefcases or similar containers that are held by the hand-wrist combination for ease of transport.
[A45C 2013/306] for attaching auxiliary articles to luggage, e.g. piggyback -- This CPC subgroup covers arrangements for fastening auxiliary accessories or additional items to luggage, such as piggyback systems, strap attachments, clips or other mounting devices. It includes devices that enable users to securely connect extra bags, pockets or accessories to a primary piece of luggage.
[A45C 2200/00] Details not otherwise provided for in -- A45C 2200/00 is a CPC subclass for purses that includes any design, construction or functional details not specifically covered by other more narrowly defined entries, such as unusual closures, decorative elements, internal compartments or material treatments that fall outside the existing subclasses. It serves as a catch-all for miscellaneous purse features within the "Hand or travelling articles" group.
[A45C 2200/05] Means for indicating features of the content from outside -- A45C 2200/05 covers purses that incorporate external indicators revealing information about their contents, such as transparent windows, visible labels, color coding or other markings that denote the type, quantity or security status of the items inside without opening the bag.
[A45C 2200/10] Transparent walls -- A45C 2200/10 covers purses that incorporate transparent walls, such as clear panels or windows that allow the contents to be seen. The feature is typically integrated into handbags, wallets or travel bags to provide visual access without opening the item. This subclass falls under Human Necessities → Hand or Travelling Articles → Purses.
[A45C 2200/15] Articles convertible into a stand, e.g. for displaying purposes -- A45C 2200/15 covers purses or similar hand-carried articles that can be reconfigured into a stand for displaying items. This includes bags whose structure or components fold, collapse or extend to provide a stable surface, enabling the product itself to serve as a display fixture.
[A45C 2200/20] Carrying beverage vessels, e.g. bottles -- A45C 2200/20 covers designs and features of purses, bags or similar items specifically devised to hold or transport beverage containers such as bottles. It includes any structural or functional elements that enable convenient carrying of drinks but does not pertain to the containers themselves.

[A45D 19/0041] Processes for treating the hair of the scalp -- The CPC subclass A45D 19/0041 covers methods used to treat the hair on the scalp, including chemical, physical or mechanical processes that cleanse, condition, color, style, protect or otherwise modify scalp hair. It encompasses procedures such as washing, conditioning, bleaching, dyeing, perming, straightening, and applying treatments that affect hair texture, health or appearance.
[A45D 19/005] Shampooing; Conditioning; Washing hair for hairdressing purposes -- A45D 19/005 relates to shampooing, conditioning and washing hair for hairdressing purposes, describing the methods and apparatus used by professionals to clean and treat the hair or scalp. It falls under the broader categories of human necessities, hand or travelling articles, and hairdressing or shaving equipment.
[A45D 19/0066] Coloring or bleaching -- A45D 19/0066 designates the portion of the CPC that deals with coloring or bleaching of hair or the scalp within the broader group for cleaning hair or scalp, itself part of hairdressing or shaving equipment under hand-or-travelling articles and human necessities. It covers methods, compositions and apparatuses used to apply dyes, bleaches or related agents to achieve hair colour change or lightening.

It falls under hairdressing or shaving equipment, specifically the subcategory for cleaning the hair or scalp, and relates to techniques that produce decorative or functional designs while washing or treating hair.

[A45D 2040/0037] by inserting the new stick at the lower, operating end of the casing -- The entry covers kitchen or cooking devices in which a replacement or auxiliary stick is placed into the lower, operating end of a housing or casing, enabling the stick to perform its function-such as dispensing, stirring, or cleaning-within the appliance. This insertion-at-the-lower-end arrangement is the defining technical feature of the claimed invention.
[A45D 2040/0043] by inserting the new stick at the upper, applying end of the casing -- A45D 2040/0043 covers the process of inserting a new stick into the upper, applying end of a casing or holder, typically for smoking articles. It relates to assembling or refurbishing a device by placing a fresh component at the top part of the casing to create a functional product.
[A45D 2040/005] by removing the old stick from the cartridge by linear, sliding movement of stick relative to the cartridge -- A45D 2040/005 covers devices or methods for extracting a used stick from a cartridge by moving the stick linearly and sliding it relative to the cartridge, enabling the old stick to be removed without rotating or disassembling the cartridge. This classification applies to smoking or vaping cartridges where a straight-line withdrawal action is employed.
[A45D 2040/0056] by removing the old stick from the cartridge by screw movement of stick relative to the cartridge -- The entry covers a device or method in which the used stick is detached from its cartridge by rotating the stick relative to the cartridge, using a screw-type movement to unscrew and remove the stick for replacement. This feature is applied to cigarette or similar smoking accessories.
[A45D 2040/0062] Stick holding cups with retaining means, e.g. clamping means -- A45D 2040/0062 covers arrangements where a cup, such as a beverage or food container, is attached to a stick or rod and secured by a retaining device, for example a clamp, bracket or locking clip. The subclass is used for portable cup-holding solutions that keep the cup stable on the stick during handling or transport.
[A45D 2040/0093] storing soluble solid substances and suitable for application after adding a liquid solvent, e.g. water -- A45D 2040/0093 covers containers or dispensing devices that hold solid substances which are soluble and intended to be used after a liquid, usually water, is added to dissolve them for application, such as powdered detergents or cleaning agents prepared by mixing with water.
[A45D 2040/105] into the retracted position -- A45D 2040/105 pertains to tables or table accessories that can be moved into a retracted position, such as collapsible legs, fold-down surfaces, telescoping mechanisms or any feature that allows the tabletop or its components to be drawn in or folded for compact storage.
[A45D 2040/201] Accessories -- A45D 2040/201 in the Cooperative Patent Classification covers accessories that complement or enhance primary items such as handbags, luggage, school bags and similar containers, including straps, decorative elements, protective covers and other supplemental components.
[A45D 2040/202] Sharpeners specially adapted for pencil-like cosmetics -- This subclass covers devices designed to sharpen cosmetic products shaped like pencils, such as lip, eye or brow pencils. It includes mechanical or electric sharpeners, refillable
[A45D 2040/204] the cosmetic being in a cartridge -- A45D 2040/204 in the Cooperative Patent Classification designates cosmetics that are provided in a cartridge, including refillable or single-use cartridges for products such as lipstick, foundation or other makeup that is stored and dispensed from the cartridge.
[A45D 2040/207] the relative movement being made by an axial action, e.g. by pushing -- A45D 2040/207 concerns furniture or furnishing components whose parts shift relative to each other by an axial force, such as a push or pull along the axis of the element. It includes mechanisms where movement is generated by straight-line pressure, for example adjustable legs, sliding sections or locking devices activated by axial motion.
[A45D 2040/208] the relative movement being made by a rotating action, e.g. effected by a screw -- The A45D 2040/208 subclass covers garments, apparel accessories or textile assemblies whose adjustment or positioning relies on a rotating mechanism, such as a screw thread, to produce relative movement between parts. It includes devices like adjustable straps, fasteners or closures where turning a screw advances or withdraws a component to tighten, loosen or set the item in place.
[A45D 2040/223] comprising a latch -- The CPC A45D 2040/223 code covers containers such as bins, boxes or cupboards intended for household or domestic use that incorporate a latch to secure the opening, covering any latch integrated into the container to retain its contents.
[A45D 2040/224] comprising a snap fastener -- A45D 2040/224 covers articles, primarily garments or accessories, whose construction includes a snap fastener as a closing or fastening element. The classification is applied to items where the snap fastener is an integral part of the product's design, enabling the joining or separating of components. It relates to the presence of snaps rather than any specific type or material of the fastener.
[A45D 2040/225] characterised by the opening or closing movement of the lid -- A45D 2040/225 relates to containers or similar devices whose primary technical feature is a lid that is moved to open or close, including hinged, sliding, spring-loaded or otherwise actuated lids used for food, beverage or other products. The classification is applied when the mechanism of opening or closing the lid is a defining element of the invention.
[A45D 2040/226] by limiting the angle between base and lid in the open position -- A45D 2040/226 relates to containers, especially food-service items, whose lid or cover is designed to restrict the angle between the base and the lid when opened. The feature ensures the lid can only open to a predetermined degree, improving safety, stability or dispensing control. This subclass is used for mechanisms that mechanically limit the opening angle of the lid.
[A45D 2040/227] by biasing the lid towards the open position -- A45D 2040/227 pertains to containers whose lids are designed to stay in an open position by a biasing mechanism, such as a spring or cam, that automatically pushes the lid outward when released. This subclass covers lid constructions that facilitate easy access while preventing accidental closure.

[A45D 2200/00] Details not otherwise provided for in -- A45D 2200/00 is a catch-all sub-group for hair-dressing or shaving equipment that does not fall within any more specific CPC subclasses. It covers miscellaneous or undefined items such as novel brushes, combs, razors or accessory parts not otherwise listed, providing a slot for inventions whose details are not otherwise provided for in the classification hierarchy.
[A45D 2200/05] Details of containers -- A45D 2200/05 covers the specific features of containers used with hair-dressing or shaving devices, such as their shape, material, opening mechanism, dispensing system or means of securing the product. It falls under the broader categories of human necessities, hand-or-travelling articles, and hair-dressing or shaving equipment.
[A45D 2200/051] Airtight containers -- A45D 2200/051 designates airtight containers within the hairdressing or shaving equipment subclass, covering sealed vessels that protect shampoos, gels, creams or other personal-care products from air and moisture. The code is used to classify devices that maintain product integrity by preventing exposure to the environment.
[A45D 2200/052] Means for indicating features of the content from outside, e.g. window -- A device attached to a hairdressing or shaving product that allows the user to see the contents from the outside, such as a transparent window or similar indicator. It provides visual information about the product's material without opening the container.
[A45D 2200/053] Transparent containers -- A45D 2200/053 covers transparent containers used in hair-dressing or shaving devices, such as clear bottles, jars, or caps that hold cosmetics, styling agents or shaving products. The subclass groups any non-specified transparent receptacles employed for storing, dispensing or protecting these personal-care substances.
[A45D 2200/054] Means for supplying liquid to the outlet of the container -- A45D 2200/054 covers devices that deliver liquid from a container to its outlet, such as pumps, sprays or dispensers used in hair-dressing or shaving equipment. It is part of the broader group for hand-or-travelling articles under human necessities. This subclass addresses any means for supplying fluid that are not otherwise specified in more detailed categories.
[A45D 2200/055] Piston or plunger for supplying the liquid to the applicator -- A45D 2200/055 covers pistons or plungers that move liquid from a reservoir into a hair-dressing or shaving applicator. The element forms part of hand-held devices such as shampoo dispensers, conditioner sprayers or gel applicators, delivering the product directly to the user's hair or skin. It is classified under hair-dressing equipment within the broader group of hand or travelling articles.
[A45D 2200/056] Reciprocating pumps, i.e. with variable volume chamber wherein pressure and vacuum are alternately generated -- A45D 2200/056 refers to hair- or shaving-related devices that incorporate a reciprocating pump with a variable-volume chamber that alternately generates pressure and vacuum. These pumps are used to dispense liquids such as shampoo, conditioner, shaving cream or similar formulations in personal-care equipment. The classification captures the mechanism rather than the specific product.
[A45D 2200/057] Spray nozzles -- Spray nozzles are components that atomize liquids for application in hairdressing or shaving devices, delivering mist or fine sprays onto hair or skin. The classification covers designs and arrangements of such nozzles when the specific details are not covered elsewhere, falling under human necessities, hand or travelling articles, and hair-dressing or shaving equipment.
[A45D 2200/058] Means for mixing different substances prior to application -- A45D 2200/058 covers devices incorporated in hair-dressing or shaving tools that combine two or more substances immediately before they are applied to hair, skin or a shaving surface. Examples include mixing chambers, dual-dispensing nozzles or blendable cartridges that merge liquids such as shampoo, conditioner, styling gels or shaving foams for a single, combined application.
[A45D 2200/10] Details of applicators -- A45D 2200/10 classifies features that describe the design or construction of applicators used with hairdressing or shaving tools, such as brushes, combs, or dispensers. It encompasses any specific details of how the applicator delivers product or performs its function, without further subdivision.
[A45D 2200/1009] Applicators comprising a pad, tissue, sponge, or the like -- This subclass covers hair-dressing or shaving tools that incorporate a pad, tissue, sponge or similar element to apply a product to the user. It includes replaceable or integral applicator heads such as cleansing pads, shaving sponges or disposable tissues that form part of a handheld device for grooming. The classification is used for patents describing these applicator components.
[A45D 2200/1018] comprising a pad, i.e. a cushion-like mass of soft material, with or without gripping means -- This subclass covers hairdressing or shaving tools that incorporate a cushion-like pad of soft material, with or without an attached gripping means, used to cushion or support the device during operation. It applies when the pad detail is not specified elsewhere in the classification.
[A45D 2200/1027] Tissues, i.e. thin applicator sheets -- A45D 2200/1027 classifies thin applicator sheets, commonly called tissues, used in hairdressing or shaving equipment. These are flexible, absorbent sheets designed for applying liquids or powders to the scalp, face, or body during personal grooming. The code falls under the hierarchy of human necessities, hand or travelling articles, and hairdressing or shaving equipment.
[A45D 2200/1036] containing a cosmetic substance, e.g. impregnated with liquid or containing a soluble solid substance -- A45D 2200/1036 classifies hairdressing or shaving articles that are designed to hold or release a cosmetic substance, such as tools impregnated with liquid or containing a soluble solid. It falls under Human Necessities → Hand or Travelling Articles → Hairdressing or Shaving Equipment, covering items where the cosmetic function is integrated into the equipment itself.
[A45D 2200/1045] with one or more internal reservoirs, e.g. rupturable microcapsules -- The entry covers hairdressing or shaving devices that incorporate one or more internal reservoirs, such as rupturable micro-capsules, to store and release substances during use. It falls under the human necessities group, specifically hand-or-travelling articles for personal grooming.
[A45D 2200/1054] Abrasive cosmetic skin peeling or scrubbing -- A45D 2200/1054 designates abrasive cosmetic devices for skin peeling or scrubbing, such as exfoliating pads or scrubbers, that are not otherwise specified. It belongs to the hierarchy of human necessities, hand-or-travelling articles, and hairdressing or shaving equipment.
[A45D 2200/1063] Removing cosmetic substances, e.g. make-up -- A45D 2200/1063 covers devices or methods used to eliminate cosmetic substances such as makeup from the skin or hair, including wipes, cleaning solutions, brushes or other tools designed for makeup removal. It falls under hairdressing or shaving equipment within the broader category of hand or travelling articles for human necessities.
[A45D 2200/1072] Eyeliners -- The CPC subclass A45D 2200/1072 covers eyeliners, which are cosmetic products formulated to colour or line the eyelid margin for decorative or corrective purposes. It includes pencils, gels, liquids and powders designed for application with a brush, pen-like tip or applicator, as well as related packaging and dispensing devices.
[A45D 2200/1081] Applying a substance on the back of the user -- This subclass refers to devices or methods that apply a substance-such as a cream, lotion, medicated gel or patch-directly to the back of the user. It covers mechanisms for dispensing, spreading or otherwise delivering the material onto the back, often integrated with hair-dressing or shaving equipment.
[A45D 2200/109] Shaving brushes with means for storing shaving products, e.g. reservoir, shaving soap holder -- This CPC subclass covers shaving brushes that incorporate built-in containers for shaving soap or other shaving products, such as a reservoir or soap holder, allowing simultaneous storage and use. It is classified under human necessities, specifically hand or travelling articles and hairdressing or shaving equipment.
[A45D 2200/15] Temperature -- A45D 2200/15 classifies hair-dressing or shaving devices that incorporate temperature-related features, such as heating elements, cooling mechanisms, thermostatic control or temperature-adjustable components. It covers any detail of the equipment that pertains to the generation, regulation or measurement of temperature.
[A45D 2200/152] Walls with thermally insulating properties, i.e. preventing the exposure of cosmetics to the exterior temperature -- A45D 2200/152 concerns walls that incorporate thermal insulation to protect cosmetic products from external temperature changes. It falls under hairdressing or shaving equipment, covering design features that keep cosmetics at a stable temperature during storage or use.
[A45D 2200/155] Heating or cooling means, i.e. for storing or applying cosmetic products at a predetermined temperature -- The code A45D 2200/155 covers heating or cooling devices used to store or apply cosmetic products at a set temperature, such as thermally regulated hair-dressing or shaving equipment. It falls under the hierarchy of Human Necessities, Hand or Travelling Articles, and Hairdressing or Shaving Equipment.
[A45D 2200/157] Heating means for mascara applicators -- The subclass A45D 2200/157 covers mascara applicators that incorporate a heating element to warm the brush or wand, facilitating smoother glide and better pigment release. It includes devices where the heat source is integrated into the applicator's body or tip, enabling thermal conditioning of the mascara during use.
[A45D 2200/20] Additional enhancing means -- A45D 2200/20 covers accessories or auxiliary features that improve the function or performance of hair-dressing or shaving devices, such as attachments, boosters or auxiliary mechanisms that enhance cutting, styling, or cleaning. It applies to any additional means incorporated into or used with the primary equipment to increase its effectiveness. This subclass falls under the broader categories of human necessities, hand-or-travelling articles, and hair-dressing or shaving equipment.
[A45D 2200/202] Ionisation -- The CPC subclass A45D 2200/202 covers hair-dressing or shaving equipment that employs ionisation, such as devices that generate ions to neutralise static electricity, improve hair smoothness, or enhance conditioning during styling or grooming. It includes any apparatus where ionising technology is integral to the tool's operation.
[A45D 2200/205] Radiation, e.g. UV, infrared -- A45D 2200/205 covers hair-dressing or shaving equipment that employs radiation such as ultraviolet or infrared energy, for example devices that use UV or infrared to style, dry, straighten or otherwise treat hair or facial hair, where the specific details are not addressed elsewhere in the classification.
[A45D 2200/207] Vibration, e.g. ultrasound -- The code A45D 2200/207 covers hair-dressing or shaving tools that incorporate vibration or ultrasonic energy, such as ultrasonic hair dryers, shavers or other grooming devices that use high-frequency motion to assist styling or cutting. It falls under human necessities, hand or travelling articles, hairdressing or shaving equipment.
[A45D 2200/25] Kits -- A45D 2200/25 covers kits that contain hair-dressing or shaving equipment, such as grouped combs, scissors, razors, brushes, or related accessories packaged together for consumer or professional use. The classification applies when the individual items are not described separately elsewhere and are presented as a combined set.

[A45D 42/22] Metal mirrors -- Metal mirrors are reflective devices made chiefly of a metallic substrate, intended for personal grooming or toiletry use. The classification covers their construction, mounting, surface treatments and accessories that enable a clear, durable reflective surface for activities such as hairdressing, shaving or makeup application. It includes both plain and ornamented metal mirrors designed for handheld or fixed placement in bathrooms or dressing areas.

[A45D 6/20] Devices for controlling the temperature of hair curlers -- A45D 6/20 covers accessories that regulate the temperature of hair-curling or hair-waving devices, such as thermostatic controls, heating elements or sensors that maintain a preset heat level to ensure consistent styling performance.
[A45D 7/00] Processes of waving, straightening or curling hair -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Illustrative examples of subject matter classified in this group.
[A45D 7/02] thermal -- A45D 7/02 covers thermal methods and devices used to wave, straighten or curl hair, such as heaters, irons, rollers or other apparatus that apply heat to modify hair shape. The classification includes both the heating elements and the associated control means for safely delivering temperature to the hair.

[A45D 7/06] combined chemical and thermal -- A45D 7/06 covers methods that combine a chemical composition with heat to modify hair shape, such as waving, straightening or curling. The process involves applying a chemical agent to the hair and then using thermal energy to set the desired form, falling under hairdressing equipment and procedures for altering hair texture.

[A45D 8/001] Multi-step processes of dressing hair using a hair-holding device -- The CPC subclass A45D 8/001 relates to multi-step methods for dressing hair with a hair-holding device such as clips, pins, bands or similar fasteners. It covers sequential procedures that position, secure and adjust the device as part of a hair-dressing process.
[A45D 8/002] Accessories therefor -- A45D 8/002 covers accessories for hair-holding devices such as clips, pins, bands and similar items used to secure hair in place.
[A45D 8/004] with decorative arrangements or form -- A45D 8/004 covers hair-holding devices that feature ornamental designs or shaped forms, such as decorative combs, clips, or barrettes. It is a subclass of hairdressing or shaving equipment within the broader groups of hand or travelling articles and human necessities.
[A45D 8/006] Interchangeable ornaments attached to hair holding devices -- The A45D 8/006 subclass pertains to detachable decorative pieces that can be fitted onto hair-holding devices such as clips, combs, pins or barrettes. It covers interchangeable charms, beads, jewels or similar ornaments intended to be attached, removed or swapped for personal styling or fashion purposes.
[A45D 8/008] Ornaments specially adapted for being attached to small strands of hair -- The CPC subclass A45D 8/008 covers ornaments specially adapted for being attached to small strands of hair, such as hairpins, barrette accessories, or decorative clips designed to secure individual hair strands. It falls under hair-holding devices in the hairdressing equipment group.

[A45F] TRAVELLING OR CAMP EQUIPMENT: SACKS OR PACKS CARRIED ON THE BODY -- **Definition:** This place covers:  **Glossary:** - Knapsack: A bag with shoulder straps, carried on the back
[A45F 2003/001] Accessories -- A45F 2003/001 covers accessories for footwear, including items such as shoelaces, fastening devices, heel protectors, insoles, footbeds, decorative elements and other supplemental parts that are attached to or used with shoes or boots. It groups inventions that improve fit, comfort, function or appearance of footwear without constituting a separate type of shoe.
[A45F 2003/002] Sacks or packs carried on the body by means of a single strap passing around the neck -- A45F 2003/002 refers to sacks or packs that are worn on the body using a single strap that loops around the neck. The classification covers designs where the neck strap is the sole means of suspending the bag, such as messenger-style or neck-strap bags, enabling hands-free carriage of the contents.
[A45F 2003/003] combined with other objects;  specially adapted for carrying specific objects -- A45F 2003/003 covers containers, carriers or transport devices that are integrated with other components or specially shaped to hold particular items, such as boxes with built-in handles, trays fitted with partitions for specific goods, or carriers that combine storage and handling functions for designated objects.
[A45F 2003/006] Sacks or packs carried on the body by means of straps passing around an arm or a leg -- A45F 2003/006 covers personal carrying devices such as sacks, packs or bags that are secured to the body with straps that loop around an arm, a leg or both, enabling the load to be transported hands-free. It includes backpacks, shoulder-bags, thigh-bags and similar equipage.
[A45F 2003/007] Sacks or packs carried on the body by means of one strap passing around the neck and one additional strap around the waist -- The CPC subclass A45F 2003/007 relates to sacks or packs that are worn on the body using one strap that loops around the neck and an additional strap that encircles the waist. It includes items such as backpacks, messenger bags and similar personal carry-on containers.
[A45F 2003/008] Sacks or packs carried on the body by means of one strap passing around the waist and additional straps around a leg or an arm -- Sacks or packs designed to be carried on the body using a single strap that loops around the waist together with an additional strap that secures the item around a leg or an arm, enabling hands-free transport while keeping the load close to the wearer.
[A45F 2003/025] and one additional strap around the waist -- A45F 2003/025 refers to bags or travel accessories that incorporate an extra strap designed to loop around the wearer's waist, providing an additional means of securing the item to the body. This feature is typically found on handbags, messenger bags or small luggage items intended for hands-free transport. It falls within the broader CPC class for personal carrying equipment.
[A45F 2003/045] and one additional strap around the waist -- A45F 2003/045 covers garments that incorporate a single extra strap encircling the waist, used to provide additional support, fastening or decorative function in clothing such as overalls, aprons or similar apparel.
[A45F 2003/122] Back cushioning parts or details of backpacks, e.g. with ventilation -- A45F 2003/122 relates to back cushioning parts or details of backpacks, such as padding inserts, air-circulating panels or ventilation structures that improve wearer comfort and heat dissipation.
[A45F 2003/125] with ventilation -- A45F 2003/125 classifies mouth-nose protectors, respirators or facemasks that incorporate a ventilation feature, such as a valve or breathing channel, allowing airflow while maintaining filtration or protection. It covers designs where the ventilation element is integral to the protective function for medical, industrial or environmental use.
[A45F 2003/127] Dorsal or hip pads for the lumbar back or for the waist -- A45F 2003/127 refers to protective or supportive pads positioned on the dorsal side or hips that are intended to guard the lumbar back or the waist. It covers designs for back-support and injury-prevention devices integrated into clothing or equipment.
[A45F 2003/142] Carrying-straps -- A45F 2003/142 covers carrying-straps, including handles, loops, shoulder straps and similar devices that enable a product to be lifted, suspended or transported by hand or over the body. The classification applies to straps affixed to containers, bags, cases or other articles where the primary function is to facilitate carrying.
[A45F 2003/144] Pack-carrying waist or torso belts -- A45F 2003/144 designates waist or torso belts specifically engineered to carry packs, pouches or similar loads on the body. The classification covers belts that integrate attachment points, straps or compartments for transporting equipment, tools or personal items, and includes variations such as load-bearing harnesses and modular carrying systems.
[A45F 2003/146] Pack-carrying harnesses -- A45F 2003/146 covers pack-carrying harnesses designed to attach, support or secure bags, backpacks, messenger or similar loads to the wearer's body, typically using straps, belts or shoulder assemblies. The classification includes adjustable or ergonomic designs that distribute weight across the torso or hips for comfort and stability.
[A45F 2003/148] Pack-carrying shoulder holsters -- A45F 2003/148 refers to shoulder-mounted holsters intended to
[A45F 2003/163] Water bottles with purification filter -- A45F 2003/163 belongs to the CPC group for containers for liquids, specifically identifying water bottles that incorporate an integrated purification filter. The code is used by the EPO and USPTO to categorize products that combine a drinking vessel with a built-in filtration device for removing contaminants during use.
[A45F 2003/205] Collapsible or foldable cups -- A45F 2003/205 classifies cups that can be reduced in size by collapsing or folding, typically for portable or space-saving use. It includes designs where the cup's walls or base are articulated or deformable, allowing the container to be compacted for storage, transport, or disposal.
[A45F 2004/003] Holders or carriers for hand articles, or for use while travelling or camping, convertible into articles for other use -- A45F 2004/003 covers devices that hold or carry hand-held items such as tools, utensils or personal accessories, especially those intended for travel or camping. The classification also includes carriers that can be converted into other useful articles, enabling multifunctional use of the same item.
[A45F 2004/006] Pack-frames, pack-carrying harnesses, carrying straps or shoulder-pads convertible into articles for other use -- The A45F 2004/006 subclass covers pack-frames, carrying harnesses, straps or shoulder-pads that are designed to be transformed into a different product after use, such as converting a backpack frame into furniture components or other functional items. It includes any load-bearing structure that can be repurposed for a secondary application.
[A45F 2004/023] into articles covered by groups  A45F3/00  -  A45F3/15 -- A45F 2004/023 classifies inventions that are incorporated into articles falling within CPC groups A45F3/00 to A45F3/15, which cover containers for liquids such as bottles, cans and similar vessels, and the associated closure mechanisms like caps, screw-tops and snap-fits.
[A45F 2004/026] into seats or sitting cushions -- A45F 2004/026 covers inventions that are applied to seats or sitting cushions, such as mechanisms, attachments, or modifications that enhance comfort, adjustability, or functionality of the seating surface. It includes integrated heating, ventilation, ergonomic padding and convertible designs applied directly to the cushion or seat structure.
[A45F 2005/002] combined with other objects -- This CPC subgroup covers household or kitchen items that are integrated with additional objects, such as a utensil built into a container or a tool combined with a cleaning device. It falls under A45F (household and kitchen articles) and relates to
[A45F 2005/006] comprising a suspension strap or lanyard -- A45F 2005/006 classifies articles that incorporate a suspension strap or lanyard enabling the item to be hung, carried, or secured to a point. The feature is typically applied to bags, purses, or similar containers to provide hands-free or detachable handling.
[A45F 2005/008] Hand articles fastened to the wrist or to the arm or to the leg -- A45F 2005/008 covers hand articles that are fastened to the wrist, arm or leg, such as gloves, cuffs, braces and similar devices intended to protect, support or ornament the hand while being secured to adjacent body parts.
[A45F 2005/023] to a fastening element of the garment, e.g. button -- A45F 2005/023 covers fastening elements for garments, such as buttons and similar devices that join fabric parts together, including their design, attachment means and functional features. It pertains to the construction and application of these closure components in clothing.
[A45F 2005/025] with a holder or item rotatably connected to the fastening device, e.g. having a rotation axis perpendicular to the garment -- The subclass covers garment fasteners that include a holder or attached item capable of rotating with respect to the fastening element, such as a rotating knob or decorative piece whose rotation axis is perpendicular to the fabric.
[A45F 2005/026] with a pin having an enlarged head detachably connected to a fastening device, e.g. to a pin receiving slot -- The CPC subclass A45F 2005/026 relates to fastening devices that use a detachable pin whose head is enlarged to lock into a receiving slot of the fastener. This arrangement allows the pin to be easily inserted and removed while maintaining a secure connection, typical in garments, upholstery and similar applications. It falls under the broader A45F group for attaching and fastening mechanisms.
[A45F 2005/027] with a horizontal and parallel rotation axis, i.e. the axis being parallel to the surface of the garment -- The entry covers garments that incorporate a rotating element whose axis runs horizontally and parallel to the garment's surface, such as skirts, sleeves or decorative panels that spin around a horizontal line while the wearer is upright. This feature is intended for aesthetic movement or functional positioning of the garment.
[A45F 2005/028] with a vertical rotation axis -- A45F 2005/028 covers chairs, seats or similar furniture that incorporate a component rotating around a vertical axis, such as swivel or rotating mechanisms that allow the seat or backrest to turn horizontally while the support remains fixed. This subclass groups designs where the rotation axis is oriented vertically, enabling users to swivel without changing the overall footprint of the furniture.
[A45F 2005/1006] with means embracing the hand article to be carried -- A45F 2005/1006 covers bags, cases or similar articles that incorporate a built-in hand-gripping feature, such as a handle, loop or strap shaped to embrace the hand for carrying. The classification applies to designs where the hand-holding element is integral to the article, enabling it to be lifted or transported directly by the user's hand.
[A45F 2005/1013] comprising a strap or band -- A45F 2005/1013 covers articles that incorporate a strap or band as a functional element, such as belts, suspenders, strap-on closures or similar components used to secure, adjust or support garments or accessories. It is a subgroup of the CPC class for fastening elements and related clothing features.
[A45F 2005/1033] and supported below the grip surface of the carrying handle -- A45F 2005/1033 pertains to carrying handles that incorporate a support element located beneath the portion of the handle that is gripped. The underlying support may be a reinforcement, pad or structural member designed to increase the handle's strength, stability or user comfort while the item is carried.
[A45F 2005/104] and supported near the lateral ends of the grip surface of the carrying handle -- A45F 2005/104 covers luggage, bags or similar articles whose carrying handle includes a grip surface that is reinforced or supported near its lateral ends, providing added stability and user comfort during transport. This feature is typically achieved by structural elements or brackets positioned at the sides of the handle to prevent deformation and improve grip.
[A45F 2005/1053] Carrying handles intended for receiving parcel cords or package bands -- A45F 2005/1053 refers to carrying handles that are specifically shaped or equipped to receive parcel cords or package bands, enabling the handle to be attached to the cord or band that secures a package during transport. This subclass covers designs where the handle integrates a slot, loop or similar feature for easy connection to the fastening elements used in shipping and handling.
[A45F 2005/106] specially adapted for crossed cords or bands -- A45F 2005/106 covers display or hanging devices that are specially adapted to use crossed cords or bands as the primary support. The classification applies to racks, frames or similar articles where the arrangement of intersecting straps, cords or bands is integral to holding or presenting items such as clothing, accessories or promotional material. This feature is distinct from standard hooks or rods, emphasizing the crossed-cord configuration.
[A45F 2005/1066] Details -- A45F 2005/1066 in the Cooperative Patent Classification, used by the European and United States patent offices, pertains to specific details of sports implements or accessories, particularly those related to golf clubs. This classification covers particular aspects such as grips, heads, or shafts with specialized features.
[A45F 2005/1073] Retaining means for controlled release of the carrying element -- A45F 2005/1073 covers devices that secure a component-such as a dispenser, capsule or other carrying element-while allowing its controlled release. It includes mechanisms like snap-fits, latches, clamps or other retaining structures that hold the element in place until a defined action or condition releases it, often used in packaging and dispensing systems.
[A45F 2005/108] Accessories -- A45F 2005/108 in the Cooperative Patent Classification covers accessories that complement or support tableware, including items such as serving trays, utensil holders, napkin rings and other supplemental components used in dining or kitchen contexts.
[A45F 2005/1086] Storage means for additional items -- A45F 2005/1086 covers storage means incorporated into furniture for holding additional items, such as built-in trays, pockets, drawers, compartments or other receptacles that provide extra space for accessories, utensils, tools or personal effects. It includes designs where the storage element is integrated with the primary furniture piece rather than being a separate, detachable unit.
[A45F 2005/1093] Labels, logos or indicators -- The A45F 2005/1093 subclass covers the application of labels, logos or other visual indicators to objects for identification, branding, safety or functional purposes. It includes printed, embossed, digital, RFID or similar markings on products, packaging, equipment or surfaces that convey information to users or facilitate recognition.
[A45F 2005/125] Mail-carriers -- A45F 2005/125 relates to mail-carrier equipment such as bags, satchels, pouches and other receptacles carried by postal workers for transporting letters and parcels. The classification covers designs that enable hands-free or ergonomic carrying, including attachment methods to belts, straps or backpacks. It also includes features for sorting, protecting or securing mail while in transit.
