[A47C 7/52] of detachable type -- The classification A47C 7/52 covers detachable components, fittings or accessories designed for chairs or stools, such as removable arms, backs, seat pads, footrests, or other interchangeable parts that can be taken off and reattached without permanent alteration of the furniture.
[A47C 7/54] Supports for the arms -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Chairs having non-adjustable support armrests, e.g. movable to inoperative position or of detachable type.
[A47C 7/541] of adjustable type  adjustable arm-rests of reclining or easy chairs  A47C1/03 -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Arm-rests adjustable to multiple use positions.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Adjustable armrests for reclining or easy chairs -> A47C1/03
[A47C 7/543] movable to inoperative position -- This subclass covers chair or stool components that can be moved into a non-functional or locked state, such as folding mechanisms, lockable hinges, or adjustable parts that render the seat unusable when engaged.
[A47C 7/546] of detachable type -- A47C 7/546 classifies detachable components, details or accessories for chairs or stools, such as removable seats, backs, arms or leg assemblies that can be assembled or disassembled without permanent fastening. This subclass falls under furniture - chairs - parts, details, or accessories of chairs or stools.
[A47C 7/56] Parts or details of tipping-up chairs, e.g. of theatre chairs -- **Definition:** This place covers:
[A47C 7/563] provided with a back-rest moving with the seat -- A47C 7/563 covers chairs whose backrest is linked to the seat so that the back moves together with the seat when the seat is tilted or adjusted. It includes mechanisms that synchronize back-rest motion with seat movement, such as recliners, lounge chairs and ergonomic office chairs.
[A47C 7/566] Resiliently mounted seat or back-rest -- A47C 7/566 relates to chairs or stools whose seat or back-rest is attached to a resilient element such as a spring, cushion or flexible support, allowing movement or give under load. The classification covers the mounting mechanism and associated components that provide shock absorption or ergonomic adjustment.
[A47C 7/58] Hinges, e.g. for mounting chairs in a curved row -- A47C 7/58 covers hinges and similar fastening devices that are used to mount individual chairs or stools so they form a continuous curved row, including any specialized pivot or locking mechanisms designed for that purpose.
[A47C 7/60] Use of locks or ledges for limiting the seat movement -- A47C 7/60 pertains to chair or stool components that employ locks, stops or ledges to restrict seat movement, such as mechanisms that lock a recliner at a fixed angle or prevent the seat from sliding. It covers any accessory designed to limit the travel of a chair's seat.
[A47C 7/62] Accessories for chairs -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Accessories for chairs, e.g. hat or coat holders, means for protecting against weather, arm-rest tables, illumination, radios, bars, telephones, ventilation, heating, cooling.
[A47C 7/622] Receptacles, e.g. cup holders, storage containers -- A47C 7/622 covers receptacles incorporated into chairs or stools, such as cup holders, trays, or storage containers, that serve as built-in accessories for holding objects. These elements are considered parts or details of the seating furniture rather than separate items.

These components are classified as accessories of chairs within the furniture group.
[A47C 7/628] accessible by displacement of seat -- A47C 7/628 designates chair or stool components that allow a user to reach the seat by moving it, such as sliding, tilting, folding or lifting mechanisms that create a clear entry path.

[A47C 7/70] of foldable type -- The code A47C 7/70 in the Cooperative Patent Classification designates parts, details or accessories specifically for foldable chairs or stools and is nested under the hierarchy human necessities, furniture, chairs.

[A47C 7/72] Adaptations for incorporating lamps, radio sets, bars, telephones, ventilation, heating or cooling arrangements or the like -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Chairs having accessories, e.g. lamps, radio, bars, telephones, display screens, ventilation, heating or cooling arrangements or the like
[A47C 7/723] with display screens -- The subclass A47C 7/723 covers components or accessories of chairs or stools that incorporate a visual display screen, such as integrated seat or back-rest panels that show information, entertainment or control interfaces, rather than a separate device. It applies to any chair part where the screen is built into the element itself.
[A47C 7/725] for illumination, e.g. lamps -- A47C 7/725 is a Cooperative Patent Classification for accessories that provide illumination to chairs or stools, such as built-in lamps or lighting attachments. [A47C 7/727] with speakers -- A47C 7/727 classifies chairs or stools that incorporate built-in speakers as part of the seat, back or armrest, or as an attached accessory. It covers designs where audio playback devices are integrated into the furniture for personal or public listening.
[A47C 7/74] for ventilation, heating or cooling -- A47C 7/74 in the Cooperative Patent Classification covers specific features of chairs and stools designed for ventilation, heating, or cooling, enhancing user comfort through temperature regulation.
[A47C 7/742] for ventilating or cooling  A47C31/116  takes precedence -- A47C 7/742 covers components of chairs or stools that are designed to provide ventilation or cooling, such as mesh backs, perforated seats, or integrated fans. These parts are classified as accessories that improve airflow to the sitter.
[A47C 7/744] with active means, e.g. by using air blowers or liquid pumps -- This subclass covers chairs or stools equipped with active mechanisms such as built-in air blowers, liquid pumps or other means that provide movement, heating, cooling or other dynamic functions. It includes integrated devices that actively modify the seat or backrest environment.
[A47C 7/746] without active means, e.g. with openings or heat conductors -- A47C 7/746 covers passive components or accessories for chairs or stools, such as ventilation openings, heat-conducting elements or other non-powered fittings. It includes parts that do not contain active means like motors or electronics, focusing on structural or thermal features that enhance comfort or function without requiring energy input.
[A47C 7/748] for heating -- A47C 7/748 covers parts, details or accessories of chairs or stools that provide heating. It includes built-in heaters, electric pads, warm-air ducts or any other means intended to keep the seat or backrest warm for user comfort. This sub-class falls under the broader groups of furniture and chairs in the Cooperative Patent Classification system.

[A47D] FURNITURE SPECIALLY ADAPTED FOR CHILDREN -- **Definition:** This place covers:  All domestic furniture (including baby-carriers and baby-cots) structurally adapted to be used by children, if not provided in other part of the classification.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - School forms; Benches or forms combined with desks -> A47B39/00 - School desks or tables -> A47B41/00
[A47D 1/00] Children's chairs -- A47D 1/00 classifies children's chairs, a sub-group of furniture specially adapted for children within the broader human necessities category. It covers chairs designed for youngsters, including various sizes, safety features and ergonomic adaptations.
[A47D 1/002] adjustable -- This CPC subclass A47D 1/002 defines adjustable children's chairs, i.e. seats specially adapted for kids that incorporate mechanisms to vary seat height, backrest angle, or other dimensions to accommodate growth or different users.
[A47D 1/004] in height -- This subgroup covers children's chairs where the seat height is a defining characteristic, including chairs designed with a specific fixed height or with an adjustable height mechanism to suit child users.
[A47D 1/006] dismountable -- A47D 1/006 covers children's chairs that are designed to be taken apart or easily separated for storage, transport or re-assembly. The classification applies to furniture specially adapted for children where the seat, backrest or legs can be disassembled without tools or with minimal effort. It falls under the hierarchy of Human Necessities → Furniture → Furniture specially adapted for children.
[A47D 1/008] with trays  children's chairs combined with tables  A47D3/00 -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Subject matter including a chair and an attached tray. The tray is an element having a horizontal surface (at least in an operative or use position) configured to support articles on top of it. In general, the tray is relatively small (a table encircling a chair is not considered a tray; see the references below) so that the supporting structure of the chair and tray is not substantially larger than required to support a chair alone. The tray is attached to only a single chair.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Children's chairs combined with tables -> A47D3/00
[A47D 1/0081] adjustable -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Subject matter in which the tray can be moved between and fixed in a plurality of use positions relative to the chair seat without the use of tools.
[A47D 1/0083] movable to inoperative position -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Subject matter in which the tray is movable to a position in which it is not usable, e.g. due to its normally horizontal surface lying in a vertical plane, while remaining attached to the chair.
[A47D 1/0085] removable -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Subject matter in which the tray is constructed so as to allow separation of the tray from the chair without destroying either one.
[A47D 1/02] Foldable chairs -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Subject matter in which the tray is movable to a position in which it is not usable, e.g. due to its normally horizontal surface lying in a vertical plane, while remaining attached to the chair.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Foldable rocking-chairs -> A47D13/101

[A47D 9/053] with curved rocking member -- A47D 9/053 covers cradles that incorporate a rocking member shaped with a continuous curve, providing a gentle back-and-forth motion. The curved element may be built into the frame or added as a separate component, distinguishing these designs from those using straight or angular rocking mechanisms.
[A47D 9/057] driven by electric motors -- A47D 9/057 designates cradles that are powered by electric motors, falling within the CPC group for furniture specially adapted for children. It is a subclass of A47D 9 covering motor-driven rocking or swinging devices used as
[A47F] SPECIAL FURNITURE, FITTINGS, OR ACCESSORIES FOR SHOPS, STOREHOUSES, BARS, RESTAURANTS OR THE LIKE -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Furniture for use in the commercial area, i.e. special furniture, fittings or accessories for shops, storehouses, bars, restaurants, museums or the like.  This subclass also relates to the furniture aspects of paying counters.  Racks and containers for displaying and dispensing merchandise in shops, restaurants or other such catering establishments;  Show cases or show cabinets;  Refrigerated or air-conditioned show cases, cabinets or counters for use in shops, restaurants or the like;  Display trays, tables or containers;  Show stands, racks, shelves, shelving units and accessories therefor;  Show stands, racks or shelves adapted for particular articles or materials;  Mannequins, dummies or busts;  Shop, bar, bank or like counters;  Paying counters;  Check-out counters of self-service shops;  Self-service shop type system furniture;  Restaurant service system furniture;  Arrangements in shop windows;  Shop arrangements bringing about special optical effects;  Shop accessories for handling merchandise.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Visual display units, as in computer or television screens -> G06F , H04N - Vending machines dispensing articles -> G07F - Display of information, advertising -> G09F - Server racks, racks for electronic equipment -> H05K  **Glossary:** - Display: The act of displaying to view or notice (Oxford English Dictionary); Used together with a term referring to products, merchandise or a device it is meant to be understood as a device for physically, as opposed to virtually or representatively, displaying items of merchandise in situ. - Rack: A framework in or on which articles may be placed or suspended.Gondola: An island counter used in self-service shops for the display of merchandise (OED)
[A47F 1/00] Racks for dispensing merchandise -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Racks and containers for displaying and dispensing merchandise in shops, restaurants or other catering establishments. Display containers are considered to be containers which are filled with merchandise on the site of display.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Dispensing or devices for the dispensing of liquids -> B67D - Vending machines, i.e. mostly coin-freed machines dispensing products (automatically) upon input of coins or tokens -> G07F
[A47F 10/00] Furniture or installations specially adapted to particular types of service systems, not otherwise provided for -- A47F 10/00 covers furniture or installations that are specially designed for specific service systems in commercial settings such as shops, bars, restaurants or similar venues, where the items are not covered by any other more specific classification. It includes custom-fit fittings and accessories that support particular operational needs of these establishments.

These mechanisms are part of special furniture and fittings used in shops, supermarkets, and similar service premises.

This category covers items adapted to restaurant operations that are not covered by broader furniture classes.

The classification covers rotating, sliding, tilting or other means that release individual units or portions to customers.
[A47F 1/035] having measuring devices -- A47F 1/035 covers racks for dispensing merchandise in shops, bars, restaurants or similar venues that incorporate built-in measuring devices to weigh or otherwise quantify the product being sold.

It includes systems that release items individually or in measured amounts for retail environments like stores, bars or restaurants.
[A47F 1/06] dispensing from top -- A47F 1/06 covers racks designed to dispense items from the top, such as elevated shelves or bins that allow customers to pull or slide products down for sale, commonly used in shops, bars, restaurants and similar retail settings.

It falls within the broader A47F class for lighting fixtures and their supporting structures. This code is used by the EPO and USPTO to index patents related to such lamp-related hardware.

[A47F 2007/195] Virtual display of clothes on the wearer by means of a mirror, screen or the like -- A47F 2007/195 relates to systems that project a visual representation of clothing onto the wearer by means of a mirror, screen or similar device, enabling the wearer to see how garments would look without physically trying them on. It includes augmented-reality mirrors, digital fitting rooms and other virtual-display technologies for apparel.
[A47F 2009/041] Accessories for check-out counters, e.g. dividers -- A47F 2009/041 covers accessories used at retail checkout stations, specifically items such as dividers, trays or barriers that separate merchandise, cash registers or customer spaces. [A47F 2009/044] dispensing from a roll -- A47F 2009/044 relates to devices that dispense material from a roll, such as paper, fabric or sheet stock, by unwinding the roll and delivering the material for use in a controlled manner.
[A47F 2010/005] using RFID elements -- The CPC subclass A47F 2010/005 covers the integration of RFID (radio-frequency identification) components into furniture, household items, or related articles for purposes such as identification, tracking, inventory control or automated interaction with the objects. It includes designs where RFID tags, chips or antennas are built into or attached to the product to enable wireless communication of data about the item.
[A47F 2010/025] using stock management systems -- A47F 2010/025 covers the use of stock-management systems in food- or drink-dispensing equipment, such as vending machines, cafeterias and kiosks. It includes sensors, data acquisition and software that monitor inventory levels, predict replenishment needs and optimise supply chains to minimise shortages and waste. The classification applies to any technical solution that integrates inventory control with the dispensing device's operation.
[A47F 2010/065] Food shields;  sneeze guards -- A47F 2010/065 relates to protective barriers used in food service environments to shield food from contamination. It includes transparent sneeze guards, desk shields and other panels positioned over counters, buffet lines or self-service stations to prevent droplets, dust or insects from reaching the food.

[A47F 9/046] Arrangement of recording means in or on check-out counters  record carriers or readers  per se   G06K -- **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Record carriers or (bar-code) readers per se -> G06K
[A47F 9/047] for recording self-service articles without cashier or assistant -- A47F 9/047 pertains to counters in shops, bars, banks or similar premises that are designed for self-service, allowing customers to obtain articles without a cashier or attendant. The code covers the integrated features that record transactions automatically on such specialised counters.
[A47F 9/048] automatically -- The Cooperative Patent Classification code A47F 9/048 pertains to shop, bar, bank, or similar counters with automatic features. This classification is used for inventions involving automated systems or mechanisms designed to enhance the functionality of such counters in commercial settings.
[A47G] HOUSEHOLD OR TABLE EQUIPMENT -- **Definition:** This place covers:  A47G relates to household or hotel equipment:
[A47G 1/00] Mirrors -- A47G 1/00 pertains to mirrors employed in household or table equipment, encompassing devices for personal grooming, decorative or functional purposes, and those fixed to walls, cabinets, furniture or designed as portable units. It covers all types of mirrors used in domestic settings, including bathroom, dressing-table and decorative mirrors. This subclass falls under Furniture → Household or Table Equipment within the Human Necessities section.
[A47G 1/02] Mirrors used as equipment -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Mirrors for home use e.g. to comb hair or to dress, bathroom mirrors, decorative mirrors, etc.
[A47G 1/04] Multi-part mirrors -- A47G 1/04 covers mirrors that consist of two or more separate reflective sections combined into a single unit, such as hinged, folding or stacked panels used on walls, cabinets or dressing tables. The classification is applied to household or table equipment where the mirror's parts can be moved, adjusted or assembled to provide variable viewing angles or compact storage.
[A47G 1/06] Picture frames -- The A47G 1/06 subclass covers picture frames, i.e., devices used to mount, protect and display pictures, photographs, prints, artworks or similar flat items. It includes frames of various shapes, materials and decorative features intended for household or table use, and may also address methods of securing the image within the frame.
[A47G 1/0605] made from extruded or moulded profiles, e.g. of plastic or metal -- A47G 1/0605 classifies mirrors that are constructed from extruded or moulded profiles, such as those formed from plastic or metal, and intended for use as household or table equipment within the furniture category.
[A47G 1/0611] the profiles having clamping action; Elongated clips -- This subgroup covers mirror-holding profiles that secure the glass by a clamping action, including elongated clip members used to mount or adjust mirrors within furniture or household equipment.

[A47G 1/0622] with illumination -- A47G 1/0622 covers mirrors that incorporate built-in lighting, such as vanity or bathroom mirrors with integrated lamps, LEDs or other illumination devices designed to provide direct light on the reflecting surface. This subclass includes designs where the light source is permanently attached to the mirror frame or backing for functional or decorative purposes.
[A47G 1/0627] with decorative strips or layers on the frame members -- A47G 1/0627 designates mirrors whose frames are finished with decorative strips or layered coatings applied to the frame members, providing an ornamental surface that enhances the mirror's appearance. This feature is considered a specific aesthetic element within household or table equipment.
[A47G 1/0633] made of sheet material -- The subclass A47G 1/0633 covers mirrors that are produced from sheet material such as glass, plastic, metal or composite sheets, intended for use as household or table equipment. It defines the technical features of sheet-based mirror constructions within the broader furniture and human necessities categories.
[A47G 1/0638] Frameless picture holders -- A47G 1/0638 covers devices that support or display pictures without enclosing them in frames, such as picture rails, hooks, adhesive strips, clips or stands designed for hanging or presenting artworks and photographs on walls or other surfaces. These items are intended for household or table use where the image is shown without a surrounding frame.
[A47G 1/0644] using a tension wire for holding the members or corner pieces together -- A47G 1/0644 covers a method of securing mirror frames by applying a tensioned wire that binds the structural members or corner pieces together, enhancing rigidity and alignment. This technique is classified under household or table equipment mirrors within the broader category of furniture.

[A47G 1/0655] with anti-theft means -- Mirrors equipped with anti-theft means are designed to prevent unauthorized removal or tampering by incorporating locking devices, alarm systems, sensors or other security features. The classification covers both decorative and functional mirrors in household or table equipment that include such protective mechanisms.

[A47G 1/10] Corner clips or corner-connecting appliances for frames -- A47G 1/10 covers devices that join or reinforce the corners of frames, such as clips, brackets or other corner-connecting appliances used in furniture and mirror frames. [A47G 11/00] Table linen -- A47G 11/00 covers table linen, which includes textiles such as tablecloths, napkins, placemats and other fabric coverings designed to protect or decorate tabletop surfaces in household or table equipment. This subclass falls under the hierarchy of human necessities, furniture, and specifically household or table equipment.
[A47G 11/001] Table napkins -- A47G 11/001 designates table napkins, the cloth or paper pieces placed on a dining table to protect the surface and absorb spills. The code falls under table-linen in the household or table equipment subgroup of the furniture class within human necessities.

It is part of the household or table equipment group within the furniture category.
[A47G 11/003] Table-cloths -- A47G 11/003 covers table-cloths, fabric or other sheet materials placed on a table surface for protection, hygiene or decoration. The code is part of the CPC hierarchy for household or table equipment within furniture, specifically the table-linen subgroup.

The code falls under Human Necessities - Furniture - Household or Table Equipment - Table linen, and it groups with related items for maintaining a neatly fitted table covering.
[A47G 11/006] Multi-part table-cloths, e.g. comprising separate skirting -- A47G 11/006 covers multi-part table-cloths in which the covering is made of separate sections, such as a main top piece combined with an attached or detachable skirting that hangs over the table edges. The classification includes designs where the skirting can be removed, replaced, or adjusted independently of the central fabric, providing a modular or decorative solution for protecting table surfaces.

[A47G 1/1606] comprising a wall member cooperating with a corresponding picture member -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Devices comprising a member to be fixed to a wall or the like surface and a member to be fixed to a picture, the shape of each member is specially adapted to the shape of the other, e.g. a male and a female member, hook-and-loop fastener. "Specially adapted" is to be interpreted as differing from the conventional wall fasteners as nails, screws, etc. and the conventional picture fasteners as suspension cords, loops, eyes etc. The picture member might be an integral part of the picture (frame).
[A47G 1/1613] and being adjustable -- **Definition:** This place covers:  "Adjustable" refers to the devices, not the pictures, mirrors, or the like.
[A47G 1/162] Picture members for connection to a conventional wall hook or nail -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Members to be fixed to a picture for connecting to a conventional wall fastener such as a nail, screw or the like. The member might be integrated in the picture (frame). A suspension cord and its arrangement on the back of a picture is not considered to be such a member. For example: picture loops made of stiff wire, eye bolts, ring-shaped fastenings (metal wire loops).
[A47G 1/1626] and being adjustable -- **Definition:** This place covers:  "Adjustable" refers to the picture member, not the picture.

[A47G 2001/002] comprising magnifying properties -- A47G 2001/002 covers tables that incorporate magnifying elements, such as built-in lenses or reflective surfaces that enlarge objects placed on the work surface. The classification is used for furniture designed to aid reading, inspection or detailed work by providing integrated magnification.
[A47G 2001/005] Frames made from flexible material, e.g. rubber -- The CPC subclass A47G 2001/005 covers frames that are formed from flexible materials such as rubber, elastomers or similar polymers. These frames are intended to provide support, containment or shaping of objects in furniture, transport, protective or related applications, where the flexibility of the material contributes to the function.
[A47G 2001/007] incorporating automatically moving parts -- A47G 2001/007 covers furniture and domestic articles that incorporate automatically moving parts, such as chairs with self-adjusting height, tables with motorised extensions, or beds with built-in lifting mechanisms. The code applies to designs where the movement is integral to the item's function.
[A47G 2001/0661] having means for holding paintings painted on canvas -- A47G 2001/0661 covers devices that support or secure canvas paintings, such as easels, frames, or mounting systems, enabling display, transport, or storage of artworks. The classification is applied to inventions that provide means for holding painted canvases in a stable, adjustable, or portable manner.
[A47G 2001/0666] Frontloading picture frames -- A47G 2001/0666 covers picture frames that are loaded from the front, typically featuring a hinged, sliding or removable front panel that lets the artwork be inserted or removed without taking the frame apart. The design simplifies changing displays in homes, galleries or commercial settings while protecting the picture and backing.
[A47G 2001/0672] employing magnets -- A47G 2001/0672 covers furniture, domestic articles or related devices that incorporate magnets for functional purposes such as fastening, holding, adjusting, or enabling magnetic attachment or levitation, for example magnetic hinges, closures or support systems in household furnishings.
[A47G 2001/0677] having means for fixing the picture or backing to the frame, e.g. clips, nails or the like -- A47G 2001/0677 covers picture-frame designs that incorporate means for attaching the picture or its backing directly to the frame, such as built-in clips, nails, screws or similar fasteners.
[A47G 2001/0683] having means for holding flowers -- A47G 2001/0683 refers to inventions that incorporate a built-in mechanism for holding flowers, such as vases, flower holders, or integrated flower-support features in furniture or domestic accessories. The classification is applied to patents describing any device where the primary function includes securing or displaying floral arrangements.
[A47G 2001/0688] where the picture is inserted through a slit in one of the frame members -- A47G covers picture-frame designs, and the subgroup 2001/0688 specifies frames in which the picture is introduced through a slit cut in one of the frame members, allowing the image to be inserted without dismantling the frame.
[A47G 2001/0694] comprising means allowing the frame to be supported on a horizontal surface -- A47G 2001/0694 classifies furniture frames that incorporate elements-such as legs, feet, brackets or other supporting features-enabling the frame to be placed and remain stable on a horizontal surface. This entry is applied to items like tables, chairs, or storage units where the support means are integral to the frame's design.
[A47G 2001/145] Picture cubes -- A47G covers toys and amusements, and the sub-group 2001/145 refers specifically to picture cubes - small cubic blocks whose faces are printed with sections of an image, designed to be assembled so that the combined cubes display a complete picture, often used as puzzles, educational toys or decorative items.
[A47G 2001/146] comprising means for adjusting the inclination of the stand -- A47G 2001/146 relates to furniture elements that provide a means for varying the angle of a stand, such as a chair back, table support or similar component. The entry includes mechanisms like levers, springs, gears or locking devices that enable the user to adjust the inclination to a preferred position.
[A47G 2001/147] Photograph stands with illumination means -- A47G 2001/147 covers devices that support photographic prints or pictures while providing built-in illumination for display. It includes lighted picture frames, illuminated photo stands and other display units that combine a holder or stand with an integrated light source.
[A47G 2001/148] adjustable to accommodate frames or pictures of varying sizes -- A47G 2001/148 covers mounting or display hardware that can be altered to hold frames or pictures of different dimensions, such as adjustable brackets, rails, hooks or similar fittings. The element is designed to accommodate varying sizes without requiring separate installations for each frame.
[A47G 2001/1673] having illumination means -- A47G 2001/1673 covers furniture or related articles that incorporate built-in lighting, such as tables, desks, or workstations equipped with integrated illumination means for functional or decorative purposes.
[A47G 2001/1693] Hand-tools for lifting picture frames onto hooks -- A47G 2001/1693 covers hand-operated tools specifically designed to lift picture frames and hook them onto hanging hardware such as nails or picture-hanging hooks. The classification groups devices used in installing or repositioning frames on walls, typical of interior-decorating and furniture-assembly practices. It falls under the broader CPC section A47G for furniture and related equipment.
[A47G 2001/207] Safety hooks preventing accidental dislodging of the picture -- A47G 2001/207 relates to safety hooks or similar fittings that secure pictures, frames or comparable decorative items to walls, panels or furniture, preventing accidental dislodgement. The classification covers designs that lock the item in place yet allow intentional removal, and is used for interior-furniture applications where picture stability is required.
[A47G 2007/003] Hand tools for lifting or positioning individual flower pots -- A47G 2007/003 belongs to the CPC gardening section and covers hand-operated devices used to lift or position single flower pots. It includes tools such as pot lifters, gripping handles and small carriers that enable safe, ergonomic handling of individual containers.
[A47G 2007/048] Flower tables, stands or hangers with means for automatically rotating the plant pot -- Devices for holding potted plants, such as tables, stands or hanging systems, that include a motorized or mechanical mechanism to rotate the pot automatically, typically to ensure uniform lighting, growth or watering.
[A47G 2007/066] adaptable for flowers of differing heights, e.g. telescopic flower vases -- The CPC subclass A47G 2007/066 covers containers for displaying flowers that can be lengthened or shortened to accommodate stems of different heights, typically telescoping vases. It includes mechanisms that allow the vase to be adjusted during arrangement.
[A47G 2009/001] Anti-allergen;  Anti-mite -- A47G 2009/001 covers inventions that reduce or neutralise allergens, especially those produced by house dust mites, in domestic settings. It includes treated textiles, cleaning agents, traps, and other methods or apparatuses designed to diminish mite-related allergen exposure.
[A47G 2009/002] Bedding to assist in intercourse -- A47G 2009/002 covers bedding products specifically designed to facilitate sexual activity, including mattresses, cushions, pillows, and other support items that enhance comfort, positioning, or stimulation during intercourse. The classification encompasses accessories that are integrated into the bed or used separately to improve ergonomic or pleasurable aspects of intimate encounters.
[A47G 2009/003] with inflatable members -- A47G 2009/003 covers furniture or domestic articles that incorporate inflatable members, such as air-filled cushions, mattresses, chairs, or other seating and sleeping devices that rely on gas or air chambers for support or comfort. This subclass groups inventions where the primary functional element is an inflatable component integrated into the product.
[A47G 2009/004] comprising magnets -- A47G 2009/004 relates to kitchen or tableware articles that incorporate magnets as a functional element, such as magnetic closures, fasteners, holders or mounting devices used in food preparation, storage or serving. The classification covers products where the magnet is integral to the item's operation rather than merely decorative
[A47G 2009/005] illuminated -- A47G 2009/005 pertains to furniture or domestic articles that incorporate built-in lighting, such as illuminated tables, cabinets, shelves, wardrobes or similar items where the illumination is an integral part of the piece rather than a separate lamp. The classification covers the design and arrangement of the lighting elements within the furniture.
[A47G 2009/006] comprising sound equipment -- A47G 2009/006 covers food, beverage or tobacco dispensing machines that incorporate sound equipment, such as speakers, alarms or music playback devices. The classification is used for vending or dispensing units where audio functions are integral to the apparatus, for example machines that play advertisements, music or audible instructions while dispensing products.
[A47G 2009/008] using a liquid as filling material -- A47G 2009/008 relates to furniture or domestic articles whose interior is filled with a liquid, such as water-filled cushions, gel-filled mattresses, or other liquid-based padding. It groups patents dealing with the integration of audio functions into items like tables, chairs, cabinets or other domestic furnishings.

These are specialized household or table equipment adapted for a specific foodstuff.

[A47G 2400/062] for cereals or other foods where liquid and non-liquid elements should be kept separate until eaten -- A47G 2400/062 covers household or table equipment designed to hold cereal or similar foods in which the liquid component - such as milk or juice - and the solid component are stored separately and only combined at the point of consumption, including bowls, containers or dishes that keep the two elements apart until they are mixed.
[A47G 2400/064] for seafood -- A47G 2400/064 pertains to household or table equipment expressly designed for handling, serving or storing seafood, including fish plates, shellfish platters and specialized utensils.

It includes plates, bowls, trays, containers and utensils intended for pasta or noodle dishes.
[A47G 2400/068] for soup -- A47G 2400/068 designates tableware or kitchen equipment specifically intended for soup, including soup bowls, tureens, ladles and related serving items. It falls under the CPC hierarchy of Human necessities → Furniture → Household or table equipment. This subclass groups products used to prepare, serve or consume soup.
[A47G 2400/08] Stabilizing containers or articles -- A47G 2400/08 relates to features that keep containers, dishes, bottles or similar articles steady while in use, such as anti-tip devices, retaining nets, straps, brackets or other supporting structures integrated with furniture or household equipment. It covers designs that prevent wobbling, spilling or displacement of objects on tables, countertops or other surfaces.
[A47G 2400/083] Bean bag -- A bean bag is a flexible, fabric-covered seat filled with lightweight beads, foam or similar material, used as casual seating or lounging furniture. It falls under household or table equipment in the Furniture subclass of the CPC, designated by code A47G 2400/083, which groups items of soft, adaptable seating intended for domestic environments.
[A47G 2400/086] Self-levelling -- A self-levelling device for household or table equipment automatically adjusts the support or legs of furniture such as tables, desks or workstations to keep the surface horizontal despite uneven flooring or load changes. The mechanism typically uses springs, pneumatic or hydraulic elements, or motor-driven actuators to detect tilt and correct it, improving stability and usability. It falls under CPC A47G 2400/086 within the furniture category.
[A47G 2400/10] Articles made from a particular material -- A47G 2400/10 designates household or table equipment whose principal feature is that it is made from a specific material, such as wood, metal, plastic or glass, when the material itself defines the article and no more detailed subclass applies. It groups together such furniture items that are otherwise not covered by more specific material-based categories.
[A47G 2400/105] Edible material -- A47G 2400/105 pertains to edible material associated with household or table equipment, covering food items placed on or incorporated into furniture and other domestic furnishings.
[A47G 2400/12] Safety aspects -- A47G 2400/12 covers safety-related features and measures applied to household or table equipment such as furniture, addressing aspects like stability, protection from injury, fire resistance, child-proofing or ergonomic safeguards, where such details are not specifically covered by other subclasses.
[A47G 2400/123] Anti-spiking -- A47G 2400/123 pertains to anti-spiking features for household or table equipment, such as tables, desks or related furnishings, that incorporate designs or mechanisms to prevent knives, forks or other pointed objects from penetrating or damaging the surface. This classification groups inventions that address safety or durability by inhibiting spiking without specifying further technical details.

This subclass groups inventions that protect such items from unauthorized removal or tampering.

[A47G 25/74] Necktie holders -- A47G 25/74 covers devices for storing or displaying neckties, such as tie racks, hooks, bars or revolving organizers, usually mounted on walls, closets or furniture. These items are classified as dwelling equipment within the household or table equipment group.
[A47G 25/743] of the clothes hanger-type -- This subgroup covers furniture items of the clothes-hanger type, such as racks, hooks or supports designed for hanging garments in a dwelling. It includes wall-mounted, free-standing or ceiling-suspended units used in homes.
[A47G 25/746] mounted on wall, ceiling or the like -- A47G 25/746 covers dwelling equipment that is attached to or suspended from walls, ceilings or similar structures, such as wall-mounted lamps, shelves, hooks, brackets and other fixtures designed to be fixed in place rather than freestanding. It pertains to furniture or household accessories installed directly on vertical or overhead surfaces in a residential setting.
[A47G 25/76] Necktie-stretching devices -- A47G 25/76 covers devices designed to stretch and shape neckties, typically used in homes to keep ties straight and prevent creasing. These accessories fall under dwelling equipment within the broader categories of furniture and household or table equipment.
[A47G 25/78] Necktie-pressing devices, e.g. combined with holding devices -- A47G 25/78 pertains to necktie-pressing devices, including those that incorporate tie-holding features, designed for use in homes or closets to flatten and maintain the shape of ties. These appliances fall under household equipment for personal grooming.

[A47G 25/82] Shoe horns -- A shoe horn is a simple hand-held implement, typically made of plastic, wood or metal, that assists a person in sliding a shoe onto the foot by providing a smooth, tapered surface. It is classified under household equipment for dwelling use, reflecting its role in daily personal grooming.
[A47G 25/84] Shoe benches -- A47G 25/84 designates shoe benches, a type of dwelling equipment classified under household or table furniture. These are low seating units intended for use while putting on or removing footwear, often incorporating storage compartments for shoes or accessories. They are designed for residential settings to combine seating and organization functions.
[A47G 25/845] specially adapted for mounting to a wall -- A47G 25/845 designates dwelling equipment that is a form of household or table furniture specially adapted for mounting to a wall, such as wall-mounted cabinets, shelves, or other built-in fixtures. It falls within the CPC hierarchy under HUMAN NECESSITIES → FURNITURE → HOUSEHOLD OR TABLE EQUIPMENT → Dwelling equipment.
[A47G 25/86] with boot-pulling devices -- A47G 25/86 in the Cooperative Patent Classification, used by the European and United States patent offices, categorizes household furniture or dwelling equipment specifically featuring boot-pulling devices. This code is part of a broader hierarchy that includes human necessities, furniture, and general household or table equipment.
[A47G 25/88] Devices for tucking ends of laces inside shoes or boots -- A47G 25/88 pertains to devices that tuck the ends of shoe or boot laces inside the footwear, creating a neater appearance and preventing the lace tips from catching or protruding. These accessories are classified under household equipment for footwear within the broader categories of furniture and human necessities.
[A47G 25/90] Devices for domestic use for assisting in putting-on or pulling-off clothing, e.g. stockings or trousers -- A47G 25/90 covers domestic assistive devices for putting on or taking off clothing such as stockings or trousers. These items fall under household or table equipment within the broader categories of furniture and dwelling equipment.

[A47G 27/025] Modular rugs -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Rugs (non wall-to-wall carpets) comprised of carpet sections, optionally a surrounding frame or frame sections, and optionally a ground sheet.

It pertains to the manufacture and finishing of floor fabric products within the household furniture sector.

The classification applies to textile or composite floor fabrics designed for easy assembly, replacement or reconfiguration in residential or commercial settings.
[A47G 27/04] Carpet fasteners -- Carpet fasteners are devices or methods used to attach carpet or floor coverings to a substrate, including tacks, staples, nails, adhesive strips or mechanical clips that hold the fabric in place on floors, walls or furniture. They are employed in household or table equipment to secure floor fabrics.

This classification is part of the Cooperative Patent Classification system used by the European Patent Office and the United States Patent and Trademark Office.
[A47G 33/0881] comprising means related to watering of the tree -- A47G 33/0881 covers devices incorporated in religious or ritual household items that provide means for watering a tree, such as built-in watering systems for sacred or ceremonial trees used within a dwelling.
[A47G 33/10] Fastening devices or clamps for Christmas tree decorations -- A47G 33/10 refers to fastening devices or clamps designed specifically for securing Christmas-tree decorations, such as ornaments, lights or garlands, to the tree. These items fall under household equipment used for religious or ritual purposes in the home.
[A47G 33/105] Fastening means for Christmas tree candle holders -- A47G 33/105 pertains to fastening devices designed to hold candle holders on Christmas trees, such as clips, brackets or ties that attach the holders securely to branches. These items fall under household equipment used for religious or ritual celebrations.
[A47G 33/12] Christmas tree stands -- The CPC entry A47G 33/12 classifies Christmas-tree stands, which are household or table equipment used to support a Christmas tree, a ritual or decorative object commonly placed in homes during the holiday season. It falls under the hierarchy of Human Necessities ► Furniture ► Household or Table Equipment ► Religious or ritual equipment in dwelling or for general use.
[A47G 33/1206] of self-clamping type -- A47G 33/1206 pertains to self-clamping mechanisms incorporated in religious or ritual equipment for domestic use, such as self-tightening holders for candles, incense burners or similar ceremonial items.
[A47G 33/1213] comprising a single handle or pedal for releasing, tensioning or locking of clamping members -- A47G 33/1213 classifies religious or ritual household items that incorporate a single handle or pedal used to release, tension or lock a clamping mechanism. It falls within the furniture group for household or table equipment, covering devices where a lone actuator controls the fastening element.
[A47G 33/1226] comprising a ball joint or similar fulcrum surface for straightening the tree -- A47G 33/1226 relates to household or table equipment used for religious or ritual purposes that incorporates a ball-joint or comparable fulcrum surface to enable straightening or adjusting a ceremonial tree. This classification covers items designed to pivot or align the tree as part of domestic ritual practice.
[A47G 33/1246] supported on a surface other than a floor, e.g. wall, ceiling, or the like -- This CPC subgroup A47G 33/1246 covers religious or ritual items used in a home that are mounted or otherwise supported on a surface other than a floor, such as a wall or ceiling. It falls within the hierarchy of human necessities → furniture → household or table equipment.
[A47G 33/126] allowing the tree to rotate or vibrate thereon -- A47G 33/126 covers devices or arrangements that permit a tree-typically a decorative or ceremonial tree used in domestic religious or ritual settings-to be rotated or made to vibrate. It falls within household or table equipment classified as ritual furnishings, enabling movement of the tree for symbolic or functional purposes such as enhancing visual effect or facilitating ritual actions.
[A47G 35/00] Other dwelling equipment -- A47G 35/00 covers equipment installed in residential dwellings that is not classified elsewhere, such as built-in storage, laundry installations, heating or ventilation units, and other permanent or semi-permanent household fittings. It falls under the broader categories of Human Necessities, Furniture and Household or Table Equipment.

[A47G 5/04] Fire screens -- A47G 5/04 covers fire screens, protective devices placed in front of a fireplace or heating source to shield surrounding areas from heat and sparks. These screens are typically made of metal or mesh and may be decorative as well as functional, fitting within the broader category of household furniture and table equipment.
[A47G 7/00] Flower holders or the like -- A47G 7/00 pertains to devices that support or display cut flowers, bouquets or stems, such as vases, pots and other ornamental containers used for decorative purposes in homes, offices or public spaces. It includes any holder designed to arrange or present floral arrangements, whether freestanding, wall-mounted or integrated into furniture.
[A47G 7/006] Transparent holders for flowers, e.g. globes for artificial flowers -- A47G 7/006 covers transparent containers designed to hold flowers, such as glass or plastic globes used for displaying artificial blossoms. These items fall under furniture, specifically household or table equipment classified as flower holders or the like.
[A47G 7/02] Devices for supporting flower-pots or cut flowers -- A47G 7/02 covers accessories designed to hold or display flower pots, cut flowers or similar arrangements in a domestic setting. It includes stands, brackets, holders and other devices that keep plants upright on tables, shelves or other household furniture. The classification is part of the CPC section for furniture, specifically household or table equipment used for decorative plant presentation.
[A47G 7/025] Flower-pot stabilisers, i.e. means to prevent flower-pots from tipping over -- A47G 7/025 covers devices that stabilise flower-pots to keep them from tipping, such as weighted bases, anchoring brackets or other support mechanisms for indoor or outdoor plant containers.
[A47G 7/03] Needle cushions or needle plates for supporting flowers in pots or vases -- A47G 7/03 falls under Human Necessities → Furniture → Household or Table Equipment → Flower holders or the like, and it covers needle cushions or needle plates designed to support flower stems in pots or vases. This subclass groups accessories that keep blooms upright and stable within containers.

The code is subordinate to A47G 7/042 and A47G 7/045, which have priority over it.

[A47H] FURNISHINGS FOR WINDOWS OR DOORS -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Devices for each of support, operation, and installation of curtains; curtain structures.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Concerned with the functioning of the door or window -> E05 - Roller blinds -> E06B
[A47H 1/00] Curtains -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Curtain suspension devices, such as curtain rails and curtain rods. It also covers means for mounting them, such as adjustable mountings and brackets. Next to rails and rods it also covers other curtain suspension devices, such as wire and cord.
[A47H 1/02] Curtain rods -- A47H 1/02 relates to curtain rods, the supporting members used to hang and guide curtains, drapes or blinds on walls, ceilings or within window frames. The subclass covers the design, mounting and adjustment features of such rods.
[A47H 1/022] extensible -- A47H 1/022 classifies curtains that are extensible, meaning they can be lengthened or shortened to fit varying window or door openings. This includes telescopic, pleated-track or other adjustable-length curtain systems designed to adapt to different dimensions. The code falls under the hierarchy of Human Necessities → Furniture → Furnishings for Windows or Doors.
[A47H 1/03] pivotably mounted for rotation with doors or windows -- A47H 1/03 covers curtains that are attached to a pivoting mechanism allowing them to rotate in sync with doors or windows, so the fabric stays aligned with the opening and moves automatically as the door or window swings.
[A47H 1/04] Curtain rails -- The subclass A47H 1/04 covers curtain rails, which are the supporting rods, tracks or channels used to hang curtains on windows or doors, including fixed, telescopic or adjustable mounting arrangements. It pertains to furniture items that furnish openings such as windows and doors.
[A47H 1/06] fixed -- A47H 1/06 covers fixed curtains, which are window or door coverings that are permanently attached and not intended to be drawn or moved. It belongs to the category of furnishings for windows or doors within the furniture group of human necessities.
[A47H 1/08] extensible -- A47H 1/08 relates to curtains that can be lengthened or shortened, using mechanisms such as telescoping rails, pleated sections or draw-strings to provide adjustable coverage for windows or doors. The classification covers any extensible system that lets the user vary the curtain's size for privacy, light control or decorative effect.
[A47H 1/10] Means for mounting curtain rods or rails -- The CPC subclass A47H 1/10 covers devices for attaching curtain rods or rails to walls, windows, doors or other support structures. It includes brackets, clips, fittings and other mounting hardware that secure curtains in place, and is nested under furnishings for windows or doors.
[A47H 11/00] Curtain cord appurtenances -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Accessories for curtain cords for horizontally opening and closing fabric curtains.
[A47H 1/102] for mounting curtain rods -- A47H 1/102 pertains to hardware used to attach curtain rods to a building structure, such as brackets, clips, rings or other fittings that secure the rod to walls, ceilings or similar surfaces. It falls under furniture → furnishings for windows or doors → curtains, covering mounting elements for rod installation.
[A47H 11/02] Engaging-pieces on curtain cords for operating curtains -- A47H 11/02 designates the components attached to curtain cords that enable the cords to be pulled, locked or released to open and close curtains. These engaging pieces include hooks, clips, beads or similar fittings that connect the cord to the curtain hardware.
[A47H 1/104] for mounting curtain rails -- A47H 1/104 designates devices and methods used to attach or support curtain rails to walls, ceilings or other structures. It falls under the CPC hierarchy for curtains within furnishings for windows or doors, a subset of furniture.
[A47H 11/04] End attachment clips, e.g. for tassels -- End attachment clips are small fasteners, often metal or plastic, designed to secure the ends of decorative tassels to curtain cords. They are used as accessories for window or door furnishings to provide a neat, detachable finish for tassel trims.
[A47H 11/06] Cord pulleys -- A47H 11/06 covers cord pulleys, the small wheels or sheaves used in curtain-cord systems to guide and redirect the pulling cord. These components are classified under furnishings for windows or doors, facilitating the opening and closing of curtains by reducing friction and allowing smooth movement.
[A47H 1/12] Adjustable mountings -- The A47H 1/12 subclass covers adjustable mounting systems for curtains, including brackets, rods, tracks, and other hardware that permit alteration of position, height, or angle after installation. It addresses devices that enable users to modify the placement or tension of window or door furnishings for functional or aesthetic purposes.
[A47H 1/122] for curtain rods -- A47H 1/122 pertains to curtain-rod devices, including the rods themselves, brackets, and mounting hardware used to support or hold curtains as part of window or door furnishings.
[A47H 1/124] for curtain rails -- The A47H 1/124 subclass refers to inventions relating to curtain-rail systems, including the means for mounting, guiding, supporting or operating rails that hold curtains. It covers components such as brackets, rollers, tensioning devices and other accessories designed to facilitate the installation and movement of curtains on windows or doors.
[A47H 1/13] Brackets or adjustable mountings for both roller blinds and drawable curtains -- A47H 1/13 classifies brackets, fittings or adjustable mounting devices that are suitable for installing both roller-type blinds and curtains that can be drawn across windows or doors. It covers hardware that enables flexible positioning, tilting or height adjustment for these window-covering systems.
[A47H 1/14] Brackets for supporting rods or rails -- A47H 1/14 covers brackets used to support curtain rods or rails
[A47H 1/142] for supporting rods -- A47H 1/142 is a CPC subclass under Curtains that specifically covers devices for supporting rods in window or door furnishings. It sits within the hierarchy Human Necessities → Furniture → Furnishings for windows or doors → Curtains.
[A47H 1/144] for supporting rails -- A47H 1/144 in the Cooperative Patent Classification pertains to devices that support curtain rails, including brackets, tracks or other mounting elements used to hold the rail in place on windows or doors.
[A47H 1/16] Holders made of wire for curtain rods -- A47H 1/16 pertains to holders made of wire that are used to support curtain rods, classified under furnishings for windows or doors within the broader category of furniture and human necessities.
[A47H 1/18] Other curtain suspension devices, e.g. wire, cord -- A47H 1/18 covers curtain-hanging mechanisms that are not covered by more specific subclasses, encompassing devices such as wires, cords or other simple suspension elements used to support curtains.
[A47H 1/19] Devices for mounting the wire, cord, or the like -- A47H 1/19 relates to hardware used to attach curtain-raising cords, wires or similar elements to a supporting structure. It includes brackets, hooks, clips, cleats, eyelets and other fastening devices that secure the cord to walls, ceilings or curtain rods, enabling the curtains to be drawn or positioned.
[A47H 13/00] Fastening curtains on curtain rods or rails -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Clamps, rings, hooks and pins for fastening curtains on curtain rails or rods.
[A47H 13/01] by clamps -- A47H 13/01 refers to devices that fasten curtains to curtain rods or rails by means of clamps, enabling quick attachment and removal of the drapery. It falls under the hierarchy Human Necessities → Furniture → Furnishings for Windows or Doors and describes clamp-type curtain-holding mechanisms.
[A47H 13/02] by rings, e.g. with additional runners -- A47H 13/02 covers devices for attaching curtains to rods or rails using rings, often combined with additional runners to guide the fabric. It falls under furnishings for windows or doors in the Human Necessities section, specifically relating to curtain-fastening mechanisms.
[A47H 13/04] by hooks, e.g. with additional runners -- A47H 13/04 covers devices that attach curtains to curtain rods or rails using hooks, often combined with extra runners to guide or support the fabric. The classification applies to fastening mechanisms that secure the curtain by hooking onto the rod, facilitating opening, closing, and positioning of the drapery.
[A47H 13/06] by pins, e.g. with additional runners -- This subclass covers devices that fasten curtains to rods or rails by means of pins, often together with additional runners, to secure the curtain in place. It is classified under furnishings for windows or doors within the furniture group of the Cooperative Patent Classification.
[A47H 13/14] Means for forming pleats -- The A47H 13/14 subclass covers devices that create pleats in curtains or draperies, typically integrated with or attached to curtain rods or rail systems for secure fastening. These mechanisms produce regular, uniform folds to improve the appearance and function of window furnishings.
[A47H 13/16] Pleat belts -- A47H 13/16 relates to pleat belts, the fabric or strap elements that gather and lock curtain folds in place on a rod or rail, enabling secure fastening of curtains as part of window or door furnishings.
[A47H 15/00] Runners or gliders for supporting curtains on rails or rods -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Gliders, i.e. gliding curtain supports and runners, i.e. supports for curtains having wheel-like rollers, for supporting curtains on rails or rods.
[A47H 15/02] Runners -- The A47H 15/02 entry covers runners or gliders that allow curtains to move smoothly along a rail or rod in window or door furnishings. These components are part of the broader category of furniture items designed for human necessities.
[A47H 15/04] Gliders -- A47H 15/04 pertains to gliders or runners that support curtains on rails or rods, allowing the curtains to slide smoothly for opening and closing. These components are attached to the curtain heading and travel within a rail system used on windows or doors.
[A47H 19/00] Rosettes for holding curtains -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Devices fastened to the side of the door or window for holding curtains tied-up in place.  **Glossary:** - Festoon: Decorative representation of a string of flowers suspended between two points; used on pottery or in architectural work
