[A47H 2001/006] End-stops, i.e. devices preventing the curtain falling off the end of the rail or rod -- A47H 2001/006 relates to end-stops, which are devices that keep a curtain from sliding off the end of its supporting rail or rod. The classification covers mechanical, magnetic or other means used in drapery and window-treatment systems to secure the curtain's trailing edge and maintain proper positioning.
[A47H 2001/0205] being curved -- A47H 2001/0205 designates furniture components that are intentionally curved, such as backs, armrests or support structures of chairs, stools and other seating devices. It falls within the CPC subclass for seats and related parts.
[A47H 2001/021] Multiple rods in parallel arrangement -- A47H 2001/021 covers domestic furniture elements that employ two or more rods arranged in parallel as the main supporting structure, such as slatted bed bases, chair backs or adjustable frame sections. It refers to designs where the parallel rods provide load-bearing or positioning functions.
[A47H 2001/0215] being tubular -- A47H 2001/0215 covers tubular components used in furniture, particularly kitchen and domestic items, such as tube-shaped legs, frames, supports or structural members that are formed from hollow cylindrical sections. The classification groups designs where the tubular shape is a defining functional or aesthetic element.
[A47H 2001/042] Curtain rails shaped as curtain rods -- The code A47H 2001/042 covers curtain rails that are shaped as curtain rods, describing hardware where the rail itself functions like a rod for hanging curtains. It belongs to class A47H, which groups window covering devices and related accessories.
[A47H 2001/045] being curved -- A47H 2001/045 classifies patents for domestic appliances where a curved shape of a component-such as a housing, handle or blade-is a defining functional or aesthetic feature of the invention.
[A47H 2001/047] with multiple tracks -- A47H 2001/047 covers cooking appliances that incorporate two or more parallel heating tracks or zones, allowing separate or simultaneous preparation of different foods. The classification applies to grills, griddles, hot plates or similar devices whose design includes multiple elongated heating elements arranged side by side. It is used for patents describing the layout, control or benefits of such multi-track cooking surfaces.
[A47H 2005/025] controlled by electronic sensors -- This CPC subgroup covers domestic appliances, especially cleaning or food-preparation devices, whose operation is regulated by electronic sensors that detect conditions such as presence, level, temperature or motion. The sensors enable automatic adjustment, activation or shut-off of the device without manual control.
[A47H 2015/005] Devices for inserting or removing runners or gliders from curtain rails -- A47H 2015/005 covers devices designed to insert or remove the runners or gliders that travel along curtain rails, facilitating the mounting, adjustment or replacement of curtains, drapes or blinds. The classification includes tools, mechanisms or accessories that engage the rail to guide or release the gliding elements.
[A47H 2/02] extensible -- A47H 2/02 covers pelmets, valances or similar decorative window/door trims that are designed to be extensible, meaning they can be lengthened, folded, telescoped or otherwise adjusted to fit openings of varying widths after manufacture. This includes mechanisms or constructions that allow the trim to expand or contract without replacing the component.
[A47H 2023/003] Additional elements for the decoration of curtains -- A47H 2023/003 covers accessories and components that are added to curtains for decorative purposes, such as tie-backs, fringe, beads, or ornamental trim. The subclass includes designs that enhance the visual appeal of curtain panels without altering their primary function of covering windows.
[A47H 2023/006] Illumination devices -- A47H 2023/006 refers to the subclass of the Cooperative Patent Classification dealing with illumination devices, covering the design, structure and functional aspects of artificial lighting equipment such as lamps, light-emitting diode modules, fixtures and control systems for domestic, commercial or industrial use. It includes innovations that improve light distribution, efficiency, safety or integration with other furniture or building elements.
[A47H 2023/025] Panel type curtains -- A47H 2023/025 covers panel-type curtains, which are fabric or material panels used to close or cover openings such as windows, doors or interior partitions. The classification includes hanging, sliding, or draw-type panels that can be raised, lowered or drawn aside, and may incorporate decorative or functional features like ties, hooks, or light-blocking layers.
[A47H 21/00] Curtain shields -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Devices that have the function of shielding off curtains, for curtains which are fixed to a window, so that the curtains are protected to external influences.
[A47H 2201/00] Means for connecting curtains -- A47H 2201/00 covers devices used to join or attach curtains to one another or to supporting hardware on windows and doors, including hooks, rings, clamps, gliders, track fittings and similar connection mechanisms that secure curtain panels in place.
[A47H 2201/01] Magnets -- The CPC subclass A47H 2201/01 designates magnets employed as fastening or connecting devices for curtains, blinds or similar window treatments. It covers magnetic strips, beads, plates or systems that hold or attach curtains to frames, rails or other components of window and door furnishings.
[A47H 2201/02] Hook-and-loop fasteners -- The CPC entry A47H 2201/02 pertains to hook-and-loop fasteners employed as a means for attaching curtains to windows or doors. These reusable fasteners provide a non-mechanical, adjustable connection within the broader class of furnishings for windows or doors.
[A47H 23/00] Curtains -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Details of curtains, such as shapes and materials. Only horizontally opening and closing fabric curtains.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Arrangements for shower curtains -> A47K3/38
[A47H 23/01] Bottom bars for stretching hanging curtains -- Bottom bars for stretching hanging curtains are narrow, rigid strips installed along the lower edge of a curtain to keep it taut and prevent sagging. They are usually removable, adjustable and mounted on the window or door frame to improve the drape and appearance of the window furnishing.
[A47H 23/02] Shapes of curtains -- A47H 23/02 relates to the geometric forms and design configurations of curtains, covering straight, pleated, gathered, and other decorative shapes used to dress windows or doors.
[A47H 23/04] Shapes of curtains -- A47H 23/04 pertains to the design and configuration of curtain shapes, covering contour, pleating and other geometric forms that determine how a curtain hangs and functions. It is applied to inventions that modify the form of curtains for aesthetic or functional purposes within the furniture category for windows or doors.
[A47H 23/05] of chain or chain mail -- A47H 23/05 covers curtains constructed from chain or chain-mail materials, typically metallic links assembled to form a flexible screen for windows or doors. The code applies to designs where the chain fabric serves as a decorative or privacy element rather than a traditional woven or fabric curtain.
[A47H 23/06] Systems consisting of two or more co-operating curtains with transparent or perforated parts behind each other -- The code A47H 23/06 covers window or door covering systems that employ two or more coordinated curtains, where one layer is transparent or perforated and positioned behind another curtain. Such arrangements are designed to provide adjustable light transmission, privacy, and decorative effects by allowing the layers to work together as a single unit.
[A47H 23/08] Selection of particular materials -- The A47H 23/08 entry concerns the selection of particular materials for curtains, detailing the criteria and methods used to choose fabrics, textiles, or other substances based on properties such as appearance, durability, light-transmission, insulation, fire resistance or ease of cleaning. It guides manufacturers and designers in matching material characteristics to functional and aesthetic requirements for window-covering applications.
[A47H 23/10] the material being plastics or the like -- A47H 23/10 designates curtains whose primary material is plastic or a similar synthetic polymeric substance. The code applies to window or door coverings made from PVC, polyester,
[A47H 23/12] the material being paper or cardboard -- A47H 23/12 covers curtains whose primary material is paper or cardboard, used as window or door furnishings. The code is part of the Cooperative Patent Classification hierarchy under human necessities → furniture → furnishings for windows or doors, and it addresses designs, manufacturing methods and functional aspects of such paper-based curtains.
[A47H 23/14] the material being woven textile with reinforcing bars, e.g. made of wood -- The CPC subclass A47H 23/14 pertains to curtains whose woven textile is reinforced with bars, often wooden, to provide extra rigidity or a decorative profile. It is situated under furnishings for windows or doors within the furniture category of human necessities. This code is applied to patents describing such reinforced textile window coverings.
[A47H 27/00] Boxes, shelves, holders or similar supports for holding flowers -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Devices for carrying flowers, flower pots, flower trays and the like on or at windows.
[A47H 3/00] Fastening, clamping, or guiding devices for the bands or cords of curtains or the like -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Clamps and cord tensioning devices. Horizontally moving fabric curtains  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Tassels for screens -> E06B9/24 , E06B9/78 - Window blinds, slats, roman blinds or roll-up curtains -> E06B9/24
[A47H 3/02] Fastening, clamping, or guiding devices for bands or cords -- A47H 3/02 is the CPC subclass for devices that attach, secure or direct the cords or bands used to operate curtains, blinds or similar window and door coverings. It includes fasteners, clamps, guides and related mechanisms for controlling the movement of these fabrics.
[A47H 3/04] Automatic cord clamps -- This classification covers devices that automatically grip and hold curtain cords or similar bands, allowing curtains to be opened, closed, or locked without manual tying. It includes mechanisms that clamp, release, or guide cords in response to user input or sensors, typically integrated into window or door furnishings.
[A47H 3/06] Non-automatic cord clamps -- A47H 3/06 refers to non-automatic cord clamps, devices that manually secure or release the cords of curtains, blinds or similar window dressings. These fittings hold the band or cord in place without motorised or spring-loaded mechanisms and are used in furnishings for windows or doors.
[A47H 3/08] Cord-tensioning devices for curtains with two cords -- This class covers devices that tension two cords in a curtain system to keep curtains level and reduce sagging. They are mechanisms that secure and adjust the tension of the cords that raise or lower curtains, often using springs, pulleys or clamps.
[A47H 3/10] Cord guides -- A47H 3/10 pertains to devices that fasten, clamp or guide the cords or bands of curtains and similar window or door furnishings, such as cord guides and related accessories. These components control the movement of textile panels, preventing tangling and ensuring smooth opening and closing.
[A47H 3/12] Belt strap holders -- A47H 3/12 in the Cooperative Patent Classification refers to belt strap holders used as fastening, clamping, or guiding devices for the bands or cords of curtains or similar window furnishings. This classification is specific to components designed to manage and secure curtain ties or straps efficiently.
[A47H 5/00] Devices for drawing draperies, curtains, or the like -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Devices for drawing horizontally moving curtains.
[A47H 5/02] Devices for opening and closing curtains -- A47H 5/02 covers mechanisms that open or close curtains, draperies or similar window treatments. It includes manual, motorized or automated devices such as pull-cords, track-mounted runners, remote-controlled systems and other apparatus that facilitate the movement of fabric panels.
[A47H 5/03] Devices with guiding means and push or draw rods -- A47H 5/03 covers mechanisms for opening or closing draperies, curtains or similar window dressings that use guiding elements together with push-or-draw rods. The subclass includes systems where rods slide within guides or tracks to move the fabric, providing controlled, linear motion for window or door furnishings.
[A47H 5/032] Devices with guiding means and draw cords -- A47H 5/032 covers mechanisms for opening or closing draperies, curtains or similar window treatments that combine guiding elements-such as tracks, rollers or channels-with draw cords. The classification includes systems where the cords are directed by these guides to ensure smooth, controlled movement and accurate positioning of the fabric.
[A47H 5/0325] using electrical or electronical drive, detecting or controlling means -- The CPC subclass A47H 5/0325 covers curtain-drawing mechanisms that are powered by an electric or electronic motor and incorporate sensors or control systems to operate, stop or adjust the movement. It includes automated or smart systems for
[A47H 5/04] Devices with lazy-tongs -- A47H 5/04 designates devices equipped with lazy-tongs for operating draperies, curtains or similar window and door furnishings. The classification covers mechanisms such as rods, chains, cords or comparable elements that allow remote opening, closing or adjusting of the textile covering.
[A47H 5/06] Devices with screw-threads on rods or spindles -- This subclass covers curtain-drawing devices that use a screw-threaded rod or spindle to move draperies, curtains or similar window or door furnishings. The mechanism advances or retracts the fabric by rotating the threaded element, providing precise positioning and locking. It is classified under furniture accessories for windows or doors within the Human Necessities section.
[A47H 5/08] Devices for drawing draperies attached to the doors or windows -- A47H 5/08 classifies mechanisms mounted on doors or windows that draw, pull or otherwise manipulate curtains, drapes, blinds or similar window dressings. It includes cords, rods, motorised pullers and other devices designed to open or close such coverings, and falls under Human Necessities → Furniture → Furnishings for windows or doors.
[A47H 5/09] Devices for swinging the curtains away from doors or windows -- A47H 5/09 refers to mechanisms that swing curtains, draperies or similar coverings away from doors or windows, typically integrated into window or door furnishings. These devices are designed to open and close the fabric without manual pulling, often using levers, springs or motorised elements, and fall under the CPC subclass for furnishings for windows or doors.
[A47H 5/14] Apparatus for lowering curtains or the like -- **Definition:** This place covers:  For lowering horizontally opening and closing curtains made of fabric.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Vertically opening and closing venetian blinds, roman shades, roller blinds or horizontally opening and closing lamellar blinds -> E06B9/24
[A47H 7/00] Devices for putting-up and removing curtain rods -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Devices for installing or removing a curtain rod; devices for lowering or lifting a curtain rod with or without a curtain attached.
[A47H 7/02] Curtain rods, capable of being lowered -- A47H 7/02 covers curtain-rod devices that can be lowered, including mechanisms for installing, adjusting and removing rods on windows or doors. It focuses on rod assemblies with built-in lowering or retractable features, enabling easy height control and storage when not in use.
[A47H 99/00] Subject matter not provided for in other groups of this subclass -- **Definition:** This place covers:  All devices, methods etc. related to horizontally moving fabric curtains not classified in other A47H groups.
[A47J] KITCHEN EQUIPMENT -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Devices for making beverages therefore include:  Household apparatuses for making beverages, e.g. by brewing or diluting;  Beverage shakers, devices for foaming milk.  Storing and handling of devices for preparing, cooking, or warming food or for making beverages refer to:  Storing and handling arrangements therefore include:  Devices for warming kitchen utensils, e.g. dishes.  Storing food refers to:  Devices for storing food therefore include:  Kitchen containers;  Thermally insulated vessels, e.g. flasks, jugs or jars.  **Glossary:** - cooking: boiling, braising, steaming, pressure cooking, frying, baking, roasting, grilling or barbecuing food. - boiling: cooking food by submerging it in boiling water, or other water-based liquid (e.g. broth or milk); keeping liquid foodstuff (or water) at a boiling temperature. - braising: cooking food with moist heat, e.g. stewing or pot roasting. - steaming: cooking food in an atmosphere with hot steam. - pressure cooking: cooking food at a pressure different (i.e. higher or lower) than that of the atmosphere. - frying: cooking food in hot oil or fat, e.g. pan frying or deep frying. - deep (fat) frying: cooking food by submerging it in hot oil or fat. - baking: cooking food by dry heat acting by conduction, normally in an oven, but also in hot ashes, or on hot stones. - roasting: cooking food with dry high heat. - grilling: applying direct dry high heat to food, most commonly from above, with heat transfer primarily via thermal radiation, e.g. by using the upper heating element of an electric oven, or inside a drawer below the flame of a gas oven. - barbecuing: cooking food with a barbecue by the heat and hot gases of a fire, smoking wood or hot coals of charcoal, or of an electrical heating element. - toasting: browning food by exposure to dry radiant heat, e.g. grilling of sliced bread on both sides. - warming: raising the temperature of food but not cooking it. - grating: disintegrating food, e.g. cheese, with a tool having several sharp protrusions.
[A47J 17/00] Household peeling, stringing, or paring implements or machines -- **Definition:** This place covers:  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Peeling, stringing, or paring machines for foodstuff in bulk -> A23N
[A47J 17/02] Hand devices for scraping or peeling vegetables or the like -- This classification covers hand-operated tools designed to scrape or peel vegetables, fruits, or similar food items. It includes devices such as peelers, scrapers and paring tools used in domestic kitchen settings. Under the CPC hierarchy it falls under human necessities, furniture, kitchen equipment.
[A47J 17/04] Citrus fruit peelers -- A47J 17/04 covers household implements or machines designed specifically for peeling citrus fruit. These devices remove the rind from oranges, lemons, limes or similar fruits, ranging from simple hand-held peelers to powered paring machines used in kitchen settings.
[A47J 17/06] Devices for stringing beans -- A47J 17/06 covers implements or machines designed to string beans, enabling removal of beans from pods and preparation for drying or cooking. These devices are classified under kitchen equipment within the furniture group of human necessities.
[A47J 17/08] Asparagus peelers -- A47J 17/08 designates asparagus peelers, a specific type of household implement used for peeling, stringing or paring asparagus. It falls within the CPC hierarchy of Human Necessities → Furniture → Kitchen Equipment → Household peeling, stringing or paring implements or machines.
[A47J 17/10] Vegetable or fruit grippers or holders for use while peeling -- A47J 17/10 covers vegetable or fruit grippers and holders designed to secure produce while it is being peeled, such as handheld clamps or small mechanical devices used in the kitchen. These tools are classified under household peeling implements within the furniture-kitchen equipment hierarchy.
[A47J 17/14] Machines for peeling -- A47J 17/14 includes machines that remove the outer skin of fruit, vegetables or other foods, employing rotating blades, abrasive surfaces, steam or similar mechanisms. These appliances are designed for kitchen or industrial use to automate the peeling, stringing or paring of produce.
[A47J 17/16] Peeling machines with rotary fruit-holding spindles and fixed or movable peeler blades -- A47J 17/16 covers peeling machines that use a rotary fruit-holding spindle while a fixed or movable blade removes the skin. These devices are intended for kitchen use and fall under the furniture and kitchen equipment group in the CPC.
[A47J 17/18] with scraping discs or rotors -- A47J 17/18 designates household peeling, stringing or paring devices that use scraping discs or rotors. The subclass falls under kitchen equipment in the furniture group of human necessities and covers implements or machines that remove outer layers of food by means of rotating abrasive elements.
[A47J 17/20] with brushes -- A47J 17/20 classifies kitchen devices that peel, string or pare food and incorporate a brush as part of the tool, such as peelers or slicers with built-in cleaning or polishing brushes. It is a sub-class of household peeling, stringing, or paring implements or machines.

[A47J 2027/006] especially adapted for preparing pasta -- The CPC subclass A47J 2027/006 covers kitchen equipment that is especially adapted for preparing pasta, such as pasta-making machines, extruders, cutters and related accessories designed to shape, cook or drain pasta products. [A47J 2027/043] for cooking food in steam -- A47J 2027/043 covers devices or methods that use steam as the primary means of preparing food, including steam ovens, steam boilers, and combined cooking appliances that generate and apply steam to heat and cook meals. The classification pertains to kitchen and catering equipment where steam cooking is integral to the cooking process.
[A47J 2027/046] Devices for extracting fruit juice by means of steam -- A47J 2027/046 covers apparatuses that obtain fruit juice by introducing steam into the fruit pulp, where the steam both heats and ruptures cell walls to release juice, after which the liquid-steam mixture is separated, condensed and collected. The classification includes steam generators, pressurised vessels, suction or discharge systems and any integrated condensers designed for this steam-assisted extraction process.

[A47J 2043/04418] with housing extending perpendicular, e.g. horizontally, from the tool axis -- This CPC subclass groups power-driven hand tools whose housing is mounted transversely to the tool's working axis, extending sideways or horizontally from the shaft. It applies to devices such as drills, saws or jackhammers where the motor or gearbox is positioned alongside the cutting or driving element rather than in line with it.
[A47J 2043/04427] with housing extending vertically in line with the tool axis -- A47J 2043/04427 covers household or domestic devices whose external casing or housing is designed to extend upward in line with the axis of the tool or functional element, such as appliances where the motor housing continues vertically above the working shaft, providing alignment and structural support.

[A47J 2043/04445] attachable to a stand -- A47J 2043/04445 covers accessories that are designed to be attached to a stand, such as brackets, clamps or mounting devices that secure items to tables, workbenches or similar supports. [A47J 2043/04454] Apparatus of counter top type -- A47J 2043/04454 pertains to kitchen appliances intended for placement on a work surface, such as mixers, blenders or food processors that remain stationary while operating. The code classifies devices whose motor and functional components are integrated into a housing designed to sit on a countertop.
[A47J 2043/04463] with a mixing unit rigidly fixed on the housing and a movable support for the bowl -- The CPC entry A47J 2043/04463 pertains to kitchen appliances in which the mixing unit is permanently attached to the housing while the mixing bowl is held on a support that can move relative to the housing, allowing the bowl to be lifted, tilted or removed without detaching the mixer itself. This arrangement enables convenient loading and unloading of the bowl while the motor and drive components remain fixed.
[A47J 2043/04472] with a mixing unit vertically movable relative to the support -- A47J 2043/04472 covers kitchen or domestic appliances that incorporate a mixing unit mounted so it can move vertically relative to its support, allowing the user to raise or lower the mixer for loading, unloading or adjusting mixing depth. This feature is typical of stand-mixers or food-processing devices where the bowl or beaters are positioned on a liftable column.
[A47J 2043/04481] with a mixing unit pivotable on the support -- This CPC subclass refers to kitchen appliances where the mixing unit is mounted on a support and can be swung or rotated about a pivot, allowing the bowl or container to be positioned for easy access. It covers devices such as stand mixers, food processors or dough kneaders with a pivoting mixing head.
[A47J 2043/0449] with a mixing unit detachable from the support -- This subgroup covers kitchen appliances in which the mixing unit is designed to be separable from its supporting structure, allowing the bowl, agitator or similar component to be removed from the base or frame for cleaning, replacement or reconfiguration. It applies to mixers, dough kneaders and comparable devices where detachment of the mixing part from the support is a defining feature.
[A47J 2043/0733] Weighing means -- A47J 2043/0733 covers weighing means incorporated in household or domestic appliances, such as scales, balances, load-cell sensors or other devices used to determine mass or weight as part of a larger machine. The classification applies to equipment where the weighing function is integral to the appliance's operation rather than a standalone scale. This includes kitchen, laundry or cleaning devices that feature built-in measurement of contents.

[A47J 27/00] Cooking -- **Definition:** This place covers:  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Egg-cookers -> A47J29/00 - Apparatus for making beverages -> A47J31/00 - Camp cooking devices without integral heating means -> A47J33/00  **Glossary:** - cooking: boiling, braising, steaming or pressure cooking food.
[A47J 27/002] Construction of cooking-vessels; Methods or processes of manufacturing specially adapted for cooking-vessels -- A47J 27/002 concerns the construction and manufacturing methods for cooking vessels such as pots, pans and similar cookware. It includes the techniques and processes used to shape, join and finish these items, and is classified under kitchen equipment within the broader human necessities group.
[A47J 27/004] with integral electrical heating means  drinking cups with integral heating means  A47J36/2466 -- A47J 27/004 classifies kitchenware designed for drinking that incorporates built-in electric heating elements, such as mugs or cups that can warm the contents while in use. The subclass covers items where the heating mechanism is integral to the vessel, enabling direct temperature control without external appliances.
[A47J 27/02] with enlarged heating surfaces -- A47J 27/02 covers kitchen cooking equipment that incorporates enlarged heating surfaces, such as ovens, grills or stovetops with extended or broadened heating elements designed to increase contact area with food. The subclass is part of the Human Necessities → Furniture → Kitchen Equipment → Cooking hierarchy.
[A47J 27/022] with enlarged bottom -- A47J 27/022 designates cooking apparatus, such as pots or pans, that feature an enlarged bottom to enhance heat distribution and stability during use. This subgroup falls under kitchen equipment within the broader furniture category of human necessities.
[A47J 27/024] with liquid-heating tubes extending outside the vessel -- This sub-class covers cooking apparatuses that use liquid-heating tubes extending outside the cooking vessel to transfer heat, such as steam or water-filled tubes positioned externally to warm the pot or pan. It includes designs where the heating element is a tube of hot liquid that contacts the vessel rather than being embedded within it.
[A47J 27/026] with conduits through the vessel for circulating heating gases -- A47J 27/026 covers cooking vessels that incorporate internal conduits allowing heating gases to circulate through the pot or pan. The feature is intended to improve heat distribution and efficiency in kitchen appliances such as ovens or stovetops.
[A47J 27/04] for cooking food in steam -- A47J 27/04 covers devices and methods for cooking food using steam, such as steam ovens, vegetable steamers and related kitchen appliances. It is classified under cooking equipment within the hierarchy of kitchen equipment, furniture and the broader human necessities category.
[A47J 27/05] Tier steam-cookers, i.e. with steam-tight joints between cooking-vessels stacked while in use -- A47J 27/05 relates to tiered steam-cookers in which several cooking vessels are stacked and sealed together with steam-tight joints while in use. The design enables simultaneous cooking of multiple items in a compact, layered arrangement.
[A47J 27/06] Steam-heated kettles for domestic use -- A47J 27/06 covers steam-heated kettles intended for household use, where water is boiled by directing steam generated from a separate heating source through the kettle. The classification includes designs, mechanisms and accessories that enable domestic preparation of hot beverages or cooking liquids using steam heating.
[A47J 27/08] Pressure-cookers -- A47J 27/08 covers pressure-cookers, including devices that use sealed vessels to raise the boiling point of water for faster cooking, and their essential components such as lids, safety valves, and locking mechanisms. The class is used for patents relating to the design, construction, and safety features of household pressure-cooking equipment.
[A47J 27/0802] Control mechanisms for pressure-cookers -- A47J 27/0802 covers control mechanisms for pressure-cookers, such as pressure-release valves, thermostatic regulators, safety interlocks and electronic controllers that monitor and adjust internal pressure and temperature to ensure safe, efficient cooking. It is classified under CPC A47J (kitchen equipment) within the hierarchy of Human necessities → Furniture → Kitchen equipment → Cooking.
[A47J 27/0804] Locking devices -- The A47J 27/0804 class covers locking mechanisms used on cooking appliances and kitchen equipment, such as stoves, ovens, hoods, and associated drawers or doors. It includes devices that secure these items to prevent unauthorized operation, improve safety, or retain heat, and is situated under the hierarchy of Human Necessities → Furniture → Kitchen Equipment → Cooking.
[A47J 27/0806] of the bayonet-type -- A47J 27/0806 covers bayonet-type components for cooking equipment, such as accessories, lids or dispensers that are fastened to ovens, ranges or other kitchen appliances by a rotating lock mechanism. The design allows quick attachment and secure holding without tools, facilitating easy replacement or cleaning of the fitted part.
[A47J 27/0808] of the bridge-type -- A47J 27/0808 pertains to bridge-type cooking appliances or components, such as grills, griddles or ovens whose heating surface spans a gap like a bridge, used in kitchen equipment.
[A47J 27/0811] using a number of pivotable clamps along the circumference of the cooking-vessel -- A47J 27/0811 covers cooking vessels that are equipped with multiple pivotable clamps positioned around their circumference to secure the vessel to a support or to each other, improving stability and handling during use. The clamps can be adjusted or released individually, allowing flexible mounting on various cooking installations. This feature is applied to kitchen equipment such as pots, pans or kettles.
[A47J 27/0813] using a clamping ring or clamping segments -- A47J 27/0813 classifies kitchen cooking equipment that incorporates a clamping ring or clamping segments to secure or hold components during use, such as lids, pans, or accessories. The feature is intended to improve stability, safety, or ease of operation in food preparation.

[A47J 31/00] Apparatus for making beverages -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Apparatus for preparing beverages, Parts, accessories and details of such apparatus.  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Household machines or implements for straining foodstuffs -> A47J19/00 - Preparation of non-alcoholic beverages, e.g. by adding ingredients to fruit or vegetable juices -> A23L2/00 - Coffee or tea pots -> A47G19/14 - Tea infusers -> A47G19/16 - Brewing of beer -> C12C - Preparation of wine or other alcoholic beverages -> C12G  **Glossary:** - Brewing, extraction, infusion: beverage production process during which water is in contact with an ingredient for a certain time, after which the ingredient is separated from the obtained beverage by a suitable filtering means - Espresso coffee machine: Coffee machine which is arranged to brew a load of coffee by means of hot water under pressure, i.e. above atmospheric pressure - Portafilter: Cup-shaped element of an espresso machine, usually provided with a handle, which is arranged to be loaded with an ingredient (typically coffee) and then detachably engage an outlet of pressurized hot water of the machine for brewing the loaded ingredient with hot water under pressure - Drip coffee maker: Coffee machine having a brewing container with a filter (usually in the form of a brewing basket with paper filter) located above the beverage container, the machine having arrangements for having hot water delivered at ambient pressure into the brewing container, with the brewed beverage dripping from the brewing container into the beverage container located below it - Turkish coffee: Coffee obtained by immersing coffee powder into a beverage container containing hot water for a given time. The obtained beverage is then drunk unfiltered directly from the beverage container, i.e. the coffee powder is not filtered out and remains at the bottom of the container after drinking - "French press" coffee machine: Coffee machine having a beverage container in which coffee powder is immersed in hot water for a given infusion time. The machine is provided with a movable, immersible filter, typically in the form of a piston, which is actuated by the user to separate the beverage from the coffee powder upon elapsing of the infusion time - Moka coffee machine: type of espresso apparatus, e.g. to put on a stove, i.e. in which the water is heated in a lower, sealed boiling vessel, raised by the steam pressure through a rising pipe and an extraction chamber and subsequently is collected in a beverage container on top of the water boiling vessel
[A47J 31/002] following a specific operational sequence, e.g. for improving the taste of the extraction product -- A47J 31/002 relates to kitchen appliances that execute a defined operational sequence to improve the taste of a beverage extract, such as programmed steps for brewing, mixing, heating or timing that optimise flavor during coffee, tea or similar drink preparation.

This subgroup falls under kitchen equipment within the furniture category of human necessities, specifying devices where the beverage-preparing process relies on steam-heated water rather than other heating methods.
[A47J 31/303] classical type of espresso apparatus, e.g. to put on a stove, i.e. in which the water is heated in a lower, sealed boiling vessel, raised by the steam pressure through a rising pipe and an extraction chamber and subsequently is collected in a beverage container on top of the water boiling vessel -- A47J 31/303 covers classical espresso apparatus in which water is heated in a sealed lower boiling vessel, the steam pressure forces the water up a rising pipe into an extraction chamber, and the brewed coffee is collected in a container positioned above the boiling vessel.

It falls under the hierarchy human necessities → furniture → kitchen equipment.
[A47J 31/32] with hot water under air pressure -- A47J 31/32 relates to kitchen equipment for preparing drinks where hot water is forced through the beverage material by air pressure, such as espresso or other pressure-brew coffee machines. The classification covers the mechanisms that generate the pressure, heat the water, and deliver it to the brewing chamber, enabling rapid extraction of flavor from coffee grounds or similar substances.
[A47J 31/34] with hot water under liquid pressure -- A47J 31/34 covers kitchen appliances that prepare beverages by forcing hot water through a liquid under pressure, such as coffee makers, espresso machines and similar devices. It is classified under kitchen equipment for beverage preparation in the CPC system.

[A47J 36/16] Inserts -- A47J 36/16 covers inserts that are placed inside cooking vessels. These are parts, details or accessories such as removable liners, racks or steamers fitted to pots, pans or other kitchen equipment. It falls under Human Necessities → Furniture → Kitchen Equipment → Parts, details or accessories of cooking-vessels.
[A47J 36/165] Stirring devices operatively connected to cooking vessels when being removably inserted inside  mixing, whipping, or beating devices  A47J43/00 -- A47J 36/165 covers stirring devices that are operatively linked to cooking vessels and are designed to be removably inserted into mixers, whisks or beaters. The classification pertains to accessories for kitchen equipment that connect the stirrer directly to the vessel while it is being used in mixing, whipping or beating operations.
[A47J 36/18] Boilers or utensils with sieves inserted therein, e.g. potato-cookers -- The subclass A47J 36/18 covers cooking vessels that incorporate a built-in sieve or draining screen, such as potato-cookers with an inserted filter that allows food to be boiled or steamed while separating the liquid. It is used to classify kitchen equipment where the sieve is an integral part of the utensil.
[A47J 36/20] Perforated bases or perforated containers to be placed inside a cooking utensil -- A47J 36/20 covers perforated bases or containers designed to be placed inside cooking utensils, such as pots or pans, to allow liquids or steam to pass through while supporting food. These accessories include trays, steam racks, or shelf inserts that improve heat distribution and drainage during cooking.
[A47J 36/22] Wire inserts -- A47J 36/22 concerns wire inserts that serve as parts, details or accessories for cooking vessels such as pots and pans. These components are classified within kitchen equipment, itself a subcategory of furniture under the broader human necessities group.
[A47J 36/24] Warming devices -- A47J 36/24 covers warming devices that are accessories to cooking vessels, such as heated trays, chafing dishes, warming cabinets or heated plates used to keep prepared food at serving temperature. These items are classified under kitchen equipment within the broader categories of furniture and human necessities.
[A47J 36/2405] for warming food contained in vessels immersed in a water bath, e.g. chafers or steam tables  A47J36/2438  takes precedence -- A47J 36/2405 covers accessories for cooking vessels that warm food by immersing the vessels in a water bath, such as chafers or steam tables, and is superseded by the more specific subclass A47J 36/2438.
[A47J 36/2411] Baby bottle warmers; Devices for warming baby food in jars -- A47J 36/2411 relates to accessories for cooking vessels that warm infant feeding items, specifically baby bottle warmers and devices for heating baby food in jars. It falls under kitchen equipment within the furniture category of human necessities.
[A47J 36/2416] with a heat storage element or material -- A47J 36/2416 covers kitchen-equipment accessories that incorporate a heat-storage element or material, such as pots, pans or other cooking vessels designed to retain and slowly release heat. The classification applies to parts, details or add-ons that improve thermal retention, enabling more efficient cooking or keeping food warm after the heat source is removed.
[A47J 36/2422] using solid fuel -- A47J 36/2422 covers kitchen equipment parts, details or accessories for cooking vessels that are intended to operate with solid fuels such as wood, charcoal, coal or solid fuel tablets. It includes designs, modifications or auxiliary elements that enable or improve solid-fuel heating in pots, pans or similar cookware.
[A47J 36/2427] with a burner, e.g. using gasolene -- A47J 36/2427 designates cooking vessels that incorporate a burner, such as those powered by gas or gasoline, and is classified under parts, details or accessories of cooking-vessels within the kitchen equipment group of the CPC system.
[A47J 36/2433] with electrical heating means -- A47J 36/2433 designates parts, details or accessories for cooking-vessels that incorporate electrical heating means. It covers items such as electric inserts, heated handles or temperature-controlled lids used in kitchen equipment.
[A47J 36/2438] for warming a water-bath or -jacket -- A47J 36/2438 covers devices or arrangements that heat a water-bath or water-jacket used to maintain a desired temperature for cooking or holding food and liquids, such as heated basins, circulators, or insulated sleeves surrounding the vessel. It includes mechanisms that supply heat to the surrounding water rather than directly to the food.

They are used to keep food warm or preheat ovens and are typically portable, self-contained units.

This subclass is applied to inventions that enable users to adjust cooking functions over a wireless or wired network rather than through built-in manual controls.

[A47J 36/38] for withdrawing or condensing cooking vapors from cooking utensils -- A47J 36/38 covers devices that withdraw or condense steam and other vapors produced while cooking, such as lids, hoods or condensers fitted to pots, pans or similar utensils. These components are intended to control moisture, improve cooking efficiency and keep the kitchen environment cleaner.
[A47J 36/40] Leak-stopping devices for repairing cooking-vessels -- A47J 36/40 covers devices designed to seal or stop leaks in cooking vessels such as pots, pans, and kettles, enabling repair without replacing the whole item. [A47J 36/42] Devices to prevent deposition of scale, i.e. fur, or the like -- A47J 36/42 covers devices that are fitted to cooking vessels to stop material such as scale, fur or similar deposits from forming on interior surfaces; examples include anti-scaling inserts, screens, filters or scrapers that keep the vessel clean during use.
[A47J 37/00] Baking -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Devices for baking, roasting, grilling, frying. Vessels uniquely adapted for baking in a domestic environment. Roasting apparatus with movably-mounted food supports or with movable heating implements. Small-size ovens. Barbecues and accessories thereof. Bread toasters and accessories thereof. Frying pans and accessories thereof (including lids). Deep fat fryers (not pressure fryers) and accessories thereof (including lids or covers).  **Limiting references (this place does not cover):** - Pressure fryers -> A47J27/0817  **Glossary:** - Small-size cooking oven: small oven generally to be located on a kitchen countertop

It includes designs that facilitate heat distribution, release of baked goods and accommodation of particular dough or batter types, and falls under the hierarchy of Human Necessities → Furniture → Kitchen Equipment → Baking.

The integrated lid enables heat and moisture retention and convenient handling during preparation, cooking and serving.
[A47J 37/103] Broiling- or heating-lids -- A47J 37/103 covers lids designed to broil or heat food while it cooks, such as oven or grill covers that trap heat and direct radiant energy onto the contents. These accessories are used on kitchen equipment for baking and roasting to improve cooking efficiency and moisture retention.
[A47J 37/105] electrically heated -- A47J 37/105 is a CPC subclass that covers kitchen baking equipment that obtains heat from an electric source. It falls under the broader categories of human necessities, furniture and kitchen equipment, specifying electrically heated ovens, roasters or other baking devices. This code is used by the EPO and USPTO to index patents for electric baking appliances.
[A47J 37/106] Integrated basting devices -- The A47J 37/106 class covers devices incorporated into ovens or other baking appliances that automatically apply basting liquid to food during cooking. These integrated basting systems may include reservoirs, pumps and spray mechanisms designed to keep meat or pastries moist. They fall under the CPC subgroup for baking equipment within the furniture group.
[A47J 37/108] Accessories, e.g. inserts, plates to hold food down during frying -- A47J 37/108 covers kitchen accessories that hold food down during frying, such as inserts or plates placed in a pan or fryer. These components are used in baking and other cooking processes to keep the food stable and prevent movement.
[A47J 37/12] Deep fat fryers, e.g. for frying fish or chips -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Frying in a fluid fat, e.g. oil.
[A47J 37/1204] for domestic use -- A47J 37/1204 covers baking appliances and tools intended for domestic use, such as home ovens, mixers and related accessories. It is situated in the CPC hierarchy under Human Necessities → Furniture → Kitchen Equipment → Baking and is used to classify patents for household baking devices.
[A47J 37/1209] electrically heated -- A47J 37/1209 refers to kitchen baking equipment that incorporates electric heating elements to generate the heat needed for cooking. It covers devices such as electric ovens, toasters, and similar appliances where the heating function is supplied solely by electricity rather than gas or other energy sources.
[A47J 37/1214] the food being transported through an oil-bath -- A47J 37/1214 covers devices or processes that convey food items through an oil bath, such as continuous fryers or conveyance mechanisms that move products while they are being cooked in oil. It relates to kitchen equipment used for baking-type operations where the food is transported within the hot oil for uniform cooking.
[A47J 37/1219] with means for lowering or raising the frying basket -- The CPC subclass A47J 37/1219 covers kitchen baking equipment that incorporates a mechanism for vertically moving the frying basket, such as pull-down or lift-up devices, allowing the basket to be lowered into or raised out of the cooking chamber during operation.
[A47J 37/1223] with means for filtering the frying liquid -- It covers kitchen baking equipment such as fryers that incorporate a built-in system to separate and collect used oil or frying liquid, typically using screens, sieves or centrifugal mechanisms. The feature is integrated into the appliance to allow continuous or periodic filtration, extending oil life and improving food quality.
[A47J 37/1228] Automatic machines for frying and dispensing metered amounts of food -- A47J 37/1228 designates automatic devices that fry food and release it in precisely measured portions, integrating cooking and portion control in a single machine. These systems are used in kitchen equipment for baking and other food-preparation tasks, streamlining production and ensuring consistent serving sizes.
[A47J 37/1233] the frying liquid being heated outside the frying vessel, e.g. by pumping it through a heat exchanger -- A47J 37/1233 covers kitchen appliances in which the frying oil or other liquid is heated external to the pan, typically by circulating it through a separate heat-exchanger before it returns to the vessel. This indirect heating method is used to control temperature more precisely during baking or frying operations.
[A47J 37/1238] and the oil being returned to the frying vessel by means of a spraying system -- A47J 37/1238 covers kitchen appliances for baking that incorporate a system which recirculates oil from the cooking zone back into the frying vessel by spraying the oil onto the food or into the vessel. The feature is intended to improve oil reuse, maintain temperature and reduce oil loss during continuous frying operations.
[A47J 37/1242] heated with burners -- A47J 37/1242 in the Cooperative Patent Classification system, used by the European and United States patent offices, specifies kitchen equipment for baking that is heated using burners. This classification helps in organizing and searching for patents related to burner-heated baking appliances.
[A47J 37/1247] Details of the burners; Details of the channels for guiding the combustion gases -- The A47J 37/1247 subclass addresses the design and specification of burners and the associated gas-flow channels that direct combustion gases within kitchen baking equipment, detailing how these components are constructed, arranged, and integrated to achieve controlled heating.
[A47J 37/1252] of the pulse combustion type -- A47J 37/1252 covers kitchen baking equipment that employs a pulse-combustion principle, in which fuel is ignited in rapid, intermittent bursts to generate high-temperature heat for baking. The classification applies to ovens, burners or similar devices using this cyclic combustion method to improve heat distribution and energy efficiency in culinary applications.

It addresses how the heater is integrated to ensure efficient, safe cooking performance.

This rule ensures the equipment operates only in a secured, closed state.

[A47J 43/0783] activated by the proper positioning of a separate food chute on the cover -- A47J 43/0783 covers kitchen equipment that is operated when a separate food chute is correctly positioned on the cover, causing the device to activate. It falls within the subclass for implements used to prepare or hold food that are not covered by other groups, under the hierarchy of human necessities → furniture → kitchen equipment.
[A47J 43/0788] activated by the proper positioning of a food chute protector -- A47J 43/0788 relates to a kitchen implement that is activated only when a food-chute protector is correctly positioned, ensuring proper flow of food and preventing spillage or contamination during preparation or serving.
[A47J 43/0794] in which operation is not possible without the use of a food pusher -- This subgroup covers kitchen devices whose operation cannot be performed without a food pusher, such as mixers, slicers or cooking appliances that require a pusher to move food through the mechanism. The food pusher is integral to the function and is not optional.
[A47J 43/08] Driving mechanisms -- A47J 43/08 covers the driving mechanisms that power kitchen equipment used for preparing or holding food, such as motors, gear trains, belts, pulleys or other power-transmission devices that are not classified elsewhere in the subclass. It pertains to the means of converting energy into motion for appliances like mixers, slicers or ovens.
[A47J 43/082] for machines with tools driven from the upper side  A47J43/087 ,  A47J43/09  take precedence -- The CPC subclass A47J 43/082 falls under Human necessities → Furniture → Kitchen equipment and covers implements for preparing or holding food that are mounted on machines whose tools are driven from the upper side. It is subordinated to the broader groups A47J 43/087 and A47J 43/09.
[A47J 43/085] for machines with tools driven from the lower side  A47J43/087 ,  A47J43/09  take precedence -- This subclass covers kitchen equipment implements for preparing or holding food where the machine's tools are powered from below, such as mixers or slicers with lower-driven mechanisms
[A47J 43/087] for machines with several driving units  A47J43/09  takes precedence -- A47J 43/087 covers kitchen equipment that prepares or holds food and is not classified elsewhere, specifically machines equipped with multiple separate driving units. It includes devices such as mixers, slicers or processors that have two or more independent power sources to perform different functions simultaneously, and it is subordinate to the broader group A47J 43/09.
[A47J 43/09] with fluid drive, e.g. by jets -- A47J 43/09 covers kitchen implements that employ a fluid power source, such as water or air jets, to perform functions like mixing, cutting, or dispensing food. These devices are classified separately because they are driven by jets rather than mechanical or electric means.
[A47J 43/10] Egg-whisks -- A47J 43/10 covers egg-whisks, hand-operated tools used to beat, stir or incorporate air into eggs and other liquid foods. They are classified under kitchen equipment for preparing or holding food, and are not included in other subclasses of furniture.
[A47J 43/1006] Hand-driven mixing devices with rotating tools, e.g. sticking out from the bottom of the mixing receptacle; with rotating bowls; with an additional function -- A47J 43/1006 covers hand-operated mixers in which the mixing tool rotates, such as designs where the agitator extends from the bottom of the bowl, mixers with a rotating bowl itself, or units that combine mixing with another function. The classification is for kitchen equipment used to prepare or hold food that does not fall within other subclasses.
[A47J 43/1012] the mixing device being fitted on a post beside the mixing receptacle -- A47J 43/1012 covers kitchen mixing devices that are mounted on a post positioned beside the mixing receptacle, allowing the agitator to operate while remaining external to the bowl.
[A47J 43/1018] the mixing device being fitted on the cover of, or directly on, the stationary mixing receptacle -- This subclass covers devices that integrate a mixer onto the lid or directly onto a stationary mixing receptacle, allowing the bowl to remain fixed while the mixing mechanism operates. It applies to kitchen equipment used for preparing or holding food.
[A47J 43/1025] with gears between the drive axis and the driven axis, e.g. for planetary motion of tools or for coaxial tools -- A47J 43/1025 pertains to kitchen implements for preparing or holding food that incorporate a gear train between the drive shaft and the driven shaft, enabling planetary motion of the tool or coaxial operation. It falls within the furniture-kitchen equipment subclass.
[A47J 43/1031] the mixing device being provided with a grip to be held by one hand, the other hand being used for driving the mixing device  A47J43/105 ,  A47J43/1056  take precedence -- A mixing device for kitchen use designed with a hand-grip that lets one hand hold it while the other hand powers the mixing action, classified under CPC A47J 43/1031 for implements for preparing or holding food. This feature falls within the more specific sub-groups A47J 43/105 and A47J 43/1056, which take precedence.
[A47J 43/1037] with two or more mixing tools -- Implements in this group are kitchen devices that combine two or more mixing tools, such as beaters, whisks or paddles, in a single unit for preparing or holding food. They are intended for tasks like dough kneading, batter beating or sauce stirring and are not classified elsewhere in the subclass.
[A47J 43/1043] with coaxial tools -- A47J 43/1043 covers kitchen implements that incorporate coaxial tools, where the functional element is arranged along the same axis as the main body or handle. This subclass includes devices such as mixers, slicers or grinders in which the cutting or processing component is coaxial with the gripping part and is not covered by other groups in the A47J subclass.
[A47J 43/105] the rotating tools being driven by a reciprocating member, e.g. by a rack-and-pinion mechanism or by a tangentially-pulled rope -- A47J 43/105 relates to kitchen implements that rotate by being driven from a reciprocating source, for example through a rack-and-pinion gear train or a rope pulled tangentially. It is classified under human necessities, furniture, kitchen equipment, specifically tools for preparing or holding food not covered in other groups.
[A47J 43/1056] the tools rotating about a horizontal or inclined axis -- A47J 43/1056 covers kitchen tools that rotate about a horizontal or inclined axis, such as turntables, rotisserie spits and rotating mixers used for preparing or holding food. These devices perform cooking, mixing or serving functions and are not classified elsewhere in the subclass.
[A47J 43/1062] the mixing devices being provided with an additional liquid receptacle, e.g. for adding oil during mixing -- A47J 43/1062 covers kitchen mixing devices that incorporate a separate liquid compartment, allowing ingredients such as oil to be added automatically during the mixing cycle. The feature is intended to improve convenience and timing in food preparation.
[A47J 43/1068] Construction of the rotating mixing tools -- A47J 43/1068 covers the design and construction of rotating mixing tools used in kitchen equipment, such as mixers or blenders, where the tool itself rotates to combine ingredients. It pertains to implements for preparing or holding food that are not covered by other subclasses.
[A47J 43/1075] Hand-driven mixing devices with reciprocating or oscillating tools -- A47J 43/1075 categorises hand-operated mixing devices that employ reciprocating or oscillating tools for food preparation or storage. This subclass covers kitchen equipment such as manually powered beaters, whisks or mixers that move their attachments back-and-forth rather than rotating. It is used for items not assigned to other more specific groups within the furniture-kitchen equipment hierarchy.
[A47J 43/1081] with rectilinearly reciprocating tools -- A47J 43/1081 covers kitchen implements that incorporate rectilinearly reciprocating tools, meaning devices whose working elements move back and forth along a straight line. Typical examples include linear-motion slicers, kneading blades or mixers that reciprocate rather than rotate, used for preparing or holding food where no other subclass group applies.
[A47J 43/1087] Whisks or similar tools comprising mixing wires -- A47J 43/1087 covers kitchen utensils such as whisks and comparable tools that consist of a handle fitted with a bundle of flexible mixing wires, used to beat, aerate or blend food ingredients and not classified elsewhere in the subclass.
[A47J 43/1093] the wires being of the closed-loop type mounted at the end of a shaft -- This group includes kitchen implements in which closed-loop wires are mounted on the end of a rotating shaft, such as whisks or beaters used for mixing, beating or aerating food.
[A47J 43/12] Whipping by introducing a stream of gas -- A47J 43/12 covers kitchen devices that create whipped or aerated food by injecting a jet of gas into the product. This falls under kitchen equipment for preparing or holding food not classified elsewhere.

It falls under the hierarchy HUMAN NECESSITIES → FURNITURE → KITCHEN EQUIPMENT for implements not provided for in other groups of the subclass.
