[A47L 23/28] Receptacles for shoe-cleaning equipment -- A47L 23/28 covers containers, trays or holders that receive and store shoe-cleaning tools, brushes, mats or water during footwear cleaning, including designs that provide drainage, support or disposal of used cleaning media.
[A47L 2401/00] Automatic detection in controlling methods of washing or rinsing machines for crockery or tableware, e.g. information provided by sensors entered into controlling devices -- This classification covers technologies that use sensors to automatically detect conditions and adjust the operation of dishwashing or rinsing machines, feeding the sensor data into the control system to optimise washing cycles for crockery and tableware. It falls under human necessities, furniture, domestic washing or cleaning.
[A47L 2401/02] Consumable products information, e.g. information on detergent, rinsing aid or salt -- A47L 2401/02 covers systems that automatically detect and use information about consumable products-such as detergent, rinsing aid or salt-in washing or rinsing machines for crockery or tableware, often by feeding sensor data into the machine's control unit.
[A47L 2401/023] Quantity or concentration of the consumable product -- The A47L 2401/023 entry covers methods that automatically monitor the amount or concentration of consumables such as detergent, rinse-aid or water in washing or rinsing machines for crockery or tableware, using sensor data fed to the machine's control system. It is part of the Cooperative Patent Classification hierarchy under Human Necessities → Furniture → Domestic Washing or Cleaning, and relates to detecting and adjusting cleaning processes based on the measured consumable levels.
[A47L 2401/026] Nature or type of the consumable product, e.g. information on detergent, e.g. 3-in-1 tablets, rinsing aid or salt -- A47L 2401/026 classifies inventions that identify the nature or type of consumable products used in domestic washing or cleaning, such as detergents, 3-in-1 tablets, rinsing aids or salt, by providing product-specific information to automatic detection and control systems for dish-washing or similar machines.
[A47L 2401/03] Operation mode, e.g. delicate washing, economy washing, reduced time, sterilizing, water softener regenerating, odor eliminating or service -- The CPC entry A47L 2401/03 covers control methods for domestic washing or cleaning appliances that automatically detect conditions and select operation modes such as delicate, economy, reduced-time, sterilising, water-softener regeneration or odour elimination for crockery and tableware. It relates to sensor-based inputs that influence the machine's washing or rinsing cycle.
[A47L 2401/04] Crockery or tableware details, e.g. material, quantity, condition -- A47L 2401/04 relates to the detection and use of information about crockery or tableware-such as material, quantity and condition-to control washing or rinsing machines. The code covers sensor-derived data that is fed into the machine's control system to optimise cycles.
[A47L 2401/06] Water supply, circulation or discharge information -- A47L 2401/06 covers the use of sensor-derived data on water flow, pressure or temperature to control the supply, circulation or discharge of water in dish-washing or tableware-rinsing machines. The classification applies to automatic detection methods that adjust washing cycles based on real-time water information.
[A47L 2401/07] Status of hydraulic components, e.g. open/close status of water inlet/outlet valves, operating position of water diverters -- A classification for domestic washing or cleaning appliances that covers the detection and reporting of hydraulic component conditions such as whether water inlet or outlet valves are open or closed and the position of water diverters, enabling sensor-derived information to be used in the control of washing or rinsing cycles for crockery or tableware.
[A47L 2401/08] Drain or recirculation pump parameters, e.g. pump rotational speed or current absorbed by the motor -- A47L 2401/08 covers the monitoring and control of drain or recirculation pump parameters, such as the pump's rotational speed or the motor's current draw, in automatic washing or rinsing machines for crockery and tableware. It falls under the hierarchy of human necessities → furniture → domestic washing or cleaning, focusing on sensor-derived data used to adjust machine cycles.
[A47L 2401/09] Water level -- The entry addresses systems that automatically detect water level in domestic washing or cleaning machines used for crockery or tableware, employing sensors whose information is fed to control devices to manage washing or rinsing cycles. It falls under the CPC subclass for water-level monitoring.
[A47L 2401/10] Water cloudiness or dirtiness, e.g. turbidity, foaming or level of bacteria -- The Cooperative Patent Classification entry A47L 2401/10 pertains to the automatic detection of water quality parameters such as cloudiness, dirtiness, turbidity, foaming, or bacterial levels in controlling methods for washing or rinsing machines designed for crockery or tableware.
[A47L 2401/11] Water hardness, acidity or basicity -- A47L 2401/11 covers the detection of water hardness, acidity or basicity in domestic washing or cleaning appliances, especially dishwashers, using sensors that feed this information to the control system to optimise washing or rinsing cycles.
[A47L 2401/12] Water temperature -- The A47L 2401/12 subclass relates to the detection and control of water temperature in automatic washing or rinsing machines for crockery or tableware. It covers sensor-based methods that provide temperature information to the machine's control unit to adjust the washing cycle.
[A47L 2401/14] Water pressure or flow rate -- The entry deals with monitoring or controlling water pressure and flow rate in automatic washing or rinsing machines for crockery or tableware, using sensor-derived information to adjust the process. It falls under the hierarchy Human Necessities → Furniture → Domestic Washing or Cleaning.
[A47L 2401/17] Air pressure -- The entry covers the use of air-pressure sensing to automatically detect load conditions and control washing or rinsing cycles in machines for crockery or tableware, integrating sensor data into the device's control system. It falls under domestic washing or cleaning within the furniture category of human necessities.
[A47L 2401/18] Air temperature -- A47L 2401/18 covers the use of air temperature data
[A47L 2401/19] Air humidity -- A47L 2401/19 relates to the detection and use of air-humidity information in the control of washing or rinsing machines for crockery or tableware. It covers sensor-derived humidity data that influence automatic washing or rinsing cycles, helping optimise performance and energy use.
[A47L 2401/20] Time, e.g. elapsed operating time -- A47L 2401/20 concerns the use of elapsed operating time in automatically controlling washing or rinsing machines for crockery or tableware. [A47L 9/00] Details or accessories of suction cleaners, e.g. mechanical means for controlling the suction or for effecting pulsating action -- **Definition:** This place covers:  Details of suction cleaners.  Details of parts, elements of the suction cleaner such as those related to the hose, nozzle or casing of the vacuum cleaner.  Accessories of suction cleaners.  Elements which are not integral part of the suction cleaner itself, but which can be used in combination with it. Examples: exchangeable nozzles or brushes, extension wands, etc.  Storing devices.  Those for storing the whole suction cleaner or elements of it. They can be elements external to the suction cleaner or elements integrated in the suction cleaner.  Carrying vehicles.  This includes any means facilitating, contributing to or making possible the displacement of the whole suction cleaner or a part of it (e.g. its nozzle), regardless of whether the means is integrated (e.g. wheels) or not in the suction cleaner.  This also includes means for avoiding mechanical obstacles, such as control means, as well as bumpers in the casing of the suction cleaner.  **Glossary:** - Agitator, beater: element striking the surface to be cleaned for dislodging dirt from it - Blower, fan, turbine: element provided with blades and rotating for generation of an underpressure - Dry filter: filter separating small particles contained in air flowing through it and which is not shaped as a bag - Suction mouth, suction head: vacuum cleaner nozzle

It relates to accessories of suction cleaners within the broader category of domestic washing or cleaning furniture.
[A47L 9/24] Hoses -- A47L 9/24 covers hoses for domestic suction cleaners, including the flexible conduits and fittings that link the motor unit to cleaning tools. The class also includes accessories that modify hose performance such as length adjusters, swivel connections or pulsating-action mechanisms.
[A47L 9/242] Hose or pipe couplings -- A47L 9/242 covers hose or pipe couplings used in domestic suction cleaners, including accessories that control suction or generate pulsating action. It pertains to mechanical devices that connect or regulate the flow in cleaning equipment.
[A47L 9/244] for telescopic or extensible hoses or pipes -- This subclass covers telescopic or extendable hoses and pipes used in suction cleaners, such as vacuum cleaners, enabling the cleaning tool to reach higher or more distant areas. The devices may incorporate mechanisms that adjust length while maintaining suction performance.
[A47L 9/246] with electrical connectors -- A47L 9/246 relates to accessories for suction cleaners that include mechanical means for controlling suction or producing a pulsating action, where the accessory is equipped with electrical connectors. This falls under domestic washing or cleaning within the broader furniture category of human necessities.
[A47L 9/248] Parts, details or accessories of hoses or pipes  nozzles  A47L9/02 -- A47L 9/248 covers components such as hoses, pipes and nozzles used in domestic suction cleaners, including mechanical devices that regulate suction or create a pulsating cleaning action. It falls under the hierarchy Human Necessities → Furniture →
[A47L 9/26] Incorporation of winding devices for electric cables -- A47L 9/26 covers the integration of cable-winding mechanisms into suction cleaners, describing devices that guide, store, or reel electric cords, often using spools, reels or spring-loaded systems, to control cord length and prevent entanglement during operation.
[A47L 9/28] Installation of the electric equipment, e.g. adaptation or attachment to the suction cleaner -- A47L 9/28 covers arrangements for installing electric components on suction cleaners, such as adapting or attaching power units, switches or controls to the vacuum cleaner.
[A47L 9/2805] Parameters or conditions being sensed -- The CPC subclass A47L 9/2805 covers suction-cleaner accessories that monitor or respond to operational parameters such as suction pressure, airflow, or pulsation frequency. It includes sensors and control mechanisms that detect these conditions to adjust cleaning performance automatically.
[A47L 9/281] the amount or condition of incoming dirt or dust -- This subclass covers features that regulate how much dirt or dust enters a suction cleaner, such as sensors, valves or controls that adjust suction power or create pulsating airflow. It relates to accessories that monitor or modify the condition of incoming debris to improve cleaning efficiency.
[A47L 9/2815] using optical detectors -- The A47L 9/2815 subclass covers accessories for suction cleaners that incorporate optical detectors to monitor or regulate suction, airflow or pulsating actions, enabling automated control of cleaning performance. It falls under the domestic washing and cleaning category of furniture for human necessities.
[A47L 9/2821] Pressure, vacuum level or airflow -- A47L 9/2821 is a CPC subclass for details or accessories of suction cleaners that regulate pressure, vacuum level or airflow, such as mechanical controllers or pulsating devices. It falls under Human Necessities → Furniture → Domestic Washing or Cleaning, covering mechanisms that adjust the suction performance of vacuum cleaners.
[A47L 9/2826] the condition of the floor -- A47L 9/2826 classifies inventions dealing with the condition of the floor in the context of domestic suction-cleaning equipment, including accessories or mechanisms that monitor, adapt to, or influence floor surfaces during cleaning. It falls under details or accessories of suction cleaners, covering features such as floor-type sensing, adjustable suction for different floor conditions, or systems that modify cleaning action according to the floor's state.
[A47L 9/2831] Motor parameters, e.g. motor load or speed -- The code A47L 9/2831 addresses motor characteristics such as load, speed and related operating parameters of accessories for suction cleaners, including mechanisms that control suction or produce pulsating action. It sits within the hierarchy of Human Necessities → Furniture → Domestic Washing or Cleaning.
[A47L 9/2836] characterised by the parts which are controlled -- The CPC subclass A47L 9/2836 pertains to accessories for suction cleaners that provide mechanical control of the suction or produce a pulsating action, such as valves, regulators, actuators or similar devices that modulate airflow. It falls under the hierarchy of Human Necessities → Furniture → Domestic washing or cleaning.
[A47L 9/2842] Suction motors or blowers -- A47L 9/2842 pertains to suction motors or blowers used as details or accessories in suction cleaners, focusing on mechanical means for controlling suction or creating pulsating action. This classification is part of the Cooperative Patent Classification system used by the EPO and USPTO.
[A47L 9/2847] Surface treating elements -- A47L 9/2847 covers surface-treating elements for suction cleaners, such as brushes, rollers or other mechanical devices that control suction or create a pulsating action. These accessories are used in domestic washing or cleaning of furniture and other household items. It falls under the hierarchy HUMAN NECESSITIES → FURNITURE → DOMESTIC WASHING OR CLEANING.
[A47L 9/2852] Elements for displacement of the vacuum cleaner or the accessories therefor, e.g. wheels, casters or nozzles -- A47L 9/2852 covers components that enable a vacuum cleaner to be moved or to direct airflow, such as wheels, casters, and nozzles, as well as related accessories for displacement. It falls under the CPC hierarchy for domestic washing or cleaning devices, specifically detailing elements that affect the suction unit's positioning or pulsating action.

[A47L 9/2863] Control elements activated by pivoting movement of the upright vacuum cleaner handle -- The code A47L 9/2863 covers accessories for suction cleaners that use the pivoting movement of an upright vacuum's handle to operate control elements, such as mechanisms that adjust suction or create pulsating action. It belongs to the hierarchy of human necessities, furniture, domestic washing or cleaning.
[A47L 9/2868] Arrangements for power supply of vacuum cleaners or the accessories thereof -- A47L 9/2868 classifies inventions that provide arrangements for supplying electrical power to vacuum cleaners or to their attached accessories, such as cords, rechargeable batteries, power adapters or integrated power modules. It covers the means of delivering, managing or controlling power within the cleaner or its detachable components.
[A47L 9/2873] Docking units or charging stations  for autonomous or robotic vacuum cleaners  A47L2201/02 -- A47L 9/2873 covers accessories for suction cleaners that provide docking or charging functions for autonomous or robotic vacuum cleaners, including mechanisms that enable the device to connect to a power source and resume operation without user intervention. It falls under the CPC hierarchy for human necessities, furniture and domestic washing or cleaning, addressing the integration of power-recharging stations with robotic cleaning devices.
[A47L 9/2878] Dual-powered vacuum cleaners, i.e. devices which can be operated with mains power supply or by batteries -- A47L 9/2878 pertains to vacuum cleaners that can operate either from a mains supply or from portable batteries, including the mechanisms that select between power sources and any integrated suction-control or pulsating features. It falls under details or accessories of suction cleaners in domestic washing or cleaning.
[A47L 9/2884] Details of arrangements of batteries or their installation -- A47L 9/2884 covers the design and mounting of batteries within suction cleaners and related accessories. It includes arrangements that control power supply, integrate batteries into the device, or enable easy installation and replacement. This subclass falls under domestic washing or cleaning equipment within the broader furniture category of human necessities.
[A47L 9/2889] Safety or protection devices or systems, e.g. for prevention of motor over-heating or for protection of the user -- A47L 9/2889 covers safety and protection devices for suction cleaners, such as mechanisms that prevent motor overheating or protect the user, and accessories that control suction or create pulsating action.
[A47L 9/2894] Details related to signal transmission in suction cleaners -- A47L 9/2894 classifies features of suction cleaners that involve transmitting signals to control suction intensity or pulsating action, including mechanical or electronic means for modulating the cleaning performance. It falls within the domestic washing and cleaning subgroup of furniture in the Human Necessities section.
[A47L 9/30] Arrangement of illuminating devices -- A47L 9/30 pertains to the design and positioning of lighting on suction cleaners and related accessories, covering integrated lamps, LEDs or other illumination means that help the user see the cleaning area. It includes any mechanical features that support or adjust the light source within the vacuum-cleaning device.
[A47L 9/32] Handles -- A47L 9/32 covers handles incorporated in domestic suction cleaners, including the design, mounting, adjustment and ergonomic features of grips, straps or push-bars that a user holds while operating the device. It also includes handles that integrate controls for suction or pulsating functions.
[A47L 9/322] for hand-supported suction cleaners -- A47L 9/322 covers hand-supported suction cleaners and their accessories, such as mechanisms that regulate suction or produce a pulsating cleaning action. It falls under the broader categories of human necessities, furniture and domestic washing or cleaning.
[A47L 9/325] for wheeled suction cleaners with steering handle -- A47L 9/325 covers wheeled suction cleaners equipped with a steering handle, including any attached mechanisms that control the suction or produce a pulsating action. It falls under the CPC group for details or accessories of suction cleaners within domestic washing or cleaning furniture.
[A47L 9/327] for suction cleaners with hose between nozzle and casing -- A47L 9/327 covers accessories for suction cleaners where a flexible hose connects the nozzle to the main housing, including mechanisms that regulate suction strength or create a pulsating airflow. It applies to designs that integrate control devices within the hose-to-casing arrangement.
